<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `/c/Users/downl/Desktop/hermes-agent-main/hermes-agent-main/tools/process_registry.py`

```text
1	"""
2	Process Registry -- In-memory registry for managed background processes.
3	
4	Tracks processes spawned via terminal(background=true), providing:
5	  - Output buffering (rolling 200KB window)
6	  - Status polling and log retrieval
7	  - Blocking wait with interrupt support
8	  - Process killing
9	  - Crash recovery via JSON checkpoint file
10	  - Session-scoped tracking for gateway reset protection
11	
12	Background processes execute THROUGH the environment interface -- nothing
13	runs on the host machine unless TERMINAL_ENV=local. For Docker, Singularity,
14	Modal, Daytona, and SSH backends, the command runs inside the sandbox.
15	
16	Usage:
17	    from tools.process_registry import process_registry
18	
19	    # Spawn a background process (called from terminal_tool)
20	    session = process_registry.spawn(env, "pytest -v", task_id="task_123")
21	
22	    # Poll for status
23	    result = process_registry.poll(session.id)
24	
25	    # Block until done
26	    result = process_registry.wait(session.id, timeout=300)
27	
28	    # Kill it
29	    process_registry.kill(session.id)
30	"""
31	
32	import json
33	import logging
34	import os
35	import platform
36	import shlex
37	import signal
38	import subprocess
39	import threading
40	import time
41	import uuid
42	
43	_IS_WINDOWS = platform.system() == "Windows"
44	from tools.environments.local import _find_shell, _sanitize_subprocess_env
45	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
46	from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
47	
48	from hermes_cli.config import get_hermes_home
49	
50	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
51	
52	
53	# Checkpoint file for crash recovery (gateway only)
54	CHECKPOINT_PATH = get_hermes_home() / "processes.json"
55	
56	# Limits
57	MAX_OUTPUT_CHARS = 200_000      # 200KB rolling output buffer
58	FINISHED_TTL_SECONDS = 1800     # Keep finished processes for 30 minutes
59	MAX_PROCESSES = 64              # Max concurrent tracked processes (LRU pruning)
60	
61	
62	@dataclass
63	class ProcessSession:
64	    """A tracked background process with output buffering."""
65	    id: str                                     # Unique session ID ("proc_xxxxxxxxxxxx")
66	    command: str                                 # Original command string
67	    task_id: str = ""                           # Task/sandbox isolation key
68	    session_key: str = ""                       # Gateway session key (for reset protection)
69	    pid: Optional[int] = None                   # OS process ID
70	    process: Optional[subprocess.Popen] = None  # Popen handle (local only)
71	    env_ref: Any = None                         # Reference to the environment object
72	    cwd: Optional[str] = None                   # Working directory
73	    started_at: float = 0.0                     # time.time() of spawn
74	    exited: bool = False                        # Whether the process has finished
75	    exit_code: Optional[int] = None             # Exit code (None if still running)
76	    output_buffer: str = ""                     # Rolling output (last MAX_OUTPUT_CHARS)
77	    max_output_chars: int = MAX_OUTPUT_CHARS
78	    detached: bool = False                      # True if recovered from crash (no pipe)
79	    # Watcher/notification metadata (persisted for crash recovery)
80	    watcher_platform: str = ""
81	    watcher_chat_id: str = ""
82	    watcher_thread_id: str = ""
83	    watcher_interval: int = 0                   # 0 = no watcher configured
84	    _lock: threading.Lock = field(default_factory=threading.Lock)
85	    _reader_thread: Optional[threading.Thread] = field(default=None, repr=False)
86	    _pty: Any = field(default=None, repr=False)  # ptyprocess handle (when use_pty=True)
87	
88	
89	class ProcessRegistry:
90	    """
91	    In-memory registry of running and finished background processes.
92	
93	    Thread-safe. Accessed from:
94	      - Executor threads (terminal_tool, process tool handlers)
95	      - Gateway asyncio loop (watcher tasks, session reset checks)
96	      - Cleanup thread (sandbox reaping coordination)
97	    """
98	
99	    _SHELL_NOISE_SUBSTRINGS = (
100	        "bash: cannot set terminal process group",
101	        "bash: no job control in this shell",
102	        "no job control in this shell",
103	        "cannot set terminal process group",
104	        "tcsetattr: Inappropriate ioctl for device",
105	    )
106	
107	    def __init__(self):
108	        self._running: Dict[str, ProcessSession] = {}
109	        self._finished: Dict[str, ProcessSession] = {}
110	        self._lock = threading.Lock()
111	
112	        # Side-channel for check_interval watchers (gateway reads after agent run)
113	        self.pending_watchers: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
114	
115	    @staticmethod
116	    def _clean_shell_noise(text: str) -> str:
117	        """Strip shell startup warnings from the beginning of output."""
118	        lines = text.split("\n")
119	        while lines and any(noise in lines[0] for noise in ProcessRegistry._SHELL_NOISE_SUBSTRINGS):
120	            lines.pop(0)
121	        return "\n".join(lines)
122	
123	    # ----- Spawn -----
124	
125	    def spawn_local(
126	        self,
127	        command: str,
128	        cwd: str = None,
129	        task_id: str = "",
130	        session_key: str = "",
131	        env_vars: dict = None,
132	        use_pty: bool = False,
133	    ) -> ProcessSession:
134	        """
135	        Spawn a background process locally.
136	
137	        Only for TERMINAL_ENV=local. Other backends use spawn_via_env().
138	
139	        Args:
140	            use_pty: If True, use a pseudo-terminal via ptyprocess for interactive
141	                     CLI tools (Codex, Claude Code, Python REPL). Falls back to
142	                     subprocess.Popen if ptyprocess is not installed.
143	        """
144	        session = ProcessSession(
145	            id=f"proc_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}",
146	            command=command,
147	            task_id=task_id,
148	            session_key=session_key,
149	            cwd=cwd or os.getcwd(),
150	            started_at=time.time(),
151	        )
152	
153	        if use_pty:
154	            # Try PTY mode for interactive CLI tools
155	            try:
156	                if _IS_WINDOWS:
157	                    from winpty import PtyProcess as _PtyProcessCls
158	                else:
159	                    from ptyprocess import PtyProcess as _PtyProcessCls
160	                user_shell = _find_shell()
161	                pty_env = _sanitize_subprocess_env(os.environ, env_vars)
162	                pty_env["PYTHONUNBUFFERED"] = "1"
163	                pty_proc = _PtyProcessCls.spawn(
164	                    [user_shell, "-lic", command],
165	                    cwd=session.cwd,
166	                    env=pty_env,
167	                    dimensions=(30, 120),
168	                )
169	                session.pid = pty_proc.pid
170	                # Store the pty handle on the session for read/write
171	                session._pty = pty_proc
172	
173	                # PTY reader thread
174	                reader = threading.Thread(
175	                    target=self._pty_reader_loop,
176	                    args=(session,),
177	                    daemon=True,
178	                    name=f"proc-pty-reader-{session.id}",
179	                )
180	                session._reader_thread = reader
181	                reader.start()
182	
183	                with self._lock:
184	                    self._prune_if_needed()
185	                    self._running[session.id] = session
186	
187	                self._write_checkpoint()
188	                return session
189	
190	            except ImportError:
191	                logger.warning("ptyprocess not installed, falling back to pipe mode")
192	            except Exception as e:
193	                logger.warning("PTY spawn failed (%s), falling back to pipe mode", e)
194	
195	        # Standard Popen path (non-PTY or PTY fallback)
196	        # Use the user's login shell for consistency with LocalEnvironment --
197	        # ensures rc files are sourced and user tools are available.
198	        user_shell = _find_shell()
199	        # Force unbuffered output for Python scripts so progress is visible
200	        # during background execution (libraries like tqdm/datasets buffer when
201	        # stdout is a pipe, hiding output from process(action="poll")).
202	        bg_env = _sanitize_subprocess_env(os.environ, env_vars)
203	        bg_env["PYTHONUNBUFFERED"] = "1"
204	        proc = subprocess.Popen(
205	            [user_shell, "-lic", command],
206	            text=True,
207	            cwd=session.cwd,
208	            env=bg_env,
209	            encoding="utf-8",
210	            errors="replace",
211	            stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
212	            stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
213	            stdin=subprocess.PIPE,
214	            preexec_fn=None if _IS_WINDOWS else os.setsid,
215	        )
216	
217	        session.process = proc
218	        session.pid = proc.pid
219	
220	        # Start output reader thread
221	        reader = threading.Thread(
222	            target=self._reader_loop,
223	            args=(session,),
224	            daemon=True,
225	            name=f"proc-reader-{session.id}",
226	        )
227	        session._reader_thread = reader
228	        reader.start()
229	
230	        with self._lock:
231	            self._prune_if_needed()
232	            self._running[session.id] = session
233	
234	        self._write_checkpoint()
235	        return session
236	
237	    def spawn_via_env(
238	        self,
239	        env: Any,
240	        command: str,
241	        cwd: str = None,
242	        task_id: str = "",
243	        session_key: str = "",
244	        timeout: int = 10,
245	    ) -> ProcessSession:
246	        """
247	        Spawn a background process through a non-local environment backend.
248	
249	        For Docker/Singularity/Modal/Daytona/SSH: runs the command inside the sandbox
250	        using the environment's execute() interface. We wrap the command to
251	        capture the in-sandbox PID and redirect output to a log file inside
252	        the sandbox, then poll the log via subsequent execute() calls.
253	
254	        This is less capable than local spawn (no live stdout pipe, no stdin),
255	        but it ensures the command runs in the correct sandbox context.
256	        """
257	        session = ProcessSession(
258	            id=f"proc_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}",
259	            command=command,
260	            task_id=task_id,
261	            session_key=session_key,
262	            cwd=cwd,
263	            started_at=time.time(),
264	            env_ref=env,
265	        )
266	
267	        # Run the command in the sandbox with output capture
268	        log_path = f"/tmp/hermes_bg_{session.id}.log"
269	        pid_path = f"/tmp/hermes_bg_{session.id}.pid"
270	        quoted_command = shlex.quote(command)
271	        bg_command = (
272	            f"nohup bash -c {quoted_command} > {log_path} 2>&1 & "
273	            f"echo $! > {pid_path} && cat {pid_path}"
274	        )
275	
276	        try:
277	            result = env.execute(bg_command, timeout=timeout)
278	            output = result.get("output", "").strip()
279	            # Try to extract the PID from the output
280	            for line in output.splitlines():
281	                line = line.strip()
282	                if line.isdigit():
283	                    session.pid = int(line)
284	                    break
285	        except Exception as e:
286	            session.exited = True
287	            session.exit_code = -1
288	            session.output_buffer = f"Failed to start: {e}"
289	
290	        if not session.exited:
291	            # Start a poller thread that periodically reads the log file
292	            reader = threading.Thread(
293	                target=self._env_poller_loop,
294	                args=(session, env, log_path, pid_path),
295	                daemon=True,
296	                name=f"proc-poller-{session.id}",
297	            )
298	            session._reader_thread = reader
299	            reader.start()
300	
301	        with self._lock:
302	            self._prune_if_needed()
303	            self._running[session.id] = session
304	
305	        self._write_checkpoint()
306	        return session
307	
308	    # ----- Reader / Poller Threads -----
309	
310	    def _reader_loop(self, session: ProcessSession):
311	        """Background thread: read stdout from a local Popen process."""
312	        first_chunk = True
313	        try:
314	            while True:
315	                chunk = session.process.stdout.read(4096)
316	                if not chunk:
317	                    break
318	                if first_chunk:
319	                    chunk = self._clean_shell_noise(chunk)
320	                    first_chunk = False
321	                with session._lock:
322	                    session.output_buffer += chunk
323	                    if len(session.output_buffer) > session.max_output_chars:
324	                        session.output_buffer = session.output_buffer[-session.max_output_chars:]
325	        except Exception as e:
326	            logger.debug("Process stdout reader ended: %s", e)
327	
328	        # Process exited
329	        try:
330	            session.process.wait(timeout=5)
331	        except Exception as e:
332	            logger.debug("Process wait timed out or failed: %s", e)
333	        session.exited = True
334	        session.exit_code = session.process.returncode
335	        self._move_to_finished(session)
336	
337	    def _env_poller_loop(
338	        self, session: ProcessSession, env: Any, log_path: str, pid_path: str
339	    ):
340	        """Background thread: poll a sandbox log file for non-local backends."""
341	        while not session.exited:
342	            time.sleep(2)  # Poll every 2 seconds
343	            try:
344	                # Read new output from the log file
345	                result = env.execute(f"cat {log_path} 2>/dev/null", timeout=10)
346	                new_output = result.get("output", "")
347	                if new_output:
348	                    with session._lock:
349	                        session.output_buffer = new_output
350	                        if len(session.output_buffer) > session.max_output_chars:
351	                            session.output_buffer = session.output_buffer[-session.max_output_chars:]
352	
353	                # Check if process is still running
354	                check = env.execute(
355	                    f"kill -0 $(cat {pid_path} 2>/dev/null) 2>/dev/null; echo $?",
356	                    timeout=5,
357	                )
358	                check_output = check.get("output", "").strip()
359	                if check_output and check_output.splitlines()[-1].strip() != "0":
360	                    # Process has exited -- get exit code
361	                    exit_result = env.execute(
362	                        f"wait $(cat {pid_path} 2>/dev/null) 2>/dev/null; echo $?",
363	                        timeout=5,
364	                    )
365	                    exit_str = exit_result.get("output", "").strip()
366	                    try:
367	                        session.exit_code = int(exit_str.splitlines()[-1].strip())
368	                    except (ValueError, IndexError):
369	                        session.exit_code = -1
370	                    session.exited = True
371	                    self._move_to_finished(session)
372	                    return
373	
374	            except Exception:
375	                # Environment might be gone (sandbox reaped, etc.)
376	                session.exited = True
377	                session.exit_code = -1
378	                self._move_to_finished(session)
379	                return
380	
381	    def _pty_reader_loop(self, session: ProcessSession):
382	        """Background thread: read output from a PTY process."""
383	        pty = session._pty
384	        try:
385	            while pty.isalive():
386	                try:
387	                    chunk = pty.read(4096)
388	                    if chunk:
389	                        # ptyprocess returns bytes
390	                        text = chunk if isinstance(chunk, str) else chunk.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
391	                        with session._lock:
392	                            session.output_buffer += text
393	                            if len(session.output_buffer) > session.max_output_chars:
394	                                session.output_buffer = session.output_buffer[-session.max_output_chars:]
395	                except EOFError:
396	                    break
397	                except Exception:
398	                    break
399	        except Exception as e:
400	            logger.debug("PTY stdout reader ended: %s", e)
401	
402	        # Process exited
403	        try:
404	            pty.wait()
405	        except Exception as e:
406	            logger.debug("PTY wait timed out or failed: %s", e)
407	        session.exited = True
408	        session.exit_code = pty.exitstatus if hasattr(pty, 'exitstatus') else -1
409	        self._move_to_finished(session)
410	
411	    def _move_to_finished(self, session: ProcessSession):
412	        """Move a session from running to finished."""
413	        with self._lock:
414	            self._running.pop(session.id, None)
415	            self._finished[session.id] = session
416	        self._write_checkpoint()
417	
418	    # ----- Query Methods -----
419	
420	    def get(self, session_id: str) -> Optional[ProcessSession]:
421	        """Get a session by ID (running or finished)."""
422	        with self._lock:
423	            return self._running.get(session_id) or self._finished.get(session_id)
424	
425	    def poll(self, session_id: str) -> dict:
426	        """Check status and get new output for a background process."""
427	        from tools.ansi_strip import strip_ansi
428	
429	        session = self.get(session_id)
430	        if session is None:
431	            return {"status": "not_found", "error": f"No process with ID {session_id}"}
432	
433	        with session._lock:
434	            output_preview = strip_ansi(session.output_buffer[-1000:]) if session.output_buffer else ""
435	
436	        result = {
437	            "session_id": session.id,
438	            "command": session.command,
439	            "status": "exited" if session.exited else "running",
440	            "pid": session.pid,
441	            "uptime_seconds": int(time.time() - session.started_at),
442	            "output_preview": output_preview,
443	        }
444	        if session.exited:
445	            result["exit_code"] = session.exit_code
446	        if session.detached:
447	            result["detached"] = True
448	            result["note"] = "Process recovered after restart -- output history unavailable"
449	        return result
450	
451	    def read_log(self, session_id: str, offset: int = 0, limit: int = 200) -> dict:
452	        """Read the full output log with optional pagination by lines."""
453	        from tools.ansi_strip import strip_ansi
454	
455	        session = self.get(session_id)
456	        if session is None:
457	            return {"status": "not_found", "error": f"No process with ID {session_id}"}
458	
459	        with session._lock:
460	            full_output = strip_ansi(session.output_buffer)
461	
462	        lines = full_output.splitlines()
463	        total_lines = len(lines)
464	
465	        # Default: last N lines
466	        if offset == 0 and limit > 0:
467	            selected = lines[-limit:]
468	        else:
469	            selected = lines[offset:offset + limit]
470	
471	        return {
472	            "session_id": session.id,
473	            "status": "exited" if session.exited else "running",
474	            "output": "\n".join(selected),
475	            "total_lines": total_lines,
476	            "showing": f"{len(selected)} lines",
477	        }
478	
479	    def wait(self, session_id: str, timeout: int = None) -> dict:
480	        """
481	        Block until a process exits, timeout, or interrupt.
482	
483	        Args:
484	            session_id: The process to wait for.
485	            timeout: Max seconds to block. Falls back to TERMINAL_TIMEOUT config.
486	
487	        Returns:
488	            dict with status ("exited", "timeout", "interrupted", "not_found")
489	            and output snapshot.
490	        """
491	        from tools.ansi_strip import strip_ansi
492	        from tools.terminal_tool import _interrupt_event
493	
494	        default_timeout = int(os.getenv("TERMINAL_TIMEOUT", "180"))
495	        max_timeout = default_timeout
496	        requested_timeout = timeout
497	        timeout_note = None
498	
499	        if requested_timeout and requested_timeout > max_timeout:
500	            effective_timeout = max_timeout
501	            timeout_note = (
502	                f"Requested wait of {requested_timeout}s was clamped "
503	                f"to configured limit of {max_timeout}s"
504	            )
505	        else:
506	            effective_timeout = requested_timeout or max_timeout
507	
508	        session = self.get(session_id)
509	        if session is None:
510	            return {"status": "not_found", "error": f"No process with ID {session_id}"}
511	
512	        deadline = time.monotonic() + effective_timeout
513	
514	        while time.monotonic() < deadline:
515	            if session.exited:
516	                result = {
517	                    "status": "exited",
518	                    "exit_code": session.exit_code,
519	                    "output": strip_ansi(session.output_buffer[-2000:]),
520	                }
521	                if timeout_note:
522	                    result["timeout_note"] = timeout_note
523	                return result
524	
525	            if _interrupt_event.is_set():
526	                result = {
527	                    "status": "interrupted",
528	                    "output": strip_ansi(session.output_buffer[-1000:]),
529	                    "note": "User sent a new message -- wait interrupted",
530	                }
531	                if timeout_note:
532	                    result["timeout_note"] = timeout_note
533	                return result
534	
535	            time.sleep(1)
536	
537	        result = {
538	            "status": "timeout",
539	            "output": strip_ansi(session.output_buffer[-1000:]),
540	        }
541	        if timeout_note:
542	            result["timeout_note"] = timeout_note
543	        else:
544	            result["timeout_note"] = f"Waited {effective_timeout}s, process still running"
545	        return result
546	
547	    def kill_process(self, session_id: str) -> dict:
548	        """Kill a background process."""
549	        session = self.get(session_id)
550	        if session is None:
551	            return {"status": "not_found", "error": f"No process with ID {session_id}"}
552	
553	        if session.exited:
554	            return {
555	                "status": "already_exited",
556	                "exit_code": session.exit_code,
557	            }
558	
559	        # Kill via PTY, Popen (local), or env execute (non-local)
560	        try:
561	            if session._pty:
562	                # PTY process -- terminate via ptyprocess
563	                try:
564	                    session._pty.terminate(force=True)
565	                except Exception:
566	                    if session.pid:
567	                        os.kill(session.pid, signal.SIGTERM)
568	            elif session.process:
569	                # Local process -- kill the process group
570	                try:
571	                    if _IS_WINDOWS:
572	                        session.process.terminate()
573	                    else:
574	                        os.killpg(os.getpgid(session.process.pid), signal.SIGTERM)
575	                except (ProcessLookupError, PermissionError):
576	                    session.process.kill()
577	            elif session.env_ref and session.pid:
578	                # Non-local -- kill inside sandbox
579	                session.env_ref.execute(f"kill {session.pid} 2>/dev/null", timeout=5)
580	            session.exited = True
581	            session.exit_code = -15  # SIGTERM
582	            self._move_to_finished(session)
583	            self._write_checkpoint()
584	            return {"status": "killed", "session_id": session.id}
585	        except Exception as e:
586	            return {"status": "error", "error": str(e)}
587	
588	    def write_stdin(self, session_id: str, data: str) -> dict:
589	        """Send raw data to a running process's stdin (no newline appended)."""
590	        session = self.get(session_id)
591	        if session is None:
592	            return {"status": "not_found", "error": f"No process with ID {session_id}"}
593	        if session.exited:
594	            return {"status": "already_exited", "error": "Process has already finished"}
595	
596	        # PTY mode -- write through pty handle (expects bytes)
597	        if hasattr(session, '_pty') and session._pty:
598	            try:
599	                pty_data = data.encode("utf-8") if isinstance(data, str) else data
600	                session._pty.write(pty_data)
601	                return {"status": "ok", "bytes_written": len(data)}
602	            except Exception as e:
603	                return {"status": "error", "error": str(e)}
604	
605	        # Popen mode -- write through stdin pipe
606	        if not session.process or not session.process.stdin:
607	            return {"status": "error", "error": "Process stdin not available (non-local backend or stdin closed)"}
608	        try:
609	            session.process.stdin.write(data)
610	            session.process.stdin.flush()
611	            return {"status": "ok", "bytes_written": len(data)}
612	        except Exception as e:
613	            return {"status": "error", "error": str(e)}
614	
615	    def submit_stdin(self, session_id: str, data: str = "") -> dict:
616	        """Send data + newline to a running process's stdin (like pressing Enter)."""
617	        return self.write_stdin(session_id, data + "\n")
618	
619	    def list_sessions(self, task_id: str = None) -> list:
620	        """List all running and recently-finished processes."""
621	        with self._lock:
622	            all_sessions = list(self._running.values()) + list(self._finished.values())
623	
624	        if task_id:
625	            all_sessions = [s for s in all_sessions if s.task_id == task_id]
626	
627	        result = []
628	        for s in all_sessions:
629	            entry = {
630	                "session_id": s.id,
631	                "command": s.command[:200],
632	                "cwd": s.cwd,
633	                "pid": s.pid,
634	                "started_at": time.strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S", time.localtime(s.started_at)),
635	                "uptime_seconds": int(time.time() - s.started_at),
636	                "status": "exited" if s.exited else "running",
637	                "output_preview": s.output_buffer[-200:] if s.output_buffer else "",
638	            }
639	            if s.exited:
640	                entry["exit_code"] = s.exit_code
641	            if s.detached:
642	                entry["detached"] = True
643	            result.append(entry)
644	        return result
645	
646	    # ----- Session/Task Queries (for gateway integration) -----
647	
648	    def has_active_processes(self, task_id: str) -> bool:
649	        """Check if there are active (running) processes for a task_id."""
650	        with self._lock:
651	            return any(
652	                s.task_id == task_id and not s.exited
653	                for s in self._running.values()
654	            )
655	
656	    def has_active_for_session(self, session_key: str) -> bool:
657	        """Check if there are active processes for a gateway session key."""
658	        with self._lock:
659	            return any(
660	                s.session_key == session_key and not s.exited
661	                for s in self._running.values()
662	            )
663	
664	    def kill_all(self, task_id: str = None) -> int:
665	        """Kill all running processes, optionally filtered by task_id. Returns count killed."""
666	        with self._lock:
667	            targets = [
668	                s for s in self._running.values()
669	                if (task_id is None or s.task_id == task_id) and not s.exited
670	            ]
671	
672	        killed = 0
673	        for session in targets:
674	            result = self.kill_process(session.id)
675	            if result.get("status") in ("killed", "already_exited"):
676	                killed += 1
677	        return killed
678	
679	    # ----- Cleanup / Pruning -----
680	
681	    def _prune_if_needed(self):
682	        """Remove oldest finished sessions if over MAX_PROCESSES. Must hold _lock."""
683	        # First prune expired finished sessions
684	        now = time.time()
685	        expired = [
686	            sid for sid, s in self._finished.items()
687	            if (now - s.started_at) > FINISHED_TTL_SECONDS
688	        ]
689	        for sid in expired:
690	            del self._finished[sid]
691	
692	        # If still over limit, remove oldest finished
693	        total = len(self._running) + len(self._finished)
694	        if total >= MAX_PROCESSES and self._finished:
695	            oldest_id = min(self._finished, key=lambda sid: self._finished[sid].started_at)
696	            del self._finished[oldest_id]
697	
698	    def cleanup_expired(self):
699	        """Public method to prune expired finished sessions."""
700	        with self._lock:
701	            self._prune_if_needed()
702	
703	    # ----- Checkpoint (crash recovery) -----
704	
705	    def _write_checkpoint(self):
706	        """Write running process metadata to checkpoint file atomically."""
707	        try:
708	            with self._lock:
709	                entries = []
710	                for s in self._running.values():
711	                    if not s.exited:
712	                        entries.append({
713	                            "session_id": s.id,
714	                            "command": s.command,
715	                            "pid": s.pid,
716	                            "cwd": s.cwd,
717	                            "started_at": s.started_at,
718	                            "task_id": s.task_id,
719	                            "session_key": s.session_key,
720	                            "watcher_platform": s.watcher_platform,
721	                            "watcher_chat_id": s.watcher_chat_id,
722	                            "watcher_thread_id": s.watcher_thread_id,
723	                            "watcher_interval": s.watcher_interval,
724	                        })
725	            
726	            # Atomic write to avoid corruption on crash
727	            from utils import atomic_json_write
728	            atomic_json_write(CHECKPOINT_PATH, entries)
729	        except Exception as e:
730	            logger.debug("Failed to write checkpoint file: %s", e, exc_info=True)
731	
732	    def recover_from_checkpoint(self) -> int:
733	        """
734	        On gateway startup, probe PIDs from checkpoint file.
735	
736	        Returns the number of processes recovered as detached.
737	        """
738	        if not CHECKPOINT_PATH.exists():
739	            return 0
740	
741	        try:
742	            entries = json.loads(CHECKPOINT_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
743	        except Exception:
744	            return 0
745	
746	        recovered = 0
747	        for entry in entries:
748	            pid = entry.get("pid")
749	            if not pid:
750	                continue
751	
752	            # Check if PID is still alive
753	            alive = False
754	            try:
755	                os.kill(pid, 0)
756	                alive = True
757	            except (ProcessLookupError, PermissionError):
758	                pass
759	
760	            if alive:
761	                session = ProcessSession(
762	                    id=entry["session_id"],
763	                    command=entry.get("command", "unknown"),
764	                    task_id=entry.get("task_id", ""),
765	                    session_key=entry.get("session_key", ""),
766	                    pid=pid,
767	                    cwd=entry.get("cwd"),
768	                    started_at=entry.get("started_at", time.time()),
769	                    detached=True,  # Can't read output, but can report status + kill
770	                    watcher_platform=entry.get("watcher_platform", ""),
771	                    watcher_chat_id=entry.get("watcher_chat_id", ""),
772	                    watcher_thread_id=entry.get("watcher_thread_id", ""),
773	                    watcher_interval=entry.get("watcher_interval", 0),
774	                )
775	                with self._lock:
776	                    self._running[session.id] = session
777	                recovered += 1
778	                logger.info("Recovered detached process: %s (pid=%d)", session.command[:60], pid)
779	
780	                # Re-enqueue watcher so gateway can resume notifications
781	                if session.watcher_interval > 0:
782	                    self.pending_watchers.append({
783	                        "session_id": session.id,
784	                        "check_interval": session.watcher_interval,
785	                        "session_key": session.session_key,
786	                        "platform": session.watcher_platform,
787	                        "chat_id": session.watcher_chat_id,
788	                        "thread_id": session.watcher_thread_id,
789	                    })
790	
791	        # Clear the checkpoint (will be rewritten as processes finish)
792	        try:
793	            from utils import atomic_json_write
794	            atomic_json_write(CHECKPOINT_PATH, [])
795	        except Exception as e:
796	            logger.debug("Could not clear checkpoint file: %s", e, exc_info=True)
797	
798	        return recovered
799	
800	
801	# Module-level singleton
802	process_registry = ProcessRegistry()
803	
804	
805	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
806	# Registry -- the "process" tool schema + handler
807	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
808	from tools.registry import registry
809	
810	PROCESS_SCHEMA = {
811	    "name": "process",
812	    "description": (
813	        "Manage background processes started with terminal(background=true). "
814	        "Actions: 'list' (show all), 'poll' (check status + new output), "
815	        "'log' (full output with pagination), 'wait' (block until done or timeout), "
816	        "'kill' (terminate), 'write' (send raw stdin data without newline), "
817	        "'submit' (send data + Enter, for answering prompts)."
818	    ),
819	    "parameters": {
820	        "type": "object",
821	        "properties": {
822	            "action": {
823	                "type": "string",
824	                "enum": ["list", "poll", "log", "wait", "kill", "write", "submit"],
825	                "description": "Action to perform on background processes"
826	            },
827	            "session_id": {
828	                "type": "string",
829	                "description": "Process session ID (from terminal background output). Required for all actions except 'list'."
830	            },
831	            "data": {
832	                "type": "string",
833	                "description": "Text to send to process stdin (for 'write' and 'submit' actions)"
834	            },
835	            "timeout": {
836	                "type": "integer",
837	                "description": "Max seconds to block for 'wait' action. Returns partial output on timeout.",
838	                "minimum": 1
839	            },
840	            "offset": {
841	                "type": "integer",
842	                "description": "Line offset for 'log' action (default: last 200 lines)"
843	            },
844	            "limit": {
845	                "type": "integer",
846	                "description": "Max lines to return for 'log' action",
847	                "minimum": 1
848	            }
849	        },
850	        "required": ["action"]
851	    }
852	}
853	
854	
855	def _handle_process(args, **kw):
856	    import json as _json
857	    task_id = kw.get("task_id")
858	    action = args.get("action", "")
859	    # Coerce to string — some models send session_id as an integer
860	    session_id = str(args.get("session_id", "")) if args.get("session_id") is not None else ""
861	
862	    if action == "list":
863	        return _json.dumps({"processes": process_registry.list_sessions(task_id=task_id)}, ensure_ascii=False)
864	    elif action in ("poll", "log", "wait", "kill", "write", "submit"):
865	        if not session_id:
866	            return _json.dumps({"error": f"session_id is required for {action}"}, ensure_ascii=False)
867	        if action == "poll":
868	            return _json.dumps(process_registry.poll(session_id), ensure_ascii=False)
869	        elif action == "log":
870	            return _json.dumps(process_registry.read_log(
871	                session_id, offset=args.get("offset", 0), limit=args.get("limit", 200)), ensure_ascii=False)
872	        elif action == "wait":
873	            return _json.dumps(process_registry.wait(session_id, timeout=args.get("timeout")), ensure_ascii=False)
874	        elif action == "kill":
875	            return _json.dumps(process_registry.kill_process(session_id), ensure_ascii=False)
876	        elif action == "write":
877	            return _json.dumps(process_registry.write_stdin(session_id, str(args.get("data", ""))), ensure_ascii=False)
878	        elif action == "submit":
879	            return _json.dumps(process_registry.submit_stdin(session_id, str(args.get("data", ""))), ensure_ascii=False)
880	    return _json.dumps({"error": f"Unknown process action: {action}. Use: list, poll, log, wait, kill, write, submit"}, ensure_ascii=False)
881	
882	
883	registry.register(
884	    name="process",
885	    toolset="terminal",
886	    schema=PROCESS_SCHEMA,
887	    handler=_handle_process,
888	    emoji="⚙️",
889	)
890
```
</details></tool-use>

1	"""Local execution environment with interrupt support and non-blocking I/O."""
2	
3	import glob
4	import os
5	import platform
6	import shutil
7	import signal
8	import subprocess
9	import threading
10	import time
11	
12	_IS_WINDOWS = platform.system() == "Windows"
13	
14	from tools.environments.base import BaseEnvironment
15	from tools.environments.persistent_shell import PersistentShellMixin
16	from tools.interrupt import is_interrupted
17	
18	# Unique marker to isolate real command output from shell init/exit noise.
19	# printf (no trailing newline) keeps the boundaries clean for splitting.
20	_OUTPUT_FENCE = "__HERMES_FENCE_a9f7b3__"
21	
22	# Hermes-internal env vars that should NOT leak into terminal subprocesses.
23	# These are loaded from ~/.hermes/.env for Hermes' own LLM/provider calls
24	# but can break external CLIs (e.g. codex) that also honor them.
25	# See: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/1002
26	#
27	# Built dynamically from the provider registry so new providers are
28	# automatically covered without manual blocklist maintenance.
29	_HERMES_PROVIDER_ENV_FORCE_PREFIX = "_HERMES_FORCE_"
30	
31	
32	def _build_provider_env_blocklist() -> frozenset:
33	    """Derive the blocklist from provider, tool, and gateway config.
34	
35	    Automatically picks up api_key_env_vars and base_url_env_var from
36	    every registered provider, plus tool/messaging env vars from the
37	    optional config registry, so new Hermes-managed secrets are blocked
38	    in subprocesses without having to maintain multiple static lists.
39	    """
40	    blocked: set[str] = set()
41	
42	    try:
43	        from hermes_cli.auth import PROVIDER_REGISTRY
44	        for pconfig in PROVIDER_REGISTRY.values():
45	            blocked.update(pconfig.api_key_env_vars)
46	            if pconfig.base_url_env_var:
47	                blocked.add(pconfig.base_url_env_var)
48	    except ImportError:
49	        pass
50	
51	    try:
52	        from hermes_cli.config import OPTIONAL_ENV_VARS
53	        for name, metadata in OPTIONAL_ENV_VARS.items():
54	            category = metadata.get("category")
55	            if category in {"tool", "messaging"}:
56	                blocked.add(name)
57	            elif category == "setting" and metadata.get("password"):
58	                blocked.add(name)
59	    except ImportError:
60	        pass
61	
62	    # Vars not covered above but still Hermes-internal / conflict-prone.
63	    blocked.update({
64	        "OPENAI_BASE_URL",
65	        "OPENAI_API_KEY",
66	        "OPENAI_API_BASE",         # legacy alias
67	        "OPENAI_ORG_ID",
68	        "OPENAI_ORGANIZATION",
69	        "OPENROUTER_API_KEY",
70	        "ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL",
71	        "ANTHROPIC_TOKEN",         # OAuth token (not in registry as env var)
72	        "CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN",
73	        "LLM_MODEL",
74	        # Expanded isolation for other major providers (Issue #1002)
75	        "GOOGLE_API_KEY",          # Gemini / Google AI Studio
76	        "DEEPSEEK_API_KEY",        # DeepSeek
77	        "MISTRAL_API_KEY",         # Mistral AI
78	        "GROQ_API_KEY",            # Groq
79	        "TOGETHER_API_KEY",        # Together AI
80	        "PERPLEXITY_API_KEY",      # Perplexity
81	        "COHERE_API_KEY",          # Cohere
82	        "FIREWORKS_API_KEY",       # Fireworks AI
83	        "XAI_API_KEY",             # xAI (Grok)
84	        "HELICONE_API_KEY",        # LLM Observability proxy
85	        "PARALLEL_API_KEY",
86	        "FIRECRAWL_API_KEY",
87	        "FIRECRAWL_API_URL",
88	        # Gateway/runtime config not represented in OPTIONAL_ENV_VARS.
89	        "TELEGRAM_HOME_CHANNEL",
90	        "TELEGRAM_HOME_CHANNEL_NAME",
91	        "DISCORD_HOME_CHANNEL",
92	        "DISCORD_HOME_CHANNEL_NAME",
93	        "DISCORD_REQUIRE_MENTION",
94	        "DISCORD_FREE_RESPONSE_CHANNELS",
95	        "DISCORD_AUTO_THREAD",
96	        "SLACK_HOME_CHANNEL",
97	        "SLACK_HOME_CHANNEL_NAME",
98	        "SLACK_ALLOWED_USERS",

100	        "WHATSAPP_MODE",
101	        "WHATSAPP_ALLOWED_USERS",
102	        "SIGNAL_HTTP_URL",
103	        "SIGNAL_ACCOUNT",
104	        "SIGNAL_ALLOWED_USERS",
105	        "SIGNAL_GROUP_ALLOWED_USERS",
106	        "SIGNAL_HOME_CHANNEL",
107	        "SIGNAL_HOME_CHANNEL_NAME",
108	        "SIGNAL_IGNORE_STORIES",
109	        "HASS_TOKEN",

114	        "EMAIL_SMTP_HOST",

116	        "EMAIL_HOME_ADDRESS_NAME",
117	        "GATEWAY_ALLOWED_USERS",
118	        # Skills Hub / GitHub app auth paths and aliases.
119	        "GH_TOKEN",
120	        "GITHUB_APP_ID",
121	        "GITHUB_APP_PRIVATE_KEY_PATH",
122	        "GITHUB_APP_INSTALLATION_ID",
123	        # Remote sandbox backend credentials.
124	        "MODAL_TOKEN_ID",
125	        "MODAL_TOKEN_SECRET",
126	        "DAYTONA_API_KEY",
127	    })
128	    return frozenset(blocked)
129	
130	
131	_HERMES_PROVIDER_ENV_BLOCKLIST = _build_provider_env_blocklist()
132	
133	
134	def _sanitize_subprocess_env(base_env: dict | None, extra_env: dict | None = None) -> dict:
135	    """Filter Hermes-managed secrets from a subprocess environment.
136	
137	    `_HERMES_FORCE_<VAR>` entries in ``extra_env`` opt a blocked variable back in
138	    intentionally for callers that truly need it.  Vars registered via
139	    :mod:`tools.env_passthrough` (skill-declared or user-configured) also
140	    bypass the blocklist.
141	    """
142	    try:
143	        from tools.env_passthrough import is_env_passthrough as _is_passthrough
144	    except Exception:
145	        _is_passthrough = lambda _: False  # noqa: E731
146	
147	    sanitized: dict[str, str] = {}
148	
149	    for key, value in (base_env or {}).items():
150	        if key.startswith(_HERMES_PROVIDER_ENV_FORCE_PREFIX):
151	            continue
152	        if key not in _HERMES_PROVIDER_ENV_BLOCKLIST or _is_passthrough(key):
153	            sanitized[key] = value
154	
155	    for key, value in (extra_env or {}).items():
156	        if key.startswith(_HERMES_PROVIDER_ENV_FORCE_PREFIX):
157	            real_key = key[len(_HERMES_PROVIDER_ENV_FORCE_PREFIX):]
158	            sanitized[real_key] = value
159	        elif key not in _HERMES_PROVIDER_ENV_BLOCKLIST or _is_passthrough(key):
160	            sanitized[key] = value
161	
162	    return sanitized
163	
164	
165	def _find_bash() -> str:
166	    """Find bash for command execution.
167	
168	    The fence wrapper uses bash syntax (semicolons, $?, printf), so we
169	    must use bash — not the user's $SHELL which could be fish/zsh/etc.
170	    On Windows: uses Git Bash (bundled with Git for Windows).
171	    """
172	    if not _IS_WINDOWS:
173	        return (
174	            shutil.which("bash")
175	            or ("/usr/bin/bash" if os.path.isfile("/usr/bin/bash") else None)
176	            or ("/bin/bash" if os.path.isfile("/bin/bash") else None)
177	            or os.environ.get("SHELL")  # last resort: whatever they have
178	            or "/bin/sh"
179	        )
180	
181	    # Windows: look for Git Bash (installed with Git for Windows).
182	    # Allow override via env var (same pattern as Claude Code).
183	    custom = os.environ.get("HERMES_GIT_BASH_PATH")
184	    if custom and os.path.isfile(custom):
185	        return custom
186	
187	    # shutil.which finds bash.exe if Git\bin is on PATH
188	    found = shutil.which("bash")
189	    if found:
190	        return found
191	
192	    # Check common Git for Windows install locations
193	    for candidate in (
194	        os.path.join(os.environ.get("ProgramFiles", r"C:\Program Files"), "Git", "bin", "bash.exe"),
195	        os.path.join(os.environ.get("ProgramFiles(x86)", r"C:\Program Files (x86)"), "Git", "bin", "bash.exe"),
196	        os.path.join(os.environ.get("LOCALAPPDATA", ""), "Programs", "Git", "bin", "bash.exe"),
197	    ):
198	        if candidate and os.path.isfile(candidate):
199	            return candidate
200

 `/c/Users/downl/Desktop/hermes-agent-main/hermes-agent-main/tools/environments/persistent_shell.py`

```text
1	"""Persistent shell mixin: file-based IPC protocol for long-lived bash shells."""
2	
3	import logging
4	import shlex
5	import subprocess
6	import threading
7	import time
8	import uuid
9	from abc import abstractmethod
10	
11	from tools.interrupt import is_interrupted
12	
13	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
14	
15	
16	class PersistentShellMixin:
17	    """Mixin that adds persistent shell capability to any BaseEnvironment.
18	
19	    Subclasses must implement ``_spawn_shell_process()``, ``_read_temp_files()``,
20	    ``_kill_shell_children()``, ``_execute_oneshot()``, and ``_cleanup_temp_files()``.
21	    """
22	
23	    persistent: bool
24	
25	    @abstractmethod
26	    def _spawn_shell_process(self) -> subprocess.Popen: ...
27	
28	    @abstractmethod
29	    def _read_temp_files(self, *paths: str) -> list[str]: ...
30	
31	    @abstractmethod
32	    def _kill_shell_children(self): ...
33	
34	    @abstractmethod
35	    def _execute_oneshot(self, command: str, cwd: str, *,
36	                         timeout: int | None = None,
37	                         stdin_data: str | None = None) -> dict: ...
38	
39	    @abstractmethod
40	    def _cleanup_temp_files(self): ...
41	
42	    _session_id: str = ""
43	    _poll_interval_start: float = 0.01  # initial poll interval (10ms)
44	    _poll_interval_max: float = 0.25    # max poll interval (250ms) — reduces I/O for long commands
45	
46	    @property
47	    def _temp_prefix(self) -> str:
48	        return f"/tmp/hermes-persistent-{self._session_id}"
49	
50	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
51	    # Lifecycle
52	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
53	
54	    def _init_persistent_shell(self):
55	        self._shell_lock = threading.Lock()
56	        self._shell_proc: subprocess.Popen | None = None
57	        self._shell_alive: bool = False
58	        self._shell_pid: int | None = None
59	
60	        self._session_id = uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]
61	        p = self._temp_prefix
62	        self._pshell_stdout = f"{p}-stdout"
63	        self._pshell_stderr = f"{p}-stderr"
64	        self._pshell_status = f"{p}-status"
65	        self._pshell_cwd = f"{p}-cwd"
66	        self._pshell_pid_file = f"{p}-pid"
67	
68	        self._shell_proc = self._spawn_shell_process()
69	        self._shell_alive = True
70	
71	        self._drain_thread = threading.Thread(
72	            target=self._drain_shell_output, daemon=True,
73	        )
74	        self._drain_thread.start()
75	
76	        init_script = (
77	            f"export TERM=${{TERM:-dumb}}\n"
78	            f"touch {self._pshell_stdout} {self._pshell_stderr} "
79	            f"{self._pshell_status} {self._pshell_cwd} {self._pshell_pid_file}\n"
80	            f"echo $$ > {self._pshell_pid_file}\n"
81	            f"pwd > {self._pshell_cwd}\n"
82	        )
83	        self._send_to_shell(init_script)
84	
85	        deadline = time.monotonic() + 3.0
86	        while time.monotonic() < deadline:
87	            pid_str = self._read_temp_files(self._pshell_pid_file)[0].strip()
88	            if pid_str.isdigit():
89	                self._shell_pid = int(pid_str)
90	                break
91	            time.sleep(0.05)
92	        else:
93	            logger.warning("Could not read persistent shell PID")
94	            self._shell_pid = None
95	
96	        if self._shell_pid:
97	            logger.info(
98	                "Persistent shell started (session=%s, pid=%d)",
99	                self._session_id, self._shell_pid,
100	            )
101	
102	        reported_cwd = self._read_temp_files(self._pshell_cwd)[0].strip()
103	        if reported_cwd:
104	            self.cwd = reported_cwd
105	
106	    def _cleanup_persistent_shell(self):
107	        if self._shell_proc is None:
108	            return
109	
110	        if self._session_id:
111	            self._cleanup_temp_files()
112	
113	        try:
114	            self._shell_proc.stdin.close()
115	        except Exception:
116	            pass
117	        try:
118	            self._shell_proc.terminate()
119	            self._shell_proc.wait(timeout=3)
120	        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
121	            self._shell_proc.kill()
122	
123	        self._shell_alive = False
124	        self._shell_proc = None
125	
126	        if hasattr(self, "_drain_thread") and self._drain_thread.is_alive():
127	            self._drain_thread.join(timeout=1.0)
128	
129	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
130	    # execute() / cleanup() — shared dispatcher, subclasses inherit
131	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
132	
133	    def execute(self, command: str, cwd: str = "", *,
134	                timeout: int | None = None,
135	                stdin_data: str | None = None) -> dict:
136	        if self.persistent:
137	            return self._execute_persistent(
138	                command, cwd, timeout=timeout, stdin_data=stdin_data,
139	            )
140	        return self._execute_oneshot(
141	            command, cwd, timeout=timeout, stdin_data=stdin_data,
142	        )
143	
144	    def cleanup(self):
145	        if self.persistent:
146	            self._cleanup_persistent_shell()
147	
148	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
149	    # Shell I/O
150	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
151	
152	    def _drain_shell_output(self):
153	        try:
154	            for _ in self._shell_proc.stdout:
155	                pass
156	        except Exception:
157	            pass
158	        self._shell_alive = False
159	
160	    def _send_to_shell(self, text: str):
161	        if not self._shell_alive or self._shell_proc is None:
162	            return
163	        try:
164	            self._shell_proc.stdin.write(text)
165	            self._shell_proc.stdin.flush()
166	        except (BrokenPipeError, OSError):
167	            self._shell_alive = False
168	
169	    def _read_persistent_output(self) -> tuple[str, int, str]:
170	        stdout, stderr, status_raw, cwd = self._read_temp_files(
171	            self._pshell_stdout, self._pshell_stderr,
172	            self._pshell_status, self._pshell_cwd,
173	        )
174	        output = self._merge_output(stdout, stderr)
175	        status = status_raw.strip()
176	        if ":" in status:
177	            status = status.split(":", 1)[1]
178	        try:
179	            exit_code = int(status.strip())
180	        except ValueError:
181	            exit_code = 1
182	        return output, exit_code, cwd.strip()
183	
184	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
185	    # Execution
186	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
187	
188	    def _execute_persistent(self, command: str, cwd: str, *,
189	                            timeout: int | None = None,
190	                            stdin_data: str | None = None) -> dict:
191	        if not self._shell_alive:
192	            logger.info("Persistent shell died, restarting...")
193	            self._init_persistent_shell()
194	
195	        exec_command, sudo_stdin = self._prepare_command(command)
196	        effective_timeout = timeout or self.timeout
197	        if stdin_data or sudo_stdin:
198	            return self._execute_oneshot(
199	                command, cwd, timeout=timeout, stdin_data=stdin_data,
200	            )
```

201	    raise RuntimeError(

208	# Backward compat — process_registry.py imports this name
209	_find_shell = _find_bash
210	
211	
212	# Noise lines emitted by interactive shells when stdin is not a terminal.
213	# Used as a fallback when output fence markers are missing.
214	_SHELL_NOISE_SUBSTRINGS = (
215	    # bash
216	    "bash: cannot set terminal process group",
217	    "bash: no job control in this shell",
218	    "no job control in this shell",
219	    "cannot set terminal process group",
220	    "tcsetattr: Inappropriate ioctl for device",
221	    # zsh / oh-my-zsh / macOS terminal session
222	    "Restored session:",
223	    "Saving session...",
224	    "Last login:",
225	    "command not found:",
226	    "Oh My Zsh",
227	    "compinit:",
228	)
229	
230	
231	def _clean_shell_noise(output: str) -> str:
232	    """Strip shell startup/exit warnings that leak when using -i without a TTY.
233	
234	    Removes lines matching known noise patterns from both the beginning
235	    and end of the output.  Lines in the middle are left untouched.
236	    """
237	
238	    def _is_noise(line: str) -> bool:
239	        return any(noise in line for noise in _SHELL_NOISE_SUBSTRINGS)
240	
241	    lines = output.split("\n")
242	
243	    # Strip leading noise
244	    while lines and _is_noise(lines[0]):
245	        lines.pop(0)
246	
247	    # Strip trailing noise (walk backwards, skip empty lines from split)
248	    end = len(lines) - 1
249	    while end >= 0 and (not lines[end] or _is_noise(lines[end])):
250	        end -= 1
251	
252	    if end < 0:
253	        return ""
254	
255	    cleaned = lines[: end + 1]
256	    result = "\n".join(cleaned)
257	
258	    # Preserve trailing newline if original had one
259	    if output.endswith("\n") and result and not result.endswith("\n"):
260	        result += "\n"
261	    return result
262	
263	
264	# Standard PATH entries for environments with minimal PATH (e.g. systemd services).
265	# Includes macOS Homebrew paths (/opt/homebrew/* for Apple Silicon).
266	_SANE_PATH = (
267	    "/opt/homebrew/bin:/opt/homebrew/sbin:"
268	    "/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin"
269	)
270	
271	
272	def _make_run_env(env: dict) -> dict:
273	    """Build a run environment with a sane PATH and provider-var stripping."""
274	    try:
275	        from tools.env_passthrough import is_env_passthrough as _is_passthrough
276	    except Exception:
277	        _is_passthrough = lambda _: False  # noqa: E731
278	
279	    merged = dict(os.environ | env)
280	    run_env = {}
281	    for k, v in merged.items():
282	        if k.startswith(_HERMES_PROVIDER_ENV_FORCE_PREFIX):
283	            real_key = k[len(_HERMES_PROVIDER_ENV_FORCE_PREFIX):]
284	            run_env[real_key] = v
285	        elif k not in _HERMES_PROVIDER_ENV_BLOCKLIST or _is_passthrough(k):
286	            run_env[k] = v
287	    existing_path = run_env.get("PATH", "")
288	    if "/usr/bin" not in existing_path.split(":"):
289	        run_env["PATH"] = f"{existing_path}:{_SANE_PATH}" if existing_path else _SANE_PATH
290	    return run_env
291	
292	
293	def _extract_fenced_output(raw: str) -> str:
294	    """Extract real command output from between fence markers.
295	
296	    The execute() method wraps each command with printf(FENCE) markers.
297	    This function finds the first and last fence and returns only the
298	    content between them, which is the actual command output free of
299	    any shell init/exit noise.
300	
301	    Falls back to pattern-based _clean_shell_noise if fences are missing.
302	    """
303	    first = raw.find(_OUTPUT_FENCE)
304	    if first == -1:
305	        return _clean_shell_noise(raw)
306	
307	    start = first + len(_OUTPUT_FENCE)
308	    last = raw.rfind(_OUTPUT_FENCE)
309	
310	    if last <= first:
311	        # Only start fence found (e.g. user command called `exit`)
312	        return _clean_shell_noise(raw[start:])
313	
314	    return raw[start:last]
315	
316	
317	class LocalEnvironment(PersistentShellMixin, BaseEnvironment):
318	    """Run commands directly on the host machine.
319	
320	    Features:
321	    - Popen + polling for interrupt support (user can cancel mid-command)
322	    - Background stdout drain thread to prevent pipe buffer deadlocks
323	    - stdin_data support for piping content (bypasses ARG_MAX limits)
324	    - sudo -S transform via SUDO_PASSWORD env var
325	    - Uses interactive login shell so full user env is available
326	    - Optional persistent shell mode (cwd/env vars survive across calls)
327	    """
328	
329	    def __init__(self, cwd: str = "", timeout: int = 60, env: dict = None,
330	                 persistent: bool = False):
331	        super().__init__(cwd=cwd or os.getcwd(), timeout=timeout, env=env)
332	        self.persistent = persistent
333	        if self.persistent:
334	            self._init_persistent_shell()
335	
336	    @property
337	    def _temp_prefix(self) -> str:
338	        return f"/tmp/hermes-local-{self._session_id}"
339	
340	    def _spawn_shell_process(self) -> subprocess.Popen:
341	        user_shell = _find_bash()
342	        run_env = _make_run_env(self.env)
343	        return subprocess.Popen(
344	            [user_shell, "-l"],
345	            stdin=subprocess.PIPE,
346	            stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
347	            stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL,
348	            text=True,
349	            env=run_env,
350	            preexec_fn=None if _IS_WINDOWS else os.setsid,
351	        )
352	
353	    def _read_temp_files(self, *paths: str) -> list[str]:
354	        results = []
355	        for path in paths:
356	            if os.path.exists(path):
357	                with open(path) as f:
358	                    results.append(f.read())
359	            else:
360	                results.append("")
361	        return results
362	
363	    def _kill_shell_children(self):
364	        if self._shell_pid is None:
365	            return
366	        try:
367	            subprocess.run(
368	                ["pkill", "-P", str(self._shell_pid)],
369	                capture_output=True, timeout=5,
370	            )
371	        except (subprocess.TimeoutExpired, FileNotFoundError):
372	            pass
373	
374	    def _cleanup_temp_files(self):
375	        for f in glob.glob(f"{self._temp_prefix}-*"):
376	            if os.path.exists(f):
377	                os.remove(f)
378	
379	    def _execute_oneshot(self, command: str, cwd: str = "", *,
380	                         timeout: int | None = None,
381	                         stdin_data: str | None = None) -> dict:
382	        work_dir = cwd or self.cwd or os.getcwd()
383	        effective_timeout = timeout or self.timeout
384	        exec_command, sudo_stdin = self._prepare_command(command)
385	
386	        if sudo_stdin is not None and stdin_data is not None:
387	            effective_stdin = sudo_stdin + stdin_data
388	        elif sudo_stdin is not None:
389	            effective_stdin = sudo_stdin
390	        else:
391	            effective_stdin = stdin_data
392	
393	        user_shell = _find_bash()
394	        # Newline-separated wrapper (not `cmd; __hermes_rc=...` on one line).
395	        # A trailing `; __hermes_rc` glued to `<<EOF` / a closing `EOF` line breaks
396	        # heredoc parsing: the delimiter must be alone on its line, otherwise the
397	        # rest of this script becomes heredoc body and leaks into stdout (e.g. gh
398	        # issue/PR flows that use here-documents for bodies).
399	        fenced_cmd = (
400	            f"printf '{_OUTPUT_FENCE}'\n"
401	            f"{exec_command}\n"
402	            f"__hermes_rc=$?\n"
403	            f"printf '{_OUTPUT_FENCE}'\n"
404	            f"exit $__hermes_rc\n"
405	        )
406	        run_env = _make_run_env(self.env)
407	
408	        proc = subprocess.Popen(
409	            [user_shell, "-lic", fenced_cmd],
410	            text=True,
411	            cwd=work_dir,
412	            env=run_env,
413	            encoding="utf-8",
414	            errors="replace",
415	            stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
416	            stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
417	            stdin=subprocess.PIPE if effective_stdin is not None else subprocess.DEVNULL,
418	            preexec_fn=None if _IS_WINDOWS else os.setsid,
419	        )
420	
421	        if effective_stdin is not None:
422	            def _write_stdin():
423	                try:
424	                    proc.stdin.write(effective_stdin)
425	                    proc.stdin.close()
426	                except (BrokenPipeError, OSError):
427	                    pass
428	            threading.Thread(target=_write_stdin, daemon=True).start()
429	
430	        _output_chunks: list[str] = []
431	
432	        def _drain_stdout():
433	            try:
434	                for line in proc.stdout:
435	                    _output_chunks.append(line)
436	            except ValueError:
437	                pass
438	            finally:
439	                try:
440	                    proc.stdout.close()
441	                except Exception:
442	                    pass
443	
444	        reader = threading.Thread(target=_drain_stdout, daemon=True)
445	        reader.start()
446	        deadline = time.monotonic() + effective_timeout
447	
448	        while proc.poll() is None:
449	            if is_interrupted():
450	                try:
451	                    if _IS_WINDOWS:
452	                        proc.terminate()
453	                    else:
454	                        pgid = os.getpgid(proc.pid)
455	                        os.killpg(pgid, signal.SIGTERM)
456	                        try:
457	                            proc.wait(timeout=1.0)
458	                        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
459	                            os.killpg(pgid, signal.SIGKILL)
460	                except (ProcessLookupError, PermissionError):
461	                    proc.kill()
462	                reader.join(timeout=2)
463	                return {
464	                    "output": "".join(_output_chunks) + "\n[Command interrupted — user sent a new message]",
465	                    "returncode": 130,
466	                }
467	            if time.monotonic() > deadline:
468	                try:
469	                    if _IS_WINDOWS:
470	                        proc.terminate()
471	                    else:
472	                        os.killpg(os.getpgid(proc.pid), signal.SIGTERM)
473	                except (ProcessLookupError, PermissionError):
474	                    proc.kill()
475	                reader.join(timeout=2)
476	                partial = "".join(_output_chunks)
477	                timeout_msg = f"\n[Command timed out after {effective_timeout}s]"
478	                return {
479	                    "output": partial + timeout_msg if partial else timeout_msg.lstrip(),
480	                    "returncode": 124,
481	                }
482	            time.sleep(0.2)
483	
484	        reader.join(timeout=5)
485	        output = _extract_fenced_output("".join(_output_chunks))
486	        return {"output": output, "returncode": proc.returncode}
487

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>

 `/c/Users/downl/Desktop/hermes-agent-main/hermes-agent-main/tools/code_execution_tool.py`

```text
1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""
3	Code Execution Tool -- Programmatic Tool Calling (PTC)
4	
5	Lets the LLM write a Python script that calls Hermes tools via RPC,
6	collapsing multi-step tool chains into a single inference turn.
7	
8	Architecture:
9	  1. Parent generates a `hermes_tools.py` stub module with RPC functions
10	  2. Parent opens a Unix domain socket and starts an RPC listener thread
11	  3. Parent spawns a child process that runs the LLM's script
12	  4. When the script calls a tool function, the call travels over the UDS
13	     back to the parent, which dispatches through handle_function_call
14	  5. Only the script's stdout is returned to the LLM; intermediate tool
15	     results never enter the context window
16	
17	Platform: Linux / macOS only (Unix domain sockets). Disabled on Windows.
18	"""
19	
20	import json
21	import logging
22	import os
23	import platform
24	import signal
25	import socket
26	import subprocess
27	import sys
28	import tempfile
29	import threading
30	import time
31	import uuid
32	
33	_IS_WINDOWS = platform.system() == "Windows"
34	from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
35	
36	# Availability gate: UDS requires a POSIX OS
37	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
38	
39	SANDBOX_AVAILABLE = sys.platform != "win32"
40	
41	# The 7 tools allowed inside the sandbox. The intersection of this list
42	# and the session's enabled tools determines which stubs are generated.
43	SANDBOX_ALLOWED_TOOLS = frozenset([
44	    "web_search",
45	    "web_extract",
46	    "read_file",
47	    "write_file",
48	    "search_files",
49	    "patch",
50	    "terminal",
51	])
52	
53	# Resource limit defaults (overridable via config.yaml → code_execution.*)
54	DEFAULT_TIMEOUT = 300        # 5 minutes
55	DEFAULT_MAX_TOOL_CALLS = 50
56	MAX_STDOUT_BYTES = 50_000    # 50 KB
57	MAX_STDERR_BYTES = 10_000    # 10 KB
58	
59	
60	def check_sandbox_requirements() -> bool:
61	    """Code execution sandbox requires a POSIX OS for Unix domain sockets."""
62	    return SANDBOX_AVAILABLE
63	
64	
65	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
66	# hermes_tools.py code generator
67	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
68	
69	# Per-tool stub templates: (function_name, signature, docstring, args_dict_expr)
70	# The args_dict_expr builds the JSON payload sent over the RPC socket.
71	_TOOL_STUBS = {
72	    "web_search": (
73	        "web_search",
74	        "query: str, limit: int = 5",
75	        '"""Search the web. Returns dict with data.web list of {url, title, description}."""',
76	        '{"query": query, "limit": limit}',
77	    ),
78	    "web_extract": (
79	        "web_extract",
80	        "urls: list",
81	        '"""Extract content from URLs. Returns dict with results list of {url, title, content, error}."""',
82	        '{"urls": urls}',
83	    ),
84	    "read_file": (
85	        "read_file",
86	        "path: str, offset: int = 1, limit: int = 500",
87	        '"""Read a file (1-indexed lines). Returns dict with "content" and "total_lines"."""',
88	        '{"path": path, "offset": offset, "limit": limit}',
89	    ),
90	    "write_file": (
91	        "write_file",
92	        "path: str, content: str",
93	        '"""Write content to a file (always overwrites). Returns dict with status."""',
94	        '{"path": path, "content": content}',
95	    ),
96	    "search_files": (
97	        "search_files",
98	        'pattern: str, target: str = "content", path: str = ".", file_glob: str = None, limit: int = 50, offset: int = 0, output_mode: str = "content", context: int = 0',
99	        '"""Search file contents (target="content") or find files by name (target="files"). Returns dict with "matches"."""',
100	        '{"pattern": pattern, "target": target, "path": path, "file_glob": file_glob, "limit": limit, "offset": offset, "output_mode": output_mode, "context": context}',
101	    ),
102	    "patch": (
103	        "patch",
104	        'path: str = None, old_string: str = None, new_string: str = None, replace_all: bool = False, mode: str = "replace", patch: str = None',
105	        '"""Targeted find-and-replace (mode="replace") or V4A multi-file patches (mode="patch"). Returns dict with status."""',
106	        '{"path": path, "old_string": old_string, "new_string": new_string, "replace_all": replace_all, "mode": mode, "patch": patch}',
107	    ),
108	    "terminal": (
109	        "terminal",
110	        "command: str, timeout: int = None, workdir: str = None",
111	        '"""Run a shell command (foreground only). Returns dict with "output" and "exit_code"."""',
112	        '{"command": command, "timeout": timeout, "workdir": workdir}',
113	    ),
114	}
115	
116	
117	def generate_hermes_tools_module(enabled_tools: List[str]) -> str:
118	    """
119	    Build the source code for the hermes_tools.py stub module.
120	
121	    Only tools in both SANDBOX_ALLOWED_TOOLS and enabled_tools get stubs.
122	    """
123	    tools_to_generate = sorted(SANDBOX_ALLOWED_TOOLS & set(enabled_tools))
124	
125	    stub_functions = []
126	    export_names = []
127	    for tool_name in tools_to_generate:
128	        if tool_name not in _TOOL_STUBS:
129	            continue
130	        func_name, sig, doc, args_expr = _TOOL_STUBS[tool_name]
131	        stub_functions.append(
132	            f"def {func_name}({sig}):\n"
133	            f"    {doc}\n"
134	            f"    return _call({func_name!r}, {args_expr})\n"
135	        )
136	        export_names.append(func_name)
137	
138	    header = '''\
139	"""Auto-generated Hermes tools RPC stubs."""
140	import json, os, socket, shlex, time
141	
142	_sock = None
143	
144	
145	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
146	# Convenience helpers (avoid common scripting pitfalls)
147	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
148	
149	def json_parse(text: str):
150	    """Parse JSON tolerant of control characters (strict=False).
151	    Use this instead of json.loads() when parsing output from terminal()
152	    or web_extract() that may contain raw tabs/newlines in strings."""
153	    return json.loads(text, strict=False)
154	
155	
156	def shell_quote(s: str) -> str:
157	    """Shell-escape a string for safe interpolation into commands.
158	    Use this when inserting dynamic content into terminal() commands:
159	        terminal(f"echo {shell_quote(user_input)}")
160	    """
161	    return shlex.quote(s)
162	
163	
164	def retry(fn, max_attempts=3, delay=2):
165	    """Retry a function up to max_attempts times with exponential backoff.
166	    Use for transient failures (network errors, API rate limits):
167	        result = retry(lambda: terminal("gh issue list ..."))
168	    """
169	    last_err = None
170	    for attempt in range(max_attempts):
171	        try:
172	            return fn()
173	        except Exception as e:
174	            last_err = e
175	            if attempt < max_attempts - 1:
176	                time.sleep(delay * (2 ** attempt))
177	    raise last_err
178	
179	def _connect():
180	    global _sock
181	    if _sock is None:
182	        _sock = socket.socket(socket.AF_UNIX, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
183	        _sock.connect(os.environ["HERMES_RPC_SOCKET"])
184	        _sock.settimeout(300)
185	    return _sock
186	
187	def _call(tool_name, args):
188	    """Send a tool call to the parent process and return the parsed result."""
189	    conn = _connect()
190	    request = json.dumps({"tool": tool_name, "args": args}) + "\\n"
191	    conn.sendall(request.encode())
192	    buf = b""
193	    while True:
194	        chunk = conn.recv(65536)
195	        if not chunk:
196	            raise RuntimeError("Agent process disconnected")
197	        buf += chunk
198	        if buf.endswith(b"\\n"):
199	            break
200	    raw = buf.decode().strip()
```
</details></tool-use>

1	"""
2	Multi-provider authentication system for Hermes Agent.
3	
4	Supports OAuth device code flows (Nous Portal, future: OpenAI Codex) and
5	traditional API key providers (OpenRouter, custom endpoints). Auth state
6	is persisted in ~/.hermes/auth.json with cross-process file locking.
7	
8	Architecture:
9	- ProviderConfig registry defines known OAuth providers
10	- Auth store (auth.json) holds per-provider credential state
11	- resolve_provider() picks the active provider via priority chain
12	- resolve_*_runtime_credentials() handles token refresh and key minting
13	- logout_command() is the CLI entry point for clearing auth
14	"""
15	
16	from __future__ import annotations
17	
18	import json
19	import logging
20	import os
21	import shutil
22	import shlex
23	import stat
24	import base64
25	import hashlib
26	import subprocess
27	import threading
28	import time
29	import uuid
30	import webbrowser
31	from contextlib import contextmanager
32	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
33	from datetime import datetime, timezone
34	from pathlib import Path
35	from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
36	
37	import httpx
38	import yaml
39	
40	from hermes_cli.config import get_hermes_home, get_config_path
41	from hermes_constants import OPENROUTER_BASE_URL
42	
43	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
44	
45	try:
46	    import fcntl
47	except Exception:
48	    fcntl = None
49	try:
50	    import msvcrt
51	except Exception:
52	    msvcrt = None
53	
54	# =============================================================================
55	# Constants
56	# =============================================================================
57	
58	AUTH_STORE_VERSION = 1
59	AUTH_LOCK_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 15.0
60	
61	# Nous Portal defaults
62	DEFAULT_NOUS_PORTAL_URL = "https://portal.nousresearch.com"
63	DEFAULT_NOUS_INFERENCE_URL = "https://inference-api.nousresearch.com/v1"
64	DEFAULT_NOUS_CLIENT_ID = "hermes-cli"
65	DEFAULT_NOUS_SCOPE = "inference:mint_agent_key"
66	DEFAULT_AGENT_KEY_MIN_TTL_SECONDS = 30 * 60  # 30 minutes
67	ACCESS_TOKEN_REFRESH_SKEW_SECONDS = 120       # refresh 2 min before expiry
68	DEVICE_AUTH_POLL_INTERVAL_CAP_SECONDS = 1     # poll at most every 1s
69	DEFAULT_CODEX_BASE_URL = "https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex"
70	DEFAULT_GITHUB_MODELS_BASE_URL = "https://api.githubcopilot.com"
71	DEFAULT_COPILOT_ACP_BASE_URL = "acp://copilot"
72	CODEX_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID = "app_EMoamEEZ73f0CkXaXp7hrann"
73	CODEX_OAUTH_TOKEN_URL = "https://auth.openai.com/oauth/token"
74	CODEX_ACCESS_TOKEN_REFRESH_SKEW_SECONDS = 120
75	
76	
77	# =============================================================================
78	# Provider Registry
79	# =============================================================================
80	
81	@dataclass
82	class ProviderConfig:
83	    """Describes a known inference provider."""
84	    id: str
85	    name: str
86	    auth_type: str  # "oauth_device_code", "oauth_external", or "api_key"
87	    portal_base_url: str = ""
88	    inference_base_url: str = ""
89	    client_id: str = ""
90	    scope: str = ""
91	    extra: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
92	    # For API-key providers: env vars to check (in priority order)
93	    api_key_env_vars: tuple = ()
94	    # Optional env var for base URL override
95	    base_url_env_var: str = ""
96	
97
