<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `/c/Users/downl/Desktop/hermes-agent-main/hermes-agent-main/hermes_cli/clipboard.py`

```text
1	"""Clipboard image extraction for macOS, Linux, and WSL2.
2	
3	Provides a single function `save_clipboard_image(dest)` that checks the
4	system clipboard for image data, saves it to *dest* as PNG, and returns
5	True on success.  No external Python dependencies — uses only OS-level
6	CLI tools that ship with the platform (or are commonly installed).
7	
8	Platform support:
9	  macOS  — osascript (always available), pngpaste (if installed)
10	  WSL2   — powershell.exe via .NET System.Windows.Forms.Clipboard
11	  Linux  — wl-paste (Wayland), xclip (X11)
12	"""
13	
14	import base64
15	import logging
16	import os
17	import subprocess
18	import sys
19	from pathlib import Path
20	
21	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
22	
23	# Cache WSL detection (checked once per process)
24	_wsl_detected: bool | None = None
25	
26	
27	def save_clipboard_image(dest: Path) -> bool:
28	    """Extract an image from the system clipboard and save it as PNG.
29	
30	    Returns True if an image was found and saved, False otherwise.
31	    """
32	    dest.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
33	    if sys.platform == "darwin":
34	        return _macos_save(dest)
35	    return _linux_save(dest)
36	
37	
38	def has_clipboard_image() -> bool:
39	    """Quick check: does the clipboard currently contain an image?
40	
41	    Lighter than save_clipboard_image — doesn't extract or write anything.
42	    """
43	    if sys.platform == "darwin":
44	        return _macos_has_image()
45	    if _is_wsl():
46	        return _wsl_has_image()
47	    if os.environ.get("WAYLAND_DISPLAY"):
48	        return _wayland_has_image()
49	    return _xclip_has_image()
50	
51	
52	# ── macOS ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
53	
54	def _macos_save(dest: Path) -> bool:
55	    """Try pngpaste first (fast, handles more formats), fall back to osascript."""
56	    return _macos_pngpaste(dest) or _macos_osascript(dest)
57	
58	
59	def _macos_has_image() -> bool:
60	    """Check if macOS clipboard contains image data."""
61	    try:
62	        info = subprocess.run(
63	            ["osascript", "-e", "clipboard info"],
64	            capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=3,
65	        )
66	        return "«class PNGf»" in info.stdout or "«class TIFF»" in info.stdout
67	    except Exception:
68	        return False
69	
70	
71	def _macos_pngpaste(dest: Path) -> bool:
72	    """Use pngpaste (brew install pngpaste) — fastest, cleanest."""
73	    try:
74	        r = subprocess.run(
75	            ["pngpaste", str(dest)],
76	            capture_output=True, timeout=3,
77	        )
78	        if r.returncode == 0 and dest.exists() and dest.stat().st_size > 0:
79	            return True
80	    except FileNotFoundError:
81	        pass  # pngpaste not installed
82	    except Exception as e:
83	        logger.debug("pngpaste failed: %s", e)
84	    return False
85	
86	
87	def _macos_osascript(dest: Path) -> bool:
88	    """Use osascript to extract PNG data from clipboard (always available)."""
89	    if not _macos_has_image():
90	        return False
91	
92	    # Extract as PNG
93	    script = (
94	        'try\n'
95	        '  set imgData to the clipboard as «class PNGf»\n'
96	        f'  set f to open for access POSIX file "{dest}" with write permission\n'
97	        '  write imgData to f\n'
98	        '  close access f\n'
99	        'on error\n'
100	        '  return "fail"\n'
101	        'end try\n'
102	    )
103	    try:
104	        r = subprocess.run(
105	            ["osascript", "-e", script],
106	            capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5,
107	        )
108	        if r.returncode == 0 and "fail" not in r.stdout and dest.exists() and dest.stat().st_size > 0:
109	            return True
110	    except Exception as e:
111	        logger.debug("osascript clipboard extract failed: %s", e)
112	    return False
113	
114	
115	# ── Linux ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
116	
117	def _is_wsl() -> bool:
118	    """Detect if running inside WSL (1 or 2)."""
119	    global _wsl_detected
120	    if _wsl_detected is not None:
121	        return _wsl_detected
122	    try:
123	        with open("/proc/version", "r") as f:
124	            _wsl_detected = "microsoft" in f.read().lower()
125	    except Exception:
126	        _wsl_detected = False
127	    return _wsl_detected
128	
129	
130	def _linux_save(dest: Path) -> bool:
131	    """Try clipboard backends in priority order: WSL → Wayland → X11."""
132	    if _is_wsl():
133	        if _wsl_save(dest):
134	            return True
135	        # Fall through — WSLg might have wl-paste or xclip working
136	
137	    if os.environ.get("WAYLAND_DISPLAY"):
138	        if _wayland_save(dest):
139	            return True
140	
141	    return _xclip_save(dest)
142	
143	
144	# ── WSL2 (powershell.exe) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
145	
146	# PowerShell script: get clipboard image as base64-encoded PNG on stdout.
147	# Using .NET System.Windows.Forms.Clipboard — always available on Windows.
148	_PS_CHECK_IMAGE = (
149	    "Add-Type -AssemblyName System.Windows.Forms;"
150	    "[System.Windows.Forms.Clipboard]::ContainsImage()"
151	)
152	
153	_PS_EXTRACT_IMAGE = (
154	    "Add-Type -AssemblyName System.Windows.Forms;"
155	    "Add-Type -AssemblyName System.Drawing;"
156	    "$img = [System.Windows.Forms.Clipboard]::GetImage();"
157	    "if ($null -eq $img) { exit 1 }"
158	    "$ms = New-Object System.IO.MemoryStream;"
159	    "$img.Save($ms, [System.Drawing.Imaging.ImageFormat]::Png);"
160	    "[System.Convert]::ToBase64String($ms.ToArray())"
161	)
162	
163	
164	def _wsl_has_image() -> bool:
165	    """Check if Windows clipboard has an image (via powershell.exe)."""
166	    try:
167	        r = subprocess.run(
168	            ["powershell.exe", "-NoProfile", "-NonInteractive", "-Command",
169	             _PS_CHECK_IMAGE],
170	            capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=8,
171	        )
172	        return r.returncode == 0 and "True" in r.stdout
173	    except FileNotFoundError:
174	        logger.debug("powershell.exe not found — WSL clipboard unavailable")
175	    except Exception as e:
176	        logger.debug("WSL clipboard check failed: %s", e)
177	    return False
178	
179	
180	def _wsl_save(dest: Path) -> bool:
181	    """Extract clipboard image via powershell.exe → base64 → decode to PNG."""
182	    try:
183	        r = subprocess.run(
184	            ["powershell.exe", "-NoProfile", "-NonInteractive", "-Command",
185	             _PS_EXTRACT_IMAGE],
186	            capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=15,
187	        )
188	        if r.returncode != 0:
189	            return False
190	
191	        b64_data = r.stdout.strip()
192	        if not b64_data:
193	            return False
194	
195	        png_bytes = base64.b64decode(b64_data)
196	        dest.write_bytes(png_bytes)
197	        return dest.exists() and dest.stat().st_size > 0
198	
199	    except FileNotFoundError:
200	        logger.debug("powershell.exe not found — WSL clipboard unavailable")
201	    except Exception as e:
202	        logger.debug("WSL clipboard extraction failed: %s", e)
203	        dest.unlink(missing_ok=True)
204	    return False
205	
206	
207	# ── Wayland (wl-paste) ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
208	
209	def _wayland_has_image() -> bool:
210	    """Check if Wayland clipboard has image content."""
211	    try:
212	        r = subprocess.run(
213	            ["wl-paste", "--list-types"],
214	            capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=3,
215	        )
216	        return r.returncode == 0 and any(
217	            t.startswith("image/") for t in r.stdout.splitlines()
218	        )
219	    except FileNotFoundError:
220	        logger.debug("wl-paste not installed — Wayland clipboard unavailable")
221	    except Exception:
222	        pass
223	    return False
224	
225	
226	def _wayland_save(dest: Path) -> bool:
227	    """Use wl-paste to extract clipboard image (Wayland sessions)."""
228	    try:
229	        # Check available MIME types
230	        types_r = subprocess.run(
231	            ["wl-paste", "--list-types"],
232	            capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=3,
233	        )
234	        if types_r.returncode != 0:
235	            return False
236	        types = types_r.stdout.splitlines()
237	
238	        # Prefer PNG, fall back to other image formats
239	        mime = None
240	        for preferred in ("image/png", "image/jpeg", "image/bmp",
241	                          "image/gif", "image/webp"):
242	            if preferred in types:
243	                mime = preferred
244	                break
245	
246	        if not mime:
247	            return False
248	
249	        # Extract the image data
250	        with open(dest, "wb") as f:
251	            subprocess.run(
252	                ["wl-paste", "--type", mime],
253	                stdout=f, stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL, timeout=5, check=True,
254	            )
255	
256	        if not dest.exists() or dest.stat().st_size == 0:
257	            dest.unlink(missing_ok=True)
258	            return False
259	
260	        # BMP needs conversion to PNG (common in WSLg where only BMP
261	        # is bridged from Windows clipboard via RDP).
262	        if mime == "image/bmp":
263	            return _convert_to_png(dest)
264	
265	        return True
266	
267	    except FileNotFoundError:
268	        logger.debug("wl-paste not installed — Wayland clipboard unavailable")
269	    except Exception as e:
270	        logger.debug("wl-paste clipboard extraction failed: %s", e)
271	        dest.unlink(missing_ok=True)
272	    return False
273	
274	
275	def _convert_to_png(path: Path) -> bool:
276	    """Convert an image file to PNG in-place (requires Pillow or ImageMagick)."""
277	    # Try Pillow first (likely installed in the venv)
278	    try:
279	        from PIL import Image
280	        img = Image.open(path)
281	        img.save(path, "PNG")
282	        return True
283	    except ImportError:
284	        pass
285	    except Exception as e:
286	        logger.debug("Pillow BMP→PNG conversion failed: %s", e)
287	
288	    # Fall back to ImageMagick convert
289	    tmp = path.with_suffix(".bmp")
290	    try:
291	        path.rename(tmp)
292	        r = subprocess.run(
293	            ["convert", str(tmp), "png:" + str(path)],
294	            capture_output=True, timeout=5,
295	        )
296	        if r.returncode == 0 and path.exists() and path.stat().st_size > 0:
297	            tmp.unlink(missing_ok=True)
298	            return True
299	        else:
300	            # Convert failed — restore the original file
301	            tmp.rename(path)
302	    except FileNotFoundError:
303	        logger.debug("ImageMagick not installed — cannot convert BMP to PNG")
304	        if tmp.exists() and not path.exists():
305	            tmp.rename(path)
306	    except Exception as e:
307	        logger.debug("ImageMagick BMP→PNG conversion failed: %s", e)
308	        if tmp.exists() and not path.exists():
309	            tmp.rename(path)
310	
311	    # Can't convert — BMP is still usable as-is for most APIs
312	    return path.exists() and path.stat().st_size > 0
313	
314	
315	# ── X11 (xclip) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
316	
317	def _xclip_has_image() -> bool:
318	    """Check if X11 clipboard has image content."""
319	    try:
320	        r = subprocess.run(
321	            ["xclip", "-selection", "clipboard", "-t", "TARGETS", "-o"],
322	            capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=3,
323	        )
324	        return r.returncode == 0 and "image/png" in r.stdout
325	    except FileNotFoundError:
326	        pass
327	    except Exception:
328	        pass
329	    return False
330	
331	
332	def _xclip_save(dest: Path) -> bool:
333	    """Use xclip to extract clipboard image (X11 sessions)."""
334	    # Check if clipboard has image content
335	    try:
336	        targets = subprocess.run(
337	            ["xclip", "-selection", "clipboard", "-t", "TARGETS", "-o"],
338	            capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=3,
339	        )
340	        if "image/png" not in targets.stdout:
341	            return False
342	    except FileNotFoundError:
343	        logger.debug("xclip not installed — X11 clipboard image paste unavailable")
344	        return False
345	    except Exception:
346	        return False
347	
348	    # Extract PNG data
349	    try:
350	        with open(dest, "wb") as f:
351	            subprocess.run(
352	                ["xclip", "-selection", "clipboard", "-t", "image/png", "-o"],
353	                stdout=f, stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL, timeout=5, check=True,
354	            )
355	        if dest.exists() and dest.stat().st_size > 0:
356	            return True
357	    except Exception as e:
358	        logger.debug("xclip image extraction failed: %s", e)
359	        dest.unlink(missing_ok=True)
360	    return False
361
```
</details></tool-use>

 `/c/Users/downl/Desktop/hermes-agent-main/hermes-agent-main/setup-hermes.sh`

```text
1	#!/bin/bash
2	# ============================================================================
3	# Hermes Agent Setup Script
4	# ============================================================================
5	# Quick setup for developers who cloned the repo manually.
6	# Uses uv for fast Python provisioning and package management.
7	#
8	# Usage:
9	#   ./setup-hermes.sh
10	#
11	# This script:
12	# 1. Installs uv if not present
13	# 2. Creates a virtual environment with Python 3.11 via uv
14	# 3. Installs all dependencies (main package + submodules)
15	# 4. Creates .env from template (if not exists)
16	# 5. Symlinks the 'hermes' CLI command into ~/.local/bin
17	# 6. Runs the setup wizard (optional)
18	# ============================================================================
19	
20	set -e
21	
22	# Colors
23	GREEN='\033[0;32m'
24	YELLOW='\033[0;33m'
25	CYAN='\033[0;36m'
26	RED='\033[0;31m'
27	NC='\033[0m'
28	
29	SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
30	cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"
31	
32	PYTHON_VERSION="3.11"
33	
34	echo ""
35	echo -e "${CYAN}⚕ Hermes Agent Setup${NC}"
36	echo ""
37	
38	# ============================================================================
39	# Install / locate uv
40	# ============================================================================
41	
42	echo -e "${CYAN}→${NC} Checking for uv..."
43	
44	UV_CMD=""
45	if command -v uv &> /dev/null; then
46	    UV_CMD="uv"
47	elif [ -x "$HOME/.local/bin/uv" ]; then
48	    UV_CMD="$HOME/.local/bin/uv"
49	elif [ -x "$HOME/.cargo/bin/uv" ]; then
50	    UV_CMD="$HOME/.cargo/bin/uv"
51	fi
52	
53	if [ -n "$UV_CMD" ]; then
54	    UV_VERSION=$($UV_CMD --version 2>/dev/null)
55	    echo -e "${GREEN}✓${NC} uv found ($UV_VERSION)"
56	else
57	    echo -e "${CYAN}→${NC} Installing uv..."
58	    if curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh 2>/dev/null; then
59	        if [ -x "$HOME/.local/bin/uv" ]; then
60	            UV_CMD="$HOME/.local/bin/uv"
61	        elif [ -x "$HOME/.cargo/bin/uv" ]; then
62	            UV_CMD="$HOME/.cargo/bin/uv"
63	        fi
64	        
65	        if [ -n "$UV_CMD" ]; then
66	            UV_VERSION=$($UV_CMD --version 2>/dev/null)
67	            echo -e "${GREEN}✓${NC} uv installed ($UV_VERSION)"
68	        else
69	            echo -e "${RED}✗${NC} uv installed but not found. Add ~/.local/bin to PATH and retry."
70	            exit 1
71	        fi
72	    else
73	        echo -e "${RED}✗${NC} Failed to install uv. Visit https://docs.astral.sh/uv/"
74	        exit 1
75	    fi
76	fi
77	
78	# ============================================================================
79	# Python check (uv can provision it automatically)
80	# ============================================================================
81	
82	echo -e "${CYAN}→${NC} Checking Python $PYTHON_VERSION..."
83	
84	if $UV_CMD python find "$PYTHON_VERSION" &> /dev/null; then
85	    PYTHON_PATH=$($UV_CMD python find "$PYTHON_VERSION")
86	    PYTHON_FOUND_VERSION=$($PYTHON_PATH --version 2>/dev/null)
87	    echo -e "${GREEN}✓${NC} $PYTHON_FOUND_VERSION found"
88	else
89	    echo -e "${CYAN}→${NC} Python $PYTHON_VERSION not found, installing via uv..."
90	    $UV_CMD python install "$PYTHON_VERSION"
91	    PYTHON_PATH=$($UV_CMD python find "$PYTHON_VERSION")
92	    PYTHON_FOUND_VERSION=$($PYTHON_PATH --version 2>/dev/null)
93	    echo -e "${GREEN}✓${NC} $PYTHON_FOUND_VERSION installed"
94	fi
95	
96	# ============================================================================
97	# Virtual environment
98	# ============================================================================
99	
100	echo -e "${CYAN}→${NC} Setting up virtual environment..."
```
</details></tool-use>

 `/c/Users/downl/Desktop/hermes-agent-main/hermes-agent-main/scripts/install.sh`

```text
1	#!/bin/bash
2	# ============================================================================
3	# Hermes Agent Installer
4	# ============================================================================
5	# Installation script for Linux and macOS.
6	# Uses uv for fast Python provisioning and package management.
7	#
8	# Usage:
9	#   curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/main/scripts/install.sh | bash
10	#
11	# Or with options:
12	#   curl -fsSL ... | bash -s -- --no-venv --skip-setup
13	#
14	# ============================================================================
15	
16	set -e
17	
18	# Colors
19	RED='\033[0;31m'
20	GREEN='\033[0;32m'
21	YELLOW='\033[0;33m'
22	BLUE='\033[0;34m'
23	MAGENTA='\033[0;35m'
24	CYAN='\033[0;36m'
25	NC='\033[0m' # No Color
26	BOLD='\033[1m'
27	
28	# Configuration
29	REPO_URL_SSH="git@github.com:NousResearch/hermes-agent.git"
30	REPO_URL_HTTPS="https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent.git"
31	HERMES_HOME="$HOME/.hermes"
32	INSTALL_DIR="${HERMES_INSTALL_DIR:-$HERMES_HOME/hermes-agent}"
33	PYTHON_VERSION="3.11"
34	NODE_VERSION="22"
35	
36	# Options
37	USE_VENV=true
38	RUN_SETUP=true
39	BRANCH="main"
40	
41	# Detect non-interactive mode (e.g. curl | bash)
42	# When stdin is not a terminal, read -p will fail with EOF,
43	# causing set -e to silently abort the entire script.
44	if [ -t 0 ]; then
45	    IS_INTERACTIVE=true
46	else
47	    IS_INTERACTIVE=false
48	fi
49	
50	# Parse arguments
51	while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
52	    case $1 in
53	        --no-venv)
54	            USE_VENV=false
55	            shift
56	            ;;
57	        --skip-setup)
58	            RUN_SETUP=false
59	            shift
60	            ;;
61	        --branch)
62	            BRANCH="$2"
63	            shift 2
64	            ;;
65	        --dir)
66	            INSTALL_DIR="$2"
67	            shift 2
68	            ;;
69	        -h|--help)
70	            echo "Hermes Agent Installer"
71	            echo ""
72	            echo "Usage: install.sh [OPTIONS]"
73	            echo ""
74	            echo "Options:"
75	            echo "  --no-venv      Don't create virtual environment"
76	            echo "  --skip-setup   Skip interactive setup wizard"
77	            echo "  --branch NAME  Git branch to install (default: main)"
78	            echo "  --dir PATH     Installation directory (default: ~/.hermes/hermes-agent)"
79	            echo "  -h, --help     Show this help"
80	            exit 0
81	            ;;
82	        *)
83	            echo "Unknown option: $1"
84	            exit 1
85	            ;;
86	    esac
87	done
88	
89	# ============================================================================
90	# Helper functions
91	# ============================================================================
92	
93	print_banner() {
94	    echo ""
95	    echo -e "${MAGENTA}${BOLD}"
96	    echo "┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐"
97	    echo "│             ⚕ Hermes Agent Installer                    │"
98	    echo "├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤"
99	    echo "│  An open source AI agent by Nous Research.              │"
100	    echo "└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘"
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>

 `/c/Users/downl/Desktop/hermes-agent-main/hermes-agent-main/tools/terminal_tool.py`

```text
1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""
3	Terminal Tool Module
4	
5	A terminal tool that executes commands in local, Docker, Modal, SSH, Singularity, and Daytona environments.
6	Supports local execution, Docker containers, and Modal cloud sandboxes.
7	
8	Environment Selection (via TERMINAL_ENV environment variable):
9	- "local": Execute directly on the host machine (default, fastest)
10	- "docker": Execute in Docker containers (isolated, requires Docker)
11	- "modal": Execute in Modal cloud sandboxes (scalable, requires Modal account)
12	
13	Features:
14	- Multiple execution backends (local, docker, modal)
15	- Background task support
16	- VM/container lifecycle management
17	- Automatic cleanup after inactivity
18	
19	Usage:
20	    from terminal_tool import terminal_tool
21	
22	    # Execute a simple command
23	    result = terminal_tool("ls -la")
24	
25	    # Execute in background
26	    result = terminal_tool("python server.py", background=True)
27	"""
28	
29	import importlib.util
30	import json
31	import logging
32	import os
33	import platform
34	import time
35	import threading
36	import atexit
37	import shutil
38	import subprocess
39	from pathlib import Path
40	from typing import Optional, Dict, Any
41	
42	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
43	
44	
45	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
46	# Global interrupt event: set by the agent when a user interrupt arrives.
47	# The terminal tool polls this during command execution so it can kill
48	# long-running subprocesses immediately instead of blocking until timeout.
49	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
50	from tools.interrupt import is_interrupted, _interrupt_event  # noqa: F401 — re-exported
51	# display_hermes_home imported lazily at call site (stale-module safety during hermes update)
52	
53	
54	# =============================================================================
55	# Custom Singularity Environment with more space
56	# =============================================================================
57	
58	# Singularity helpers (scratch dir, SIF cache) now live in tools/environments/singularity.py
59	from tools.environments.singularity import _get_scratch_dir
60	
61	
62	# Disk usage warning threshold (in GB)
63	DISK_USAGE_WARNING_THRESHOLD_GB = float(os.getenv("TERMINAL_DISK_WARNING_GB", "500"))
64	
65	
66	def _check_disk_usage_warning():
67	    """Check if total disk usage exceeds warning threshold."""
68	    try:
69	        scratch_dir = _get_scratch_dir()
70	
71	        # Get total size of hermes directories
72	        total_bytes = 0
73	        import glob
74	        for path in glob.glob(str(scratch_dir / "hermes-*")):
75	            for f in Path(path).rglob('*'):
76	                if f.is_file():
77	                    try:
78	                        total_bytes += f.stat().st_size
79	                    except OSError as e:
80	                        logger.debug("Could not stat file %s: %s", f, e)
81	        
82	        total_gb = total_bytes / (1024 ** 3)
83	        
84	        if total_gb > DISK_USAGE_WARNING_THRESHOLD_GB:
85	            logger.warning("Disk usage (%.1fGB) exceeds threshold (%.0fGB). Consider running cleanup_all_environments().",
86	                           total_gb, DISK_USAGE_WARNING_THRESHOLD_GB)
87	            return True
88	        
89	        return False
90	    except Exception as e:
91	        logger.debug("Disk usage warning check failed: %s", e, exc_info=True)
92	        return False
93	
94	
95	# Session-cached sudo password (persists until CLI exits)
96	_cached_sudo_password: str = ""
97	
98	# Optional UI callbacks for interactive prompts. When set, these are called
99	# instead of the default /dev/tty or input() readers. The CLI registers these
100	# so prompts route through prompt_toolkit's event loop.
101	#   _sudo_password_callback() -> str  (return password or "" to skip)
102	#   _approval_callback(command, description) -> str  ("once"/"session"/"always"/"deny")
103	_sudo_password_callback = None
104	_approval_callback = None
105	
106	
107	def set_sudo_password_callback(cb):
108	    """Register a callback for sudo password prompts (used by CLI)."""
109	    global _sudo_password_callback
110	    _sudo_password_callback = cb
111	
112	
113	def set_approval_callback(cb):
114	    """Register a callback for dangerous command approval prompts (used by CLI)."""
115	    global _approval_callback
116	    _approval_callback = cb
117	
118	# =============================================================================
119	# Dangerous Command Approval System
120	# =============================================================================
121	
122	# Dangerous command detection + approval now consolidated in tools/approval.py
123	from tools.approval import (
124	    check_dangerous_command as _check_dangerous_command_impl,
125	    check_all_command_guards as _check_all_guards_impl,
126	)
127	
128	
129	def _check_dangerous_command(command: str, env_type: str) -> dict:
130	    """Delegate to the consolidated approval module, passing the CLI callback."""
131	    return _check_dangerous_command_impl(command, env_type,
132	                                         approval_callback=_approval_callback)
133	
134	
135	def _check_all_guards(command: str, env_type: str) -> dict:
136	    """Delegate to consolidated guard (tirith + dangerous cmd) with CLI callback."""
137	    return _check_all_guards_impl(command, env_type,
138	                                  approval_callback=_approval_callback)
139	
140	
141	def _handle_sudo_failure(output: str, env_type: str) -> str:
142	    """
143	    Check for sudo failure and add helpful message for messaging contexts.
144	    
145	    Returns enhanced output if sudo failed in messaging context, else original.
146	    """
147	    is_gateway = os.getenv("HERMES_GATEWAY_SESSION")
148	    
149	    if not is_gateway:
150	        return output
```

```text
1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""
3	Terminal Tool Module
4	
5	A terminal tool that executes commands in local, Docker, Modal, SSH, Singularity, and Daytona environments.
6	Supports local execution, Docker containers, and Modal cloud sandboxes.
7	
8	Environment Selection (via TERMINAL_ENV environment variable):
9	- "local": Execute directly on the host machine (default, fastest)
10	- "docker": Execute in Docker containers (isolated, requires Docker)
11	- "modal": Execute in Modal cloud sandboxes (scalable, requires Modal account)
12	
13	Features:
14	- Multiple execution backends (local, docker, modal)
15	- Background task support
16	- VM/container lifecycle management
17	- Automatic cleanup after inactivity
18	
19	Usage:
20	    from terminal_tool import terminal_tool
21	
22	    # Execute a simple command
23	    result = terminal_tool("ls -la")
24	
25	    # Execute in background
26	    result = terminal_tool("python server.py", background=True)
27	"""
28	
29	import importlib.util
30	import json
31	import logging
32	import os
33	import platform
34	import time
35	import threading
36	import atexit
37	import shutil
38	import subprocess
39	from pathlib import Path
40	from typing import Optional, Dict, Any
41	
42	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
43	
44	
45	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
46	# Global interrupt event: set by the agent when a user interrupt arrives.
47	# The terminal tool polls this during command execution so it can kill
48	# long-running subprocesses immediately instead of blocking until timeout.
49	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
50	from tools.interrupt import is_interrupted, _interrupt_event  # noqa: F401 — re-exported
51	# display_hermes_home imported lazily at call site (stale-module safety during hermes update)
52	
53	
54	# =============================================================================
55	# Custom Singularity Environment with more space
56	# =============================================================================
57	
58	# Singularity helpers (scratch dir, SIF cache) now live in tools/environments/singularity.py
59	from tools.environments.singularity import _get_scratch_dir
60	
61	
62	# Disk usage warning threshold (in GB)
63	DISK_USAGE_WARNING_THRESHOLD_GB = float(os.getenv("TERMINAL_DISK_WARNING_GB", "500"))
64	
65	
66	def _check_disk_usage_warning():
67	    """Check if total disk usage exceeds warning threshold."""
68	    try:
69	        scratch_dir = _get_scratch_dir()
70	
71	        # Get total size of hermes directories
72	        total_bytes = 0
73	        import glob
74	        for path in glob.glob(str(scratch_dir / "hermes-*")):
75	            for f in Path(path).rglob('*'):
76	                if f.is_file():
77	                    try:
78	                        total_bytes += f.stat().st_size
79	                    except OSError as e:
80	                        logger.debug("Could not stat file %s: %s", f, e)
81	        
82	        total_gb = total_bytes / (1024 ** 3)
83	        
84	        if total_gb > DISK_USAGE_WARNING_THRESHOLD_GB:
85	            logger.warning("Disk usage (%.1fGB) exceeds threshold (%.0fGB). Consider running cleanup_all_environments().",
86	                           total_gb, DISK_USAGE_WARNING_THRESHOLD_GB)
87	            return True
88	        
89	        return False
90	    except Exception as e:
91	        logger.debug("Disk usage warning check failed: %s", e, exc_info=True)
92	        return False
93	
94	
95	# Session-cached sudo password (persists until CLI exits)
96	_cached_sudo_password: str = ""
97	
98	# Optional UI callbacks for interactive prompts. When set, these are called
99	# instead of the default /dev/tty or input() readers. The CLI registers these
100	# so prompts route through prompt_toolkit's event loop.
```

```text
685	def write_tty(text: str) -> None:
686	    """Write directly to /dev/tty, bypassing stdout capture."""
687	    try:
688	        fd = os.open("/dev/tty", os.O_WRONLY)
689	        os.write(fd, text.encode("utf-8"))
690	        os.close(fd)
691	    except OSError:
692	        sys.stdout.write(text)
693	        sys.stdout.flush()

696	# =========================================================================

698	# =========================================================================
699	
700	# ANSI color codes for context pressure tiers
701	_CYAN = "\033[36m"
702	_YELLOW = "\033[33m"
703	_BOLD = "\033[1m"
704	_DIM_ANSI = "\033[2m"
705	
706	# Bar characters

708	_BAR_EMPTY = "▱"
709	_BAR_WIDTH = 20

711	
712	def format_context_pressure(
713	    compaction_progress: float,
714	    threshold_tokens: int,
715	    threshold_percent: float,
716	    compression_enabled: bool = True,
717	) -> str:
718	    """Build a formatted context pressure line for CLI display.
719	
720	    The bar and percentage show progress toward the compaction threshold,
721	    NOT the raw context window.  100% = compaction fires.
722	
723	    Args:
724	        compaction_progress: How close to compaction (0.0–1.0, 1.0 = fires).
725	        threshold_tokens: Compaction threshold in tokens.
726	        threshold_percent: Compaction threshold as a fraction of context window.
727	        compression_enabled: Whether auto-compression is active.
728	    """
729	    pct_int = min(int(compaction_progress * 100), 100)
```

```text
190	
191	    result = {"password": None, "done": False}
192	    
193	    def read_password_thread():
194	        """Read password with echo disabled. Uses msvcrt on Windows, /dev/tty on Unix."""
195	        tty_fd = None
196	        old_attrs = None
197	        try:
198	            if platform.system() == "Windows":
199	                import msvcrt
200	                chars = []
201	                while True:
202	                    c = msvcrt.getwch()
203	                    if c in ("\r", "\n"):
204	                        break
205	                    if c == "\x03":
206	                        raise KeyboardInterrupt
207	                    chars.append(c)
208	                result["password"] = "".join(chars)
209	            else:
210	                import termios
211	                tty_fd = os.open("/dev/tty", os.O_RDONLY)
212	                old_attrs = termios.tcgetattr(tty_fd)
213	                new_attrs = termios.tcgetattr(tty_fd)
214	                new_attrs[3] = new_attrs[3] & ~termios.ECHO
215	                termios.tcsetattr(tty_fd, termios.TCSAFLUSH, new_attrs)
216	                chars = []
217	                while True:
218	                    b = os.read(tty_fd, 1)
219	                    if not b or b in (b"\n", b"\r"):
220	                        break
221	                    chars.append(b)
222	                result["password"] = b"".join(chars).decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
223	        except (EOFError, KeyboardInterrupt, OSError):
224	            result["password"] = ""
225	        except Exception:
226	            result["password"] = ""
227	        finally:
228	            if tty_fd is not None and old_attrs is not None:
229	                try:
230	                    import termios as _termios
231	                    _termios.tcsetattr(tty_fd, _termios.TCSAFLUSH, old_attrs)
232	                except Exception as e:
233	                    logger.debug("Failed to restore terminal attributes: %s", e)
234	            if tty_fd is not None:
235	                try:
236	                    os.close(tty_fd)
237	                except Exception as e:
238	                    logger.debug("Failed to close tty fd: %s", e)
239	            result["done"] = True
240	    
241	    try:
242	        os.environ["HERMES_SPINNER_PAUSE"] = "1"
243	        time_module.sleep(0.2)
244	        
245	        print()
246	        print("┌" + "─" * 58 + "┐")
247	        print("│  🔐 SUDO PASSWORD REQUIRED" + " " * 30 + "│")
248	        print("├" + "─" * 58 + "┤")
249	        print("│  Enter password below (input is hidden), or:            │")
250	        print("│    • Press Enter to skip (command fails gracefully)     │")
251	        print(f"│    • Wait {timeout_seconds}s to auto-skip" + " " * 27 + "│")
252	        print("└" + "─" * 58 + "┘")
253	        print()
254	        print("  Password (hidden): ", end="", flush=True)
255	        
256	        password_thread = threading.Thread(target=read_password_thread, daemon=True)
257	        password_thread.start()
258	        password_thread.join(timeout=timeout_seconds)
259	        
260	        if result["done"]:
261	            password = result["password"] or ""
262	            print()  # newline after hidden input
263	            if password:
264	                print("  ✓ Password received (cached for this session)")
265	            else:
266	                print("  ⏭ Skipped - continuing without sudo")
267	            print()
268	            sys.stdout.flush()
269	            return password
270	        else:
271	            print("\n  ⏱ Timeout - continuing without sudo")
272	            print("    (Press Enter to dismiss)")
273	            print()
274	            sys.stdout.flush()
275	            return ""
276	            
277	    except (EOFError, KeyboardInterrupt):
278	        print()
279	        print("  ⏭ Cancelled - continuing without sudo")
280	        print()
281	        sys.stdout.flush()
282	        return ""
283	    except Exception as e:
284	        print(f"\n  [sudo prompt error: {e}] - continuing without sudo\n")
285	        sys.stdout.flush()
286	        return ""
287	    finally:
288	        if "HERMES_SPINNER_PAUSE" in os.environ:
289	            del os.environ["HERMES_SPINNER_PAUSE"]
```

```text
60	def _get_process_start_time(pid: int) -> Optional[int]:
61	    """Return the kernel start time for a process when available."""
62	    stat_path = Path(f"/proc/{pid}/stat")
63	    try:
64	        # Field 22 in /proc/<pid>/stat is process start time (clock ticks).
65	        return int(stat_path.read_text().split()[21])
66	    except (FileNotFoundError, IndexError, PermissionError, ValueError, OSError):
67	        return None
68	
69	
70	def _read_process_cmdline(pid: int) -> Optional[str]:
71	    """Return the process command line as a space-separated string."""
72	    cmdline_path = Path(f"/proc/{pid}/cmdline")
73	    try:
74	        raw = cmdline_path.read_bytes()
75	    except (FileNotFoundError, PermissionError, OSError):
76	        return None
77	
78	    if not raw:
79	        return None
80	    return raw.replace(b"\x00", b" ").decode("utf-8", errors="ignore").strip()
81	
82	
83	def _looks_like_gateway_process(pid: int) -> bool:
84	    """Return True when the live PID still looks like the Hermes gateway."""
85	    cmdline = _read_process_cmdline(pid)
86	    if not cmdline:
87	        return False
88	
89	    patterns = (
90	        "hermes_cli.main gateway",
91	        "hermes_cli/main.py gateway",
92	        "hermes gateway",
93	        "gateway/run.py",
94	    )
95	    return any(pattern in cmdline for pattern in patterns)
96	
97	
98	def _record_looks_like_gateway(record: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
99	    """Validate gateway identity from PID-file metadata when cmdline is unavailable."""
100	    if record.get("kind") != _GATEWAY_KIND:
101	        return False
102	
103	    argv = record.get("argv")
104	    if not isinstance(argv, list) or not argv:
105	        return False
106	
107	    cmdline = " ".join(str(part) for part in argv)
108	    patterns = (
109	        "hermes_cli.main gateway",
110	        "hermes_cli/main.py gateway",
111	        "hermes gateway",
112	        "gateway/run.py",
113	    )
114	    return any(pattern in cmdline for pattern in patterns)
115	
116	
117	def _build_pid_record() -> dict:
118	    return {
119	        "pid": os.getpid(),
120	        "kind": _GATEWAY_KIND,
121	        "argv": list(sys.argv),
122	        "start_time": _get_process_start_time(os.getpid()),
123	    }
124	
125	
126	def _build_runtime_status_record() -> dict[str, Any]:
127	    payload = _build_pid_record()
128	    payload.update({
129	        "gateway_state": "starting",
130	        "exit_reason": None,
131	        "platforms": {},
132	        "updated_at": _utc_now_iso(),
133	    })
134	    return payload
135	
136	
137	def _read_json_file(path: Path) -> Optional[dict[str, Any]]:
138	    if not path.exists():
139	        return None
```

```text
1	"""
2	ToolContext -- Unrestricted Tool Access for Reward Functions
3	
4	A per-rollout handle that gives reward/verification functions direct access to
5	ALL hermes-agent tools, scoped to the rollout's task_id. The same task_id means
6	the terminal/browser session is the SAME one the model used during its rollout --
7	all state (files, processes, browser tabs) is preserved.
8	
9	The verifier author decides which tools to use. Nothing is hardcoded or gated.
10	
11	Example usage in a compute_reward():
12	    async def compute_reward(self, item, result, ctx):
13	        # Run tests in the model's terminal sandbox
14	        test = ctx.terminal("pytest -v")
15	        if test["exit_code"] == 0:
16	            return 1.0
17	
18	        # Check if a file was created
19	        content = ctx.read_file("/workspace/solution.py")
20	        if content.get("content"):
21	            return 0.5
22	
23	        return 0.0
24	"""
25	
26	import json
27	import logging
28	import os
29	from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
30	
31	import asyncio
32	import concurrent.futures
33	
34	from model_tools import handle_function_call
35	from tools.terminal_tool import cleanup_vm
36	from tools.browser_tool import cleanup_browser
37	
38	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
39	
40	# Thread pool for running sync tool calls that internally use asyncio.run()
41	_tool_executor = concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=4)
42	
43	
44	def _run_tool_in_thread(tool_name: str, arguments: Dict[str, Any], task_id: str) -> str:
45	    """
46	    Run a tool call in a thread pool executor so backends that use asyncio.run()
47	    internally (modal, docker, daytona) get a clean event loop.
48	
49	    If we're already in an async context, executes handle_function_call() in a
50	    disposable worker thread and blocks for the result.
51	    If not (e.g., called from sync code), runs directly.
52	    """
53	    try:
54	        loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
55	        # We're in an async context -- need to run in thread
56	        import concurrent.futures
57	        with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1) as pool:
58	            future = pool.submit(
59	                handle_function_call, tool_name, arguments, task_id
60	            )
61	            return future.result(timeout=300)
62	    except RuntimeError:
63	        # No running event loop -- safe to call directly
64	        return handle_function_call(tool_name, arguments, task_id)
65	
66	
67	class ToolContext:
68	    """
69	    Open-ended access to all hermes-agent tools for a specific rollout.
70	
71	    Passed to compute_reward() so verifiers can use any tool they need:
72	    terminal commands, file reads/writes, web searches, browser automation, etc.
73	    All calls share the rollout's task_id for session isolation.
74	    """
75	
76	    def __init__(self, task_id: str):
77	        self.task_id = task_id
78	
79	    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
80	    # Terminal tools
81	    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
82	
83	    def terminal(self, command: str, timeout: int = 180) -> Dict[str, Any]:
84	        """
85	        Run a command in the rollout's terminal session.
86	
87	        Args:
88	            command: Shell command to execute
89	            timeout: Command timeout in seconds
90	
91	        Returns:
92	            Dict with 'exit_code' (int) and 'output' (str)
93	        """
94	        import os
95	        backend = os.getenv("TERMINAL_ENV", "local")
96	        logger.debug("ToolContext.terminal [%s backend] task=%s: %s", backend, self.task_id[:8], command[:100])
97	
98	        # Run via thread helper so modal/docker/daytona backends' asyncio.run() doesn't deadlock
99	        result = _run_tool_in_thread(
100	            "terminal",
101	            {"command": command, "timeout": timeout},
102	            self.task_id,
103	        )
104	        try:
105	            return json.loads(result)
106	        except json.JSONDecodeError:
107	            return {"exit_code": -1, "output": result}
108	
109	    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
110	    # File tools
111	    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
112	
113	    def read_file(self, path: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
114	        """
115	        Read a file from the rollout's filesystem.
116	
117	        Args:
118	            path: File path to read
119	
120	        Returns:
121	            Dict with file content or error
122	        """
123	        result = handle_function_call(
124	            "read_file", {"path": path}, task_id=self.task_id
125	        )
126	        try:
127	            return json.loads(result)
128	        except json.JSONDecodeError:
129	            return {"error": result}
130	
131	    def write_file(self, path: str, content: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
132	        """
133	        Write a TEXT file in the rollout's filesystem.
134	
135	        Uses a shell heredoc under the hood, so this is only safe for text content.
136	        For binary files (images, compiled artifacts, etc.), use upload_file() instead.
137	
138	        Args:
139	            path: File path to write
140	            content: Text content to write
141	
142	        Returns:
143	            Dict with success status or error
144	        """
145	        result = handle_function_call(
146	            "write_file", {"path": path, "content": content}, task_id=self.task_id
147	        )
148	        try:
149	            return json.loads(result)
150	        except json.JSONDecodeError:
151	            return {"error": result}
152	
153	    def upload_file(self, local_path: str, remote_path: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
154	        """
155	        Upload a local file to the rollout's sandbox (binary-safe).
156	
157	        Unlike write_file() which passes content through a shell heredoc (text-only),
158	        this method base64-encodes the file and decodes it inside the sandbox.
159	        Safe for any file type: binaries, images, archives, etc.
160	
161	        For large files (>1MB), the content is split into chunks to avoid
162	        hitting shell command-length limits.
163	
164	        Args:
165	            local_path: Path to a local file on the host
166	            remote_path: Destination path inside the sandbox
167	
168	        Returns:
169	            Dict with 'exit_code' and 'output'
170	        """
171	        import base64
172	        from pathlib import Path as _Path
173	
174	        local = _Path(local_path)
175	        if not local.exists():
176	            return {"exit_code": -1, "output": f"Local file not found: {local_path}"}
177	
178	        raw = local.read_bytes()
179	        b64 = base64.b64encode(raw).decode("ascii")
180	
181	        # Ensure parent directory exists in the sandbox
182	        parent = str(_Path(remote_path).parent)
183	        if parent not in (".", "/"):
184	            self.terminal(f"mkdir -p {parent}", timeout=10)
185	
186	        # For small files, single command is fine
187	        chunk_size = 60_000  # ~60KB per chunk (well within shell limits)
188	        if len(b64) <= chunk_size:
189	            result = self.terminal(
190	                f"printf '%s' '{b64}' | base64 -d > {remote_path}",
191	                timeout=30,
192	            )
193	        else:
194	            # For larger files, write base64 in chunks then decode
195	            tmp_b64 = "/tmp/_hermes_upload.b64"
196	            self.terminal(f": > {tmp_b64}", timeout=5)  # truncate
197	            for i in range(0, len(b64), chunk_size):
198	                chunk = b64[i : i + chunk_size]
199	                self.terminal(f"printf '%s' '{chunk}' >> {tmp_b64}", timeout=15)
200	            result = self.terminal(
201	                f"base64 -d {tmp_b64} > {remote_path} && rm -f {tmp_b64}",
202	                timeout=30,
203	            )
204	
205	        return result
206	
207	    def upload_dir(self, local_dir: str, remote_dir: str) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
208	        """
209	        Upload an entire local directory to the rollout's sandbox (binary-safe).
210	
211	        Recursively uploads all files, preserving directory structure.
212	
213	        Args:
214	            local_dir: Path to a local directory on the host
215	            remote_dir: Destination directory inside the sandbox
216	
217	        Returns:
218	            List of results, one per file uploaded
219	        """
220	        from pathlib import Path as _Path
221	
222	        local = _Path(local_dir)
223	        if not local.exists() or not local.is_dir():
224	            return [{"exit_code": -1, "output": f"Local directory not found: {local_dir}"}]
225	
226	        results = []
227	        for file_path in sorted(local.rglob("*")):
228	            if file_path.is_file():
229	                relative = file_path.relative_to(local)
230	                target = f"{remote_dir}/{relative}"
231	                results.append(self.upload_file(str(file_path), target))
232	        return results
233	
234	    def download_file(self, remote_path: str, local_path: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
235	        """
236	        Download a file from the rollout's sandbox to the host (binary-safe).
237	
238	        The inverse of upload_file(). Base64-encodes the file inside the sandbox,
239	        reads the encoded data through the terminal, and decodes it locally.
240	        Safe for any file type.
241	
242	        Args:
243	            remote_path: Path to the file inside the sandbox
244	            local_path: Destination path on the host
245	
246	        Returns:
247	            Dict with 'success' (bool) and 'bytes' (int) or 'error' (str)
248	        """
249	        import base64
250	        from pathlib import Path as _Path
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
54	def _resolve_origin(job: dict) -> Optional[dict]:
55	    """Extract origin info from a job, preserving any extra routing metadata."""
56	    origin = job.get("origin")
57	    if not origin:
58	        return None
59	    platform = origin.get("platform")
60	    chat_id = origin.get("chat_id")
61	    if platform and chat_id:
62	        return origin
63	    return None
```

```text
1	#!/bin/bash
2	# ============================================================================
3	# Hermes Agent Installer
4	# ============================================================================
5	# Installation script for Linux and macOS.
6	# Uses uv for fast Python provisioning and package management.
7	#
8	# Usage:
9	#   curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/main/scripts/install.sh | bash
10	#
11	# Or with options:
12	#   curl -fsSL ... | bash -s -- --no-venv --skip-setup
13	#
14	# ============================================================================
15	
16	set -e
17	
18	# Colors
19	RED='\033[0;31m'
20	GREEN='\033[0;32m'
21	YELLOW='\033[0;33m'
22	BLUE='\033[0;34m'
23	MAGENTA='\033[0;35m'
24	CYAN='\033[0;36m'
25	NC='\033[0m' # No Color
26	BOLD='\033[1m'
27	
28	# Configuration
29	REPO_URL_SSH="git@github.com:NousResearch/hermes-agent.git"
30	REPO_URL_HTTPS="https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent.git"
31	HERMES_HOME="$HOME/.hermes"
32	INSTALL_DIR="${HERMES_INSTALL_DIR:-$HERMES_HOME/hermes-agent}"
33	PYTHON_VERSION="3.11"
34	NODE_VERSION="22"
35	
36	# Options
37	USE_VENV=true
38	RUN_SETUP=true
39	BRANCH="main"
40	
41	# Detect non-interactive mode (e.g. curl | bash)
42	# When stdin is not a terminal, read -p will fail with EOF,
43	# causing set -e to silently abort the entire script.
44	if [ -t 0 ]; then
45	    IS_INTERACTIVE=true
46	else
47	    IS_INTERACTIVE=false
48	fi
49	
50	# Parse arguments
51	while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
52	    case $1 in
53	        --no-venv)
54	            USE_VENV=false
55	            shift
56	            ;;
57	        --skip-setup)
58	            RUN_SETUP=false
59	            shift
60	            ;;
61	        --branch)
62	            BRANCH="$2"
63	            shift 2
64	            ;;
65	        --dir)
66	            INSTALL_DIR="$2"
67	            shift 2
68	            ;;
69	        -h|--help)
70	            echo "Hermes Agent Installer"
71	            echo ""
72	            echo "Usage: install.sh [OPTIONS]"
73	            echo ""
74	            echo "Options:"
75	            echo "  --no-venv      Don't create virtual environment"
76	            echo "  --skip-setup   Skip interactive setup wizard"
77	            echo "  --branch NAME  Git branch to install (default: main)"
78	            echo "  --dir PATH     Installation directory (default: ~/.hermes/hermes-agent)"
79	            echo "  -h, --help     Show this help"
80	            exit 0
81	            ;;
82	        *)
83	            echo "Unknown option: $1"
84	            exit 1
85	            ;;
86	    esac
87	done
88	
89	# ============================================================================
90	# Helper functions
91	# ============================================================================
92	
93	print_banner() {
94	    echo ""
95	    echo -e "${MAGENTA}${BOLD}"
96	    echo "┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐"
97	    echo "│             ⚕ Hermes Agent Installer                    │"
98	    echo "├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤"
99	    echo "│  An open source AI agent by Nous Research.              │"
100	    echo "└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘"
101	    echo -e "${NC}"
102	}
103	
104	log_info() {
105	    echo -e "${CYAN}→${NC} $1"
106	}
107	
108	log_success() {
109	    echo -e "${GREEN}✓${NC} $1"
110	}
111	
112	log_warn() {
113	    echo -e "${YELLOW}⚠${NC} $1"
114	}
115	
116	log_error() {
117	    echo -e "${RED}✗${NC} $1"
118	}
119	
120	# ============================================================================
121	# System detection
122	# ============================================================================
123	
124	detect_os() {
125	    case "$(uname -s)" in
126	        Linux*)
127	            OS="linux"
128	            if [ -f /etc/os-release ]; then
129	                . /etc/os-release
130	                DISTRO="$ID"
131	            else
132	                DISTRO="unknown"
133	            fi
134	            ;;
135	        Darwin*)
136	            OS="macos"
137	            DISTRO="macos"
138	            ;;
139	        CYGWIN*|MINGW*|MSYS*)
140	            OS="windows"
141	            DISTRO="windows"
142	            log_error "Windows detected. Please use the PowerShell installer:"
143	            log_info "  irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/main/scripts/install.ps1 | iex"
144	            exit 1
145	            ;;
146	        *)
147	            OS="unknown"
148	            DISTRO="unknown"
149	            log_warn "Unknown operating system"
150	            ;;
151	    esac
152	
153	    log_success "Detected: $OS ($DISTRO)"
154	}
155	
156	# ============================================================================
157	# Dependency checks
158	# ============================================================================
159	
160	install_uv() {
161	    log_info "Checking for uv package manager..."
162	
163	    # Check common locations for uv
164	    if command -v uv &> /dev/null; then
165	        UV_CMD="uv"
166	        UV_VERSION=$($UV_CMD --version 2>/dev/null)
167	        log_success "uv found ($UV_VERSION)"
168	        return 0
169	    fi
170	
171	    # Check ~/.local/bin (default uv install location) even if not on PATH yet
172	    if [ -x "$HOME/.local/bin/uv" ]; then
173	        UV_CMD="$HOME/.local/bin/uv"
174	        UV_VERSION=$($UV_CMD --version 2>/dev/null)
175	        log_success "uv found at ~/.local/bin ($UV_VERSION)"
176	        return 0
177	    fi
178	
179	    # Check ~/.cargo/bin (alternative uv install location)
180	    if [ -x "$HOME/.cargo/bin/uv" ]; then
181	        UV_CMD="$HOME/.cargo/bin/uv"
182	        UV_VERSION=$($UV_CMD --version 2>/dev/null)
183	        log_success "uv found at ~/.cargo/bin ($UV_VERSION)"
184	        return 0
185	    fi
186	
187	    # Install uv
188	    log_info "Installing uv (fast Python package manager)..."
189	    if curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh 2>/dev/null; then
190	        # uv installs to ~/.local/bin by default
191	        if [ -x "$HOME/.local/bin/uv" ]; then
192	            UV_CMD="$HOME/.local/bin/uv"
193	        elif [ -x "$HOME/.cargo/bin/uv" ]; then
194	            UV_CMD="$HOME/.cargo/bin/uv"
195	        elif command -v uv &> /dev/null; then
196	            UV_CMD="uv"
197	        else
198	            log_error "uv installed but not found on PATH"
199	            log_info "Try adding ~/.local/bin to your PATH and re-running"
200	            exit 1
```

```text
3	Verifies that os.setsid and os.killpg are never called unconditionally,
4	and that each module uses a platform guard before invoking POSIX-only functions.

11	# Files that must have Windows-safe process management
12	GUARDED_FILES = [
13	    "tools/environments/local.py",
14	    "tools/process_registry.py",
15	    "tools/code_execution_tool.py",
16	    "gateway/platforms/whatsapp.py",
17	]
18	
19	PROJECT_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent
20	
21	
22	def _get_preexec_fn_values(filepath: Path) -> list:
23	    """Find all preexec_fn= keyword arguments in Popen calls."""
24	    source = filepath.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
25	    tree = ast.parse(source, filename=str(filepath))
26	    values = []
27	    for node in ast.walk(tree):
28	        if isinstance(node, ast.keyword) and node.arg == "preexec_fn":
29	            values.append(ast.dump(node.value))
30	    return values
31	
32	
33	class TestNoUnconditionalSetsid:
34	    """preexec_fn must never be a bare os.setsid reference."""
35	
36	    @pytest.mark.parametrize("relpath", GUARDED_FILES)
37	    def test_preexec_fn_is_guarded(self, relpath):
38	        filepath = PROJECT_ROOT / relpath
39	        if not filepath.exists():
40	            pytest.skip(f"{relpath} not found")
41	        values = _get_preexec_fn_values(filepath)
42	        for val in values:
43	            # A bare os.setsid would be: Attribute(value=Name(id='os'), attr='setsid')
44	            assert "attr='setsid'" not in val or "IfExp" in val or "None" in val, (
45	                f"{relpath} has unconditional preexec_fn=os.setsid"
46	            )
47	
48	
49	class TestIsWindowsConstant:
50	    """Each guarded file must define _IS_WINDOWS."""
51	
52	    @pytest.mark.parametrize("relpath", GUARDED_FILES)
53	    def test_has_is_windows(self, relpath):
54	        filepath = PROJECT_ROOT / relpath
55	        if not filepath.exists():
56	            pytest.skip(f"{relpath} not found")
57	        source = filepath.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
58	        assert "_IS_WINDOWS" in source, (
59	            f"{relpath} missing _IS_WINDOWS platform guard"
60	        )
61	
62	
63	class TestKillpgGuarded:
64	    """os.killpg must always be behind a platform check."""
65	
66	    @pytest.mark.parametrize("relpath", GUARDED_FILES)
67	    def test_no_unguarded_killpg(self, relpath):
68	        filepath = PROJECT_ROOT / relpath
69	        if not filepath.exists():
70	            pytest.skip(f"{relpath} not found")
71	        source = filepath.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
72	        lines = source.splitlines()
73	        for i, line in enumerate(lines):
74	            stripped = line.strip()
75	            if "os.killpg" in stripped or "os.getpgid" in stripped:
76	                # Check that there's an _IS_WINDOWS guard in the surrounding context
77	                context = "\n".join(lines[max(0, i - 15):i + 1])
78	                assert "_IS_WINDOWS" in context or "else:" in context, (
79	                    f"{relpath}:{i + 1} has unguarded os.killpg/os.getpgid call"
80	                )
81
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
4790	        return "❌ Command denied."
4791	
4792	    async def _handle_update_command(self, event: MessageEvent) -> str:
4793	        """Handle /update command — update Hermes Agent to the latest version.
4794	
4795	        Spawns ``hermes update`` in a detached session (via ``setsid``) so it
4796	        survives the gateway restart that ``hermes update`` may trigger. Marker
4797	        files are written so either the current gateway process or the next one
4798	        can notify the user when the update finishes.
4799	        """
4800	        import json
4801	        import shutil
4802	        import subprocess
4803	        from datetime import datetime
4804	        from hermes_cli.config import is_managed, format_managed_message
4805	
4806	        if is_managed():
4807	            return f"✗ {format_managed_message('update Hermes Agent')}"
4808	
4809	        project_root = Path(__file__).parent.parent.resolve()
4810	        git_dir = project_root / '.git'
4811	
4812	        if not git_dir.exists():
4813	            return "✗ Not a git repository — cannot update."
4814	
4815	        hermes_cmd = _resolve_hermes_bin()
4816	        if not hermes_cmd:
4817	            return (
4818	                "✗ Could not locate the `hermes` command. "
4819	                "Hermes is running, but the update command could not find the "
4820	                "executable on PATH or via the current Python interpreter. "
4821	                "Try running `hermes update` manually in your terminal."
4822	            )
4823	
4824	        pending_path = _hermes_home / ".update_pending.json"
4825	        output_path = _hermes_home / ".update_output.txt"
4826	        exit_code_path = _hermes_home / ".update_exit_code"
4827	        pending = {
4828	            "platform": event.source.platform.value,
4829	            "chat_id": event.source.chat_id,
4830	            "user_id": event.source.user_id,
4831	            "timestamp": datetime.now().isoformat(),
4832	        }
4833	        pending_path.write_text(json.dumps(pending))
4834	        exit_code_path.unlink(missing_ok=True)
4835	
4836	        # Spawn `hermes update` detached so it survives gateway restart.
4837	        # Use setsid for portable session detach (works under system services
4838	        # where systemd-run --user fails due to missing D-Bus session).
4839	        hermes_cmd_str = " ".join(shlex.quote(part) for part in hermes_cmd)
4840	        update_cmd = (
4841	            f"{hermes_cmd_str} update > {shlex.quote(str(output_path))} 2>&1; "
4842	            f"status=$?; printf '%s' \"$status\" > {shlex.quote(str(exit_code_path))}"
4843	        )
4844	        try:
4845	            setsid_bin = shutil.which("setsid")
4846	            if setsid_bin:
4847	                # Preferred: setsid creates a new session, fully detached
4848	                subprocess.Popen(
4849	                    [setsid_bin, "bash", "-c", update_cmd],
4850	                    stdout=subprocess.DEVNULL,
4851	                    stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL,
4852	                    start_new_session=True,
4853	                )
4854	            else:
4855	                # Fallback: start_new_session=True calls os.setsid() in child
4856	                subprocess.Popen(
4857	                    ["bash", "-c", update_cmd],
4858	                    stdout=subprocess.DEVNULL,
4859	                    stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL,
4860	                    start_new_session=True,
4861	                )
4862	        except Exception as e:
4863	            pending_path.unlink(missing_ok=True)
4864	            exit_code_path.unlink(missing_ok=True)
4865	            return f"✗ Failed to start update: {e}"
4866	
4867	        self._schedule_update_notification_watch()
4868	        return "⚕ Starting Hermes update… I'll notify you when it's done."
4869
```

```text
1	# ============================================================================
2	# Hermes Agent Installer for Windows
3	# ============================================================================
4	# Installation script for Windows (PowerShell).
5	# Uses uv for fast Python provisioning and package management.
6	#
7	# Usage:
8	#   irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/main/scripts/install.ps1 | iex
9	#
10	# Or download and run with options:
11	#   .\install.ps1 -NoVenv -SkipSetup
12	#
13	# ============================================================================
14	
15	param(
16	    [switch]$NoVenv,
17	    [switch]$SkipSetup,
18	    [string]$Branch = "main",
19	    [string]$HermesHome = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\hermes",
20	    [string]$InstallDir = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\hermes\hermes-agent"
21	)
22	
23	$ErrorActionPreference = "Stop"
24	
25	# ============================================================================
26	# Configuration
27	# ============================================================================
28	
29	$RepoUrlSsh = "git@github.com:NousResearch/hermes-agent.git"
30	$RepoUrlHttps = "https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent.git"
31	$PythonVersion = "3.11"
32	$NodeVersion = "22"
33	
34	# ============================================================================
35	# Helper functions
36	# ============================================================================
37	
38	function Write-Banner {
39	    Write-Host ""
40	    Write-Host "┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐" -ForegroundColor Magenta
41	    Write-Host "│             ⚕ Hermes Agent Installer                   │" -ForegroundColor Magenta
42	    Write-Host "├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤" -ForegroundColor Magenta
43	    Write-Host "│  An open source AI agent by Nous Research.              │" -ForegroundColor Magenta
44	    Write-Host "└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘" -ForegroundColor Magenta
45	    Write-Host ""
46	}
47	
48	function Write-Info {
49	    param([string]$Message)
50	    Write-Host "→ $Message" -ForegroundColor Cyan
51	}
52	
53	function Write-Success {
54	    param([string]$Message)
55	    Write-Host "✓ $Message" -ForegroundColor Green
56	}
57	
58	function Write-Warn {
59	    param([string]$Message)
60	    Write-Host "⚠ $Message" -ForegroundColor Yellow
61	}
62	
63	function Write-Err {
64	    param([string]$Message)
65	    Write-Host "✗ $Message" -ForegroundColor Red
66	}
67	
68	# ============================================================================
69	# Dependency checks
70	# ============================================================================
71	
72	function Install-Uv {
73	    Write-Info "Checking for uv package manager..."
74	    
75	    # Check if uv is already available
76	    if (Get-Command uv -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
77	        $version = uv --version
78	        $script:UvCmd = "uv"
79	        Write-Success "uv found ($version)"
80	        return $true
81	    }
82	    
83	    # Check common install locations
84	    $uvPaths = @(
85	        "$env:USERPROFILE\.local\bin\uv.exe",
86	        "$env:USERPROFILE\.cargo\bin\uv.exe"
87	    )
88	    foreach ($uvPath in $uvPaths) {
89	        if (Test-Path $uvPath) {
90	            $script:UvCmd = $uvPath
91	            $version = & $uvPath --version
92	            Write-Success "uv found at $uvPath ($version)"
93	            return $true
94	        }
95	    }
96	    
97	    # Install uv
98	    Write-Info "Installing uv (fast Python package manager)..."
99	    try {
100	        powershell -ExecutionPolicy ByPass -c "irm https://astral.sh/uv/install.ps1 | iex" 2>&1 | Out-Null
101	        
102	        # Find the installed binary
103	        $uvExe = "$env:USERPROFILE\.local\bin\uv.exe"
104	        if (-not (Test-Path $uvExe)) {
105	            $uvExe = "$env:USERPROFILE\.cargo\bin\uv.exe"
106	        }
107	        if (-not (Test-Path $uvExe)) {
108	            # Refresh PATH and try again
109	            $env:Path = [Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("Path", "User") + ";" + [Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("Path", "Machine")
110	            if (Get-Command uv -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
111	                $uvExe = (Get-Command uv).Source
112	            }
113	        }
114	        
115	        if (Test-Path $uvExe) {
116	            $script:UvCmd = $uvExe
117	            $version = & $uvExe --version
118	            Write-Success "uv installed ($version)"
119	            return $true
120	        }
121	        
122	        Write-Err "uv installed but not found on PATH"
123	        Write-Info "Try restarting your terminal and re-running"
124	        return $false
125	    } catch {
126	        Write-Err "Failed to install uv"
127	        Write-Info "Install manually: https://docs.astral.sh/uv/getting-started/installation/"
128	        return $false
129	    }
130	}
131	
132	function Test-Python {
133	    Write-Info "Checking Python $PythonVersion..."
134	    
135	    # Let uv find or install Python
136	    try {
137	        $pythonPath = & $UvCmd python find $PythonVersion 2>$null
138	        if ($pythonPath) {
139	            $ver = & $pythonPath --version 2>$null
140	            Write-Success "Python found: $ver"
141	            return $true
142	        }
143	    } catch { }
144	    
145	    # Python not found — use uv to install it (no admin needed!)
146	    Write-Info "Python $PythonVersion not found, installing via uv..."
147	    try {
148	        $uvOutput = & $UvCmd python install $PythonVersion 2>&1
149	        if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) {
150	            $pythonPath = & $UvCmd python find $PythonVersion 2>$null
151	            if ($pythonPath) {
152	                $ver = & $pythonPath --version 2>$null
153	                Write-Success "Python installed: $ver"
154	                return $true
155	            }
156	        } else {
157	            Write-Warn "uv python install output:"
158	            Write-Host $uvOutput -ForegroundColor DarkGray
159	        }
160	    } catch {
161	        Write-Warn "uv python install error: $_"
162	    }
163	
164	    # Fallback: check if ANY Python 3.10+ is already available on the system
165	    Write-Info "Trying to find any existing Python 3.10+..."
166	    foreach ($fallbackVer in @("3.12", "3.13", "3.10")) {
167	        try {
168	            $pythonPath = & $UvCmd python find $fallbackVer 2>$null
169	            if ($pythonPath) {
170	                $ver = & $pythonPath --version 2>$null
171	                Write-Success "Found fallback: $ver"
172	                $script:PythonVersion = $fallbackVer
173	                return $true
174	            }
175	        } catch { }
176	    }
177	
178	    # Fallback: try system python
179	    if (Get-Command python -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
180	        $sysVer = python --version 2>$null
181	        if ($sysVer -match "3\.(1[0-9]|[1-9][0-9])") {
182	            Write-Success "Using system Python: $sysVer"
183	            return $true
184	        }
185	    }
186	    
187	    Write-Err "Failed to install Python $PythonVersion"
188	    Write-Info "Install Python 3.11 manually, then re-run this script:"
189	    Write-Info "  https://www.python.org/downloads/"
190	    Write-Info "  Or: winget install Python.Python.3.11"
191	    return $false
192	}
193	
194	function Test-Git {
195	    Write-Info "Checking Git..."
196	    
197	    if (Get-Command git -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue) {
198	        $version = git --version
199	        Write-Success "Git found ($version)"
200	        return $true
```

```text
1	"""Persistent shell mixin: file-based IPC protocol for long-lived bash shells."""
2	
3	import logging
4	import shlex
5	import subprocess
6	import threading
7	import time
8	import uuid
9	from abc import abstractmethod
10	
11	from tools.interrupt import is_interrupted
12	
13	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
14	
15	
16	class PersistentShellMixin:
17	    """Mixin that adds persistent shell capability to any BaseEnvironment.
18	
19	    Subclasses must implement ``_spawn_shell_process()``, ``_read_temp_files()``,
20	    ``_kill_shell_children()``, ``_execute_oneshot()``, and ``_cleanup_temp_files()``.
21	    """
22	
23	    persistent: bool
24	
25	    @abstractmethod
26	    def _spawn_shell_process(self) -> subprocess.Popen: ...
27	
28	    @abstractmethod
29	    def _read_temp_files(self, *paths: str) -> list[str]: ...
30	
31	    @abstractmethod
32	    def _kill_shell_children(self): ...
33	
34	    @abstractmethod
35	    def _execute_oneshot(self, command: str, cwd: str, *,
36	                         timeout: int | None = None,
37	                         stdin_data: str | None = None) -> dict: ...
38	
39	    @abstractmethod
40	    def _cleanup_temp_files(self): ...
41	
42	    _session_id: str = ""
43	    _poll_interval_start: float = 0.01  # initial poll interval (10ms)
44	    _poll_interval_max: float = 0.25    # max poll interval (250ms) — reduces I/O for long commands
45	
46	    @property
47	    def _temp_prefix(self) -> str:
48	        return f"/tmp/hermes-persistent-{self._session_id}"
49	
50	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
51	    # Lifecycle
52	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
53	
54	    def _init_persistent_shell(self):
55	        self._shell_lock = threading.Lock()
56	        self._shell_proc: subprocess.Popen | None = None
57	        self._shell_alive: bool = False
58	        self._shell_pid: int | None = None
59	
60	        self._session_id = uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]
61	        p = self._temp_prefix
62	        self._pshell_stdout = f"{p}-stdout"
63	        self._pshell_stderr = f"{p}-stderr"
64	        self._pshell_status = f"{p}-status"
65	        self._pshell_cwd = f"{p}-cwd"
66	        self._pshell_pid_file = f"{p}-pid"
67	
68	        self._shell_proc = self._spawn_shell_process()
69	        self._shell_alive = True
70	
71	        self._drain_thread = threading.Thread(
72	            target=self._drain_shell_output, daemon=True,
73	        )
74	        self._drain_thread.start()
75	
76	        init_script = (
77	            f"export TERM=${{TERM:-dumb}}\n"
78	            f"touch {self._pshell_stdout} {self._pshell_stderr} "
79	            f"{self._pshell_status} {self._pshell_cwd} {self._pshell_pid_file}\n"
80	            f"echo $$ > {self._pshell_pid_file}\n"
81	            f"pwd > {self._pshell_cwd}\n"
82	        )
83	        self._send_to_shell(init_script)
84	
85	        deadline = time.monotonic() + 3.0
86	        while time.monotonic() < deadline:
87	            pid_str = self._read_temp_files(self._pshell_pid_file)[0].strip()
88	            if pid_str.isdigit():
89	                self._shell_pid = int(pid_str)
90	                break
91	            time.sleep(0.05)
92	        else:
93	            logger.warning("Could not read persistent shell PID")
94	            self._shell_pid = None
95	
96	        if self._shell_pid:
97	            logger.info(
98	                "Persistent shell started (session=%s, pid=%d)",
99	                self._session_id, self._shell_pid,
100	            )
101	
102	        reported_cwd = self._read_temp_files(self._pshell_cwd)[0].strip()
103	        if reported_cwd:
104	            self.cwd = reported_cwd
105	
106	    def _cleanup_persistent_shell(self):
107	        if self._shell_proc is None:
108	            return
109	
110	        if self._session_id:
111	            self._cleanup_temp_files()
112	
113	        try:
114	            self._shell_proc.stdin.close()
115	        except Exception:
116	            pass
117	        try:
118	            self._shell_proc.terminate()
119	            self._shell_proc.wait(timeout=3)
120	        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
121	            self._shell_proc.kill()
122	
123	        self._shell_alive = False
124	        self._shell_proc = None
125	
126	        if hasattr(self, "_drain_thread") and self._drain_thread.is_alive():
127	            self._drain_thread.join(timeout=1.0)
128	
129	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
130	    # execute() / cleanup() — shared dispatcher, subclasses inherit
131	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
132	
133	    def execute(self, command: str, cwd: str = "", *,
134	                timeout: int | None = None,
135	                stdin_data: str | None = None) -> dict:
136	        if self.persistent:
137	            return self._execute_persistent(
138	                command, cwd, timeout=timeout, stdin_data=stdin_data,
139	            )
140	        return self._execute_oneshot(
141	            command, cwd, timeout=timeout, stdin_data=stdin_data,
142	        )
143	
144	    def cleanup(self):
145	        if self.persistent:
146	            self._cleanup_persistent_shell()
147	
148	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
149	    # Shell I/O
150	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
19	# printf (no trailing newline) keeps the boundaries clean for splitting.
20	_OUTPUT_FENCE = "__HERMES_FENCE_a9f7b3__"
21	
22	# Hermes-internal env vars that should NOT leak into terminal subprocesses.
23	# These are loaded from ~/.hermes/.env for Hermes' own LLM/provider calls
24	# but can break external CLIs (e.g. codex) that also honor them.
25	# See: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/issues/1002
26	#
27	# Built dynamically from the provider registry so new providers are
28	# automatically covered without manual blocklist maintenance.
29	_HERMES_PROVIDER_ENV_FORCE_PREFIX = "_HERMES_FORCE_"
30	
31	
32	def _build_provider_env_blocklist() -> frozenset:
33	    """Derive the blocklist from provider, tool, and gateway config.
34	
35	    Automatically picks up api_key_env_vars and base_url_env_var from
36	    every registered provider, plus tool/messaging env vars from the
37	    optional config registry, so new Hermes-managed secrets are blocked
38	    in subprocesses without having to maintain multiple static lists.
39	    """
40	    blocked: set[str] = set()
41	
42	    try:
43	        from hermes_cli.auth import PROVIDER_REGISTRY
44	        for pconfig in PROVIDER_REGISTRY.values():
45	            blocked.update(pconfig.api_key_env_vars)
46	            if pconfig.base_url_env_var:
47	                blocked.add(pconfig.base_url_env_var)
48	    except ImportError:
49	        pass
50	
51	    try:
52	        from hermes_cli.config import OPTIONAL_ENV_VARS
53	        for name, metadata in OPTIONAL_ENV_VARS.items():
54	            category = metadata.get("category")
55	            if category in {"tool", "messaging"}:
56	                blocked.add(name)
57	            elif category == "setting" and metadata.get("password"):
58	                blocked.add(name)
59	    except ImportError:
60	        pass
61	
62	    # Vars not covered above but still Hermes-internal / conflict-prone.
63	    blocked.update({
64	        "OPENAI_BASE_URL",
65	        "OPENAI_API_KEY",
66	        "OPENAI_API_BASE",         # legacy alias
67	        "OPENAI_ORG_ID",
68	        "OPENAI_ORGANIZATION",
69	        "OPENROUTER_API_KEY",
70	        "ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL",
71	        "ANTHROPIC_TOKEN",         # OAuth token (not in registry as env var)
72	        "CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN",
73	        "LLM_MODEL",
74	        # Expanded isolation for other major providers (Issue #1002)
75	        "GOOGLE_API_KEY",          # Gemini / Google AI Studio
76	        "DEEPSEEK_API_KEY",        # DeepSeek
77	        "MISTRAL_API_KEY",         # Mistral AI

81	        "COHERE_API_KEY",          # Cohere
82	        "FIREWORKS_API_KEY",       # Fireworks AI
83	        "XAI_API_KEY",             # xAI (Grok)
84	        "HELICONE_API_KEY",        # LLM Observability proxy
85	        "PARALLEL_API_KEY",
86	        "FIRECRAWL_API_KEY",
87	        "FIRECRAWL_API_URL",
88	        # Gateway/runtime config not represented in OPTIONAL_ENV_VARS.

91	        "DISCORD_HOME_CHANNEL",

132	
133	
134	def _sanitize_subprocess_env(base_env: dict | None, extra_env: dict | None = None) -> dict:
135	    """Filter Hermes-managed secrets from a subprocess environment.
136	
137	    `_HERMES_FORCE_<VAR>` entries in ``extra_env`` opt a blocked variable back in
138	    intentionally for callers that truly need it.  Vars registered via
139	    :mod:`tools.env_passthrough` (skill-declared or user-configured) also
140	    bypass the blocklist.
141	    """

144	    except Exception:

146	
147	    sanitized: dict[str, str] = {}
148	
149	    for key, value in (base_env or {}).items():
150	        if key.startswith(_HERMES_PROVIDER_ENV_FORCE_PREFIX):
```
