133	
134	def _sanitize_subprocess_env(base_env: dict | None, extra_env: dict | None = None) -> dict:
135	    """Filter Hermes-managed secrets from a subprocess environment.
136	
137	    `_HERMES_FORCE_<VAR>` entries in ``extra_env`` opt a blocked variable back in
138	    intentionally for callers that truly need it.  Vars registered via
139	    :mod:`tools.env_passthrough` (skill-declared or user-configured) also
140	    bypass the blocklist.
141	    """
142	    try:
143	        from tools.env_passthrough import is_env_passthrough as _is_passthrough
144	    except Exception:
145	        _is_passthrough = lambda _: False  # noqa: E731
146	
147	    sanitized: dict[str, str] = {}
148	
149	    for key, value in (base_env or {}).items():
150	        if key.startswith(_HERMES_PROVIDER_ENV_FORCE_PREFIX):
151	            continue
152	        if key not in _HERMES_PROVIDER_ENV_BLOCKLIST or _is_passthrough(key):
153	            sanitized[key] = value
154	
155	    for key, value in (extra_env or {}).items():
156	        if key.startswith(_HERMES_PROVIDER_ENV_FORCE_PREFIX):
157	            real_key = key[len(_HERMES_PROVIDER_ENV_FORCE_PREFIX):]
158	            sanitized[real_key] = value
159	        elif key not in _HERMES_PROVIDER_ENV_BLOCKLIST or _is_passthrough(key):
160	            sanitized[key] = value
161	
162	    return sanitized
163	
164	
165	def _find_bash() -> str:
166	    """Find bash for command execution.
167	
168	    The fence wrapper uses bash syntax (semicolons, $?, printf), so we
169	    must use bash — not the user's $SHELL which could be fish/zsh/etc.
170	    On Windows: uses Git Bash (bundled with Git for Windows).
171	    """
172	    if not _IS_WINDOWS:
173	        return (
174	            shutil.which("bash")
175	            or ("/usr/bin/bash" if os.path.isfile("/usr/bin/bash") else None)
176	            or ("/bin/bash" if os.path.isfile("/bin/bash") else None)
177	            or os.environ.get("SHELL")  # last resort: whatever they have
178	            or "/bin/sh"
179	        )
180	
181	    # Windows: look for Git Bash (installed with Git for Windows).
182	    # Allow override via env var (same pattern as Claude Code).
183	    custom = os.environ.get("HERMES_GIT_BASH_PATH")
184	    if custom and os.path.isfile(custom):
185	        return custom
186	
187	    # shutil.which finds bash.exe if Git\bin is on PATH
188	    found = shutil.which("bash")
189	    if found:
190	        return found
191	
192	    # Check common Git for Windows install locations
193	    for candidate in (
194	        os.path.join(os.environ.get("ProgramFiles", r"C:\Program Files"), "Git", "bin", "bash.exe"),
195	        os.path.join(os.environ.get("ProgramFiles(x86)", r"C:\Program Files (x86)"), "Git", "bin", "bash.exe"),
196	        os.path.join(os.environ.get("LOCALAPPDATA", ""), "Programs", "Git", "bin", "bash.exe"),
197	    ):
198	        if candidate and os.path.isfile(candidate):
199	            return candidate
200	
201	    raise RuntimeError(
202	        "Git Bash not found. Hermes Agent requires Git for Windows on Windows.\n"
203	        "Install it from: https://git-scm.com/download/win\n"
204	        "Or set HERMES_GIT_BASH_PATH to your bash.exe location."
205	    )
206	
207	
208	# Backward compat — process_registry.py imports this name
209	_find_shell = _find_bash
210	
211	
212	# Noise lines emitted by interactive shells when stdin is not a terminal.
213	# Used as a fallback when output fence markers are missing.
214	_SHELL_NOISE_SUBSTRINGS = (
215	    # bash
216	    "bash: cannot set terminal process group",
217	    "bash: no job control in this shell",
218	    "no job control in this shell",
219	    "cannot set terminal process group",
220	    "tcsetattr: Inappropriate ioctl for device",
221	    # zsh / oh-my-zsh / macOS terminal session
222	    "Restored session:",
223	    "Saving session...",
224	    "Last login:",
225	    "command not found:",
226	    "Oh My Zsh",
227	    "compinit:",
228	)
229	
230	
231	def _clean_shell_noise(output: str) -> str:
232	    """Strip shell startup/exit warnings that leak when using -i without a TTY.
233	
234	    Removes lines matching known noise patterns from both the beginning
235	    and end of the output.  Lines in the middle are left untouched.
236	    """
237	
238	    def _is_noise(line: str) -> bool:
239	        return any(noise in line for noise in _SHELL_NOISE_SUBSTRINGS)
240	
241	    lines = output.split("\n")
242	
243	    # Strip leading noise
244	    while lines and _is_noise(lines[0]):
245	        lines.pop(0)
246	
247	    # Strip trailing noise (walk backwards, skip empty lines from split)
248	    end = len(lines) - 1
249	    while end >= 0 and (not lines[end] or _is_noise(lines[end])):
250	        end -= 1
251	
252	    if end < 0:
253	        return ""
254	
255	    cleaned = lines[: end + 1]
256	    result = "\n".join(cleaned)
257	
258	    # Preserve trailing newline if original had one
259	    if output.endswith("\n") and result and not result.endswith("\n"):
260	        result += "\n"
261	    return result
262	
263	
264	# Standard PATH entries for environments with minimal PATH (e.g. systemd services).
265	# Includes macOS Homebrew paths (/opt/homebrew/* for Apple Silicon).
266	_SANE_PATH = (
267	    "/opt/homebrew/bin:/opt/homebrew/sbin:"
268	    "/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin"
269	)
270	
271	
272	def _make_run_env(env: dict) -> dict:
273	    """Build a run environment with a sane PATH and provider-var stripping."""
274	    try:
275	        from tools.env_passthrough import is_env_passthrough as _is_passthrough
276	    except Exception:
277	        _is_passthrough = lambda _: False  # noqa: E731
278	
279	    merged = dict(os.environ | env)
280	    run_env = {}
281	    for k, v in merged.items():
282	        if k.startswith(_HERMES_PROVIDER_ENV_FORCE_PREFIX):
283	            real_key = k[len(_HERMES_PROVIDER_ENV_FORCE_PREFIX):]
284	            run_env[real_key] = v
285	        elif k not in _HERMES_PROVIDER_ENV_BLOCKLIST or _is_passthrough(k):
286	            run_env[k] = v
287	    existing_path = run_env.get("PATH", "")
288	    if "/usr/bin" not in existing_path.split(":"):
289	        run_env["PATH"] = f"{existing_path}:{_SANE_PATH}" if existing_path else _SANE_PATH
290	    return run_env
291	
292	
293	def _extract_fenced_output(raw: str) -> str:
294	    """Extract real command output from between fence markers.
295	
296	    The execute() method wraps each command with printf(FENCE) markers.
297	    This function finds the first and last fence and returns only the
298	    content between them, which is the actual command output free of
299	    any shell init/exit noise.
300	
301	    Falls back to pattern-based _clean_shell_noise if fences are missing.
302	    """
303	    first = raw.find(_OUTPUT_FENCE)
304	    if first == -1:
305	        return _clean_shell_noise(raw)
306	
307	    start = first + len(_OUTPUT_FENCE)
308	    last = raw.rfind(_OUTPUT_FENCE)
309	
310	    if last <= first:
311	        # Only start fence found (e.g. user command called `exit`)
312	        return _clean_shell_noise(raw[start:])
313	
314	    return raw[start:last]
315	
316	
317	class LocalEnvironment(PersistentShellMixin, BaseEnvironment):
318	    """Run commands directly on the host machine.
319	
320	    Features:
321	    - Popen + polling for interrupt support (user can cancel mid-command)
322	    - Background stdout drain thread to prevent pipe buffer deadlocks
323	    - stdin_data support for piping content (bypasses ARG_MAX limits)
324	    - sudo -S transform via SUDO_PASSWORD env var
325	    - Uses interactive login shell so full user env is available
326	    - Optional persistent shell mode (cwd/env vars survive across calls)
327	    """
328	
329	    def __init__(self, cwd: str = "", timeout: int = 60, env: dict = None,
330	                 persistent: bool = False):
331	        super().__init__(cwd=cwd or os.getcwd(), timeout=timeout, env=env)
332	        self.persistent = persistent
333	        if self.persistent:
334	            self._init_persistent_shell()
335	
336	    @property
337	    def _temp_prefix(self) -> str:
338	        return f"/tmp/hermes-local-{self._session_id}"
339	
340	    def _spawn_shell_process(self) -> subprocess.Popen:
341	        user_shell = _find_bash()
342	        run_env = _make_run_env(self.env)
343	        return subprocess.Popen(
344	            [user_shell, "-l"],
345	            stdin=subprocess.PIPE,
346	            stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
347	            stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL,
348	            text=True,
349	            env=run_env,
350	            preexec_fn=None if _IS_WINDOWS else os.setsid,
351	        )
352	
353	    def _read_temp_files(self, *paths: str) -> list[str]:
354	        results = []
355	        for path in paths:
356	            if os.path.exists(path):
357	                with open(path) as f:
358	                    results.append(f.read())
359	            else:
360	                results.append("")
361	        return results
362	
363	    def _kill_shell_children(self):
364	        if self._shell_pid is None:
365	            return
366	        try:
367	            subprocess.run(
368	                ["pkill", "-P", str(self._shell_pid)],
369	                capture_output=True, timeout=5,
370	            )
371	        except (subprocess.TimeoutExpired, FileNotFoundError):
372	            pass
373	
374	    def _cleanup_temp_files(self):
375	        for f in glob.glob(f"{self._temp_prefix}-*"):
376	            if os.path.exists(f):
377	                os.remove(f)
378	
379	    def _execute_oneshot(self, command: str, cwd: str = "", *,
380	                         timeout: int | None = None,
381	                         stdin_data: str | None = None) -> dict:
382	        work_dir = cwd or self.cwd or os.getcwd()
383	        effective_timeout = timeout or self.timeout
384	        exec_command, sudo_stdin = self._prepare_command(command)
385	
386	        if sudo_stdin is not None and stdin_data is not None:
387	            effective_stdin = sudo_stdin + stdin_data
388	        elif sudo_stdin is not None:
389	            effective_stdin = sudo_stdin
390	        else:
391	            effective_stdin = stdin_data
392	
393	        user_shell = _find_bash()
394	        # Newline-separated wrapper (not `cmd; __hermes_rc=...` on one line).
395	        # A trailing `; __hermes_rc` glued to `<<EOF` / a closing `EOF` line breaks
396	        # heredoc parsing: the delimiter must be alone on its line, otherwise the
397	        # rest of this script becomes heredoc body and leaks into stdout (e.g. gh
398	        # issue/PR flows that use here-documents for bodies).
399	        fenced_cmd = (
400	            f"printf '{_OUTPUT_FENCE}'\n"
401	            f"{exec_command}\n"
402	            f"__hermes_rc=$?\n"
403	            f"printf '{_OUTPUT_FENCE}'\n"
404	            f"exit $__hermes_rc\n"
405	        )
406	        run_env = _make_run_env(self.env)
407	
408	        proc = subprocess.Popen(
409	            [user_shell, "-lic", fenced_cmd],
410	            text=True,
411	            cwd=work_dir,
412	            env=run_env,
413	            encoding="utf-8",
414	            errors="replace",
415	            stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
416	            stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
417	            stdin=subprocess.PIPE if effective_stdin is not None else subprocess.DEVNULL,
418	            preexec_fn=None if _IS_WINDOWS else os.setsid,
419	        )
420	
421	        if effective_stdin is not None:
422	            def _write_stdin():
423	                try:
424	                    proc.stdin.write(effective_stdin)
425	                    proc.stdin.close()
426	                except (BrokenPipeError, OSError):
427	                    pass
428	            threading.Thread(target=_write_stdin, daemon=True).start()
429	
430	        _output_chunks: list[str] = []
431	
432	        def _drain_stdout():
433	            try:
434	                for line in proc.stdout:
435	                    _output_chunks.append(line)
436	            except ValueError:
437	                pass
438	            finally:
439	                try:
440	                    proc.stdout.close()
441	                except Exception:
442	                    pass
443	
444	        reader = threading.Thread(target=_drain_stdout, daemon=True)
445	        reader.start()
446	        deadline = time.monotonic() + effective_timeout
447	
448	        while proc.poll() is None:
449	            if is_interrupted():
450	                try:
451	                    if _IS_WINDOWS:
452	                        proc.terminate()
453	                    else:
454	                        pgid = os.getpgid(proc.pid)
455	                        os.killpg(pgid, signal.SIGTERM)
456	                        try:
457	                            proc.wait(timeout=1.0)
458	                        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
459	                            os.killpg(pgid, signal.SIGKILL)
460	                except (ProcessLookupError, PermissionError):
461	                    proc.kill()
462	                reader.join(timeout=2)
463	                return {
464	                    "output": "".join(_output_chunks) + "\n[Command interrupted — user sent a new message]",
465	                    "returncode": 130,
466	                }
467	            if time.monotonic() > deadline:
468	                try:
469	                    if _IS_WINDOWS:
470	                        proc.terminate()
471	                    else:
472	                        os.killpg(os.getpgid(proc.pid), signal.SIGTERM)
473	                except (ProcessLookupError, PermissionError):
474	                    proc.kill()
475	                reader.join(timeout=2)
476	                partial = "".join(_output_chunks)
477	                timeout_msg = f"\n[Command timed out after {effective_timeout}s]"
478	                return {
479	                    "output": partial + timeout_msg if partial else timeout_msg.lstrip(),
480	                    "returncode": 124,
481	                }
482	            time.sleep(0.2)
483	
484	        reader.join(timeout=5)
485	        output = _extract_fenced_output("".join(_output_chunks))
486	        return {"output": output, "returncode": proc.returncode}
487
```
</details></tool-use>

```md
# Windowsネイティブ対応: 全機能修正プラン

## Context

Hermes Agentは正式にWindows対応を謳っているが、ローカル実行時に3つの問題でクラッシュまたは誤動作する。具体的には、persistent shellモードでPythonが`/tmp`パスを直接`open()`しようとして失敗し、Windowsサブプロセス環境にUnixパスが混入し、子プロセス終了にUnix専用の`pkill`が使われている。

## 変更ファイル

### 1. `tools/environments/persistent_shell.py` (line 1-14, 46-48)

`_temp_prefix` がハードコードで `/tmp` を返しており、`_init_persistent_shell` 内で Python の `open()` がこのパスを開く。Windows では `/tmp` が存在しないため失敗する。

**修正**: `platform` と `tempfile` をインポートし、Windows の場合は `tempfile.gettempdir()` を使用（Git BashもPythonもフォワードスラッシュを受け付ける）。

\```python
# 追加インポート
import platform
import tempfile

_IS_WINDOWS = platform.system() == "Windows"

# _temp_prefix プロパティを変更
@property
def _temp_prefix(self) -> str:
    if _IS_WINDOWS:
        base = tempfile.gettempdir().replace('\\', '/')
        return f"{base}/hermes-persistent-{self._session_id}"
    return f"/tmp/hermes-persistent-{self._session_id}"
\```

---

### 2. `tools/environments/local.py`

**2a. `LocalEnvironment._temp_prefix` (line 337-338)**

同上の問題。`LocalEnvironment` が `PersistentShellMixin` の `_temp_prefix` をオーバーライドしている。

\```python
# import tempfile を追加（ファイル先頭）
import tempfile

# _temp_prefix メソッドを変更
@property
def _temp_prefix(self) -> str:
    if _IS_WINDOWS:
        base = tempfile.gettempdir().replace('\\', '/')
        return f"{base}/hermes-local-{self._session_id}"
    return f"/tmp/hermes-local-{self._session_id}"
\```

**2b. `_make_run_env` PATH インジェクション (line 287-289)**

Windowsでは`/usr/bin`がPATHに存在しないため、常にUnixパス群がWindowsのサブプロセスPATHに追加される。

\```python
# 変更前
if "/usr/bin" not in existing_path.split(":"):
    run_env["PATH"] = f"{existing_path}:{_SANE_PATH}" if existing_path else _SANE_PATH

# 変更後
if not _IS_WINDOWS and "/usr/bin" not in existing_path.split(":"):
    run_env["PATH"] = f"{existing_path}:{_SANE_PATH}" if existing_path else _SANE_PATH
\```

**2c. `_kill_shell_children` (line 363-372)**

`pkill` はWindowsに存在しない。現在は `FileNotFoundError` でサイレントに失敗するが、persistentシェル内で実行中のコマンドが終了しないままになる。

\```python
def _kill_shell_children(self):
    if self._shell_pid is None:
        return
    try:
        if _IS_WINDOWS:
            subprocess.run(
                ["taskkill", "/F", "/FI", f"PPID eq {self._shell_pid}"],
                capture_output=True, timeout=5,
            )
        else:
            subprocess.run(
                ["pkill", "-P", str(self._shell_pid)],
                capture_output=True, timeout=5,
            )
    except (subprocess.TimeoutExpired, FileNotFoundError):
        pass
\```

## 変更が不要な箇所（確認済み）

- `os.setsid/os.killpg` → 全箇所で `_IS_WINDOWS` ガード済み
- `gateway/platforms/whatsapp.py` の `os.killpg` → `_IS_WINDOWS` ガード済み
- `tools/process_registry.py` の `/tmp` → bash経由でのみアクセス（bash on Git Windowsが処理）
- `environments/tool_context.py` の `/tmp` → bash経由でのみアクセス
- `termios/fcntl` → プラットフォーム分岐実装済み
- `/dev/tty` → `OSError` フォールバック実装済み

## 検証方法

1. `python -c "from tools.environments.local import LocalEnvironment; e = LocalEnvironment(persistent=True); print(e._temp_prefix)"` → Windows tempパスが返ること
2. persistentシェルモードで `hermes` を起動しコマンドを実行
3. `hermes` CLI 起動時にUNIXパスがPATHに混入しないことを確認
4. persistent shellで長時間コマンド実行後に割り込み（Ctrl+C）が動作することを確認

```

```text
File created successfully at: C:\Users\downl\.claude\plans\clever-exploring-sonnet.md
```
</details></tool-use>

## Approved Plan:
# Windowsネイティブ対応: 全機能修正プラン

## Context

Hermes Agentは正式にWindows対応を謳っているが、ローカル実行時に3つの問題でクラッシュまたは誤動作する。具体的には、persistent shellモードでPythonが`/tmp`パスを直接`open()`しようとして失敗し、Windowsサブプロセス環境にUnixパスが混入し、子プロセス終了にUnix専用の`pkill`が使われている。

## 変更ファイル

### 1. `tools/environments/persistent_shell.py` (line 1-14, 46-48)

`_temp_prefix` がハードコードで `/tmp` を返しており、`_init_persistent_shell` 内で Python の `open()` がこのパスを開く。Windows では `/tmp` が存在しないため失敗する。

**修正**: `platform` と `tempfile` をインポートし、Windows の場合は `tempfile.gettempdir()` を使用（Git BashもPythonもフォワードスラッシュを受け付ける）。

```python
# 追加インポート
import platform
import tempfile

_IS_WINDOWS = platform.system() == "Windows"

# _temp_prefix プロパティを変更
@property
def _temp_prefix(self) -> str:
    if _IS_WINDOWS:
        base = tempfile.gettempdir().replace('\\', '/')
        return f"{base}/hermes-persistent-{self._session_id}"
    return f"/tmp/hermes-persistent-{self._session_id}"
```

---

### 2. `tools/environments/local.py`

**2a. `LocalEnvironment._temp_prefix` (line 337-338)**

同上の問題。`LocalEnvironment` が `PersistentShellMixin` の `_temp_prefix` をオーバーライドしている。

```python
# import tempfile を追加（ファイル先頭）
import tempfile

# _temp_prefix メソッドを変更
@property
def _temp_prefix(self) -> str:
    if _IS_WINDOWS:
        base = tempfile.gettempdir().replace('\\', '/')
        return f"{base}/hermes-local-{self._session_id}"
    return f"/tmp/hermes-local-{self._session_id}"
```

**2b. `_make_run_env` PATH インジェクション (line 287-289)**

Windowsでは`/usr/bin`がPATHに存在しないため、常にUnixパス群がWindowsのサブプロセスPATHに追加される。

```python
# 変更前
if "/usr/bin" not in existing_path.split(":"):
    run_env["PATH"] = f"{existing_path}:{_SANE_PATH}" if existing_path else _SANE_PATH

# 変更後
if not _IS_WINDOWS and "/usr/bin" not in existing_path.split(":"):
    run_env["PATH"] = f"{existing_path}:{_SANE_PATH}" if existing_path else _SANE_PATH
```

**2c. `_kill_shell_children` (line 363-372)**

`pkill` はWindowsに存在しない。現在は `FileNotFoundError` でサイレントに失敗するが、persistentシェル内で実行中のコマンドが終了しないままになる。

```python
def _kill_shell_children(self):
    if self._shell_pid is None:
        return
    try:
        if _IS_WINDOWS:
            subprocess.run(
                ["taskkill", "/F", "/FI", f"PPID eq {self._shell_pid}"],
                capture_output=True, timeout=5,
            )
        else:
            subprocess.run(
                ["pkill", "-P", str(self._shell_pid)],
                capture_output=True, timeout=5,
            )
    except (subprocess.TimeoutExpired, FileNotFoundError):
        pass
```

## 変更が不要な箇所（確認済み）

- `os.setsid/os.killpg` → 全箇所で `_IS_WINDOWS` ガード済み
- `gateway/platforms/whatsapp.py` の `os.killpg` → `_IS_WINDOWS` ガード済み
- `tools/process_registry.py` の `/tmp` → bash経由でのみアクセス（bash on Git Windowsが処理）
- `environments/tool_context.py` の `/tmp` → bash経由でのみアクセス
- `termios/fcntl` → プラットフォーム分岐実装済み
- `/dev/tty` → `OSError` フォールバック実装済み

# Windows ネイティブ対応 実装計画

## 全体概要

修正対象は2ファイル、3箇所の独立した問題です。各修正は互いに依存しないため、任意の順序で適用可能ですが、リスクの低い順に実施することを推奨します。

---

## 修正1: `local.py` の `_temp_prefix` プロパティ（行338）

### 対象

`C:\Users\downl\Desktop\hermes-agent-main\hermes-agent-main\tools\environments\local.py`、337〜338行目

### 問題の詳細

`_read_temp_files()` メソッド（353〜361行目）は Python の `open()` を直接呼び出す。`/tmp/hermes-local-XXXX` というパスはGit Bash内では有効だが、Pythonのネイティブファイルシステムアクセスでは `C:\tmp\hermes-local-XXXX` と解釈され、そのディレクトリが存在しない場合はファイルが作られず、永続シェルモードが完全に動作しない。

### Before（337〜338行目）

```python
@property
def _temp_prefix(self) -> str:
    return f"/tmp/hermes-local-{self._session_id}"
```

### After

```python
@property
def _temp_prefix(self) -> str:
    if _IS_WINDOWS:
        import tempfile
        tmp = tempfile.gettempdir().replace("\\", "/")
        return f"{tmp}/hermes-local-{self._session_id}"
    return f"/tmp/hermes-local-{self._session_id}"
```

### 設計根拠

- `tempfile.gettempdir()` は `C:\Users\XXXX\AppData\Local\Temp` を返す
- `.replace("\\", "/")` でフォワードスラッシュに正規化することで、Python の `open()` とGit Bash の両方から同一パスにアクセスできる（Git Bash は `/c/Users/...` のマウントポイント経由でフォワードスラッシュを処理する）
- `import tempfile` はローカルインポートにする。ファイル冒頭の `import` セクションに追加してもよいが、変更箇所を最小化するためインラインで記述する方が安全

### 注意点

`_cleanup_temp_files()`（374〜377行目）の `glob.glob(f"{self._temp_prefix}-*")` も同じプレフィックスを参照する。`_temp_prefix` プロパティを修正すれば glob のパスも自動的に正しくなるため、追加変更は不要。

---

## 修正2: `local.py` の `_make_run_env()` 関数（行287〜289）

### 対象

`C:\Users\downl\Desktop\hermes-agent-main\hermes-agent-main\tools\environments\local.py`、287〜289行目

### 問題の詳細

Windowsの `PATH` 環境変数はセミコロン区切りで、Unix パスが存在しない。条件 `"/usr/bin" not in existing_path.split(":")` はWindowsでは常にTrueになるため、`_SANE_PATH`（Unix専用の `/opt/homebrew/bin:...` 等）が必ずWindowsのPATHに追加される。これによってサブプロセス起動時の環境変数が汚染される。

### Before（287〜289行目）

```python
existing_path = run_env.get("PATH", "")
if "/usr/bin" not in existing_path.split(":"):
    run_env["PATH"] = f"{existing_path}:{_SANE_PATH}" if existing_path else _SANE_PATH
return run_env
```

### After

```python
existing_path = run_env.get("PATH", "")
if not _IS_WINDOWS and "/usr/bin" not in existing_path.split(":"):
    run_env["PATH"] = f"{existing_path}:{_SANE_PATH}" if existing_path else _SANE_PATH
return run_env
```

### 設計根拠

- `_IS_WINDOWS` はファイル冒頭（12行目）で既に定義済みのため、インポート追加は不要
- Windowsの場合、Git Bash 自身が `/usr/bin` 等を内部的にマウントして処理するため、Python側からPATHを補完する必要がない
- `_SANE_PATH` 定数自体は削除しない。他の環境（SSH経由でLinuxに接続する場合等）で引き続き使用される可能性があるため

---

## 修正3: `local.py` の `_kill_shell_children()` メソッド（行363〜372）

### 対象

`C:\Users\downl\Desktop\hermes-agent-main\hermes-agent-main\tools\environments\local.py`、363〜372行目

### 問題の詳細

`pkill -P <pid>` は指定PIDを親に持つ子プロセスを全終了させるUnixコマンド。Windowsには存在しない。現状は `FileNotFoundError` を無言で握りつぶしているため、タイムアウト/割り込み時に子プロセスがゾンビとして残留する。

### Before（363〜372行目）

```python
def _kill_shell_children(self):
    if self._shell_pid is None:
        return
    try:
        subprocess.run(
            ["pkill", "-P", str(self._shell_pid)],
            capture_output=True, timeout=5,
        )
    except (subprocess.TimeoutExpired, FileNotFoundError):
        pass
```

### After

```python
def _kill_shell_children(self):
    if self._shell_pid is None:
        return
    try:
        if _IS_WINDOWS:
            subprocess.run(
                ["taskkill", "/F", "/FI", f"PPID eq {self._shell_pid}"],
                capture_output=True, timeout=5,
            )
        else:
            subprocess.run(
                ["pkill", "-P", str(self._shell_pid)],
                capture_output=True, timeout=5,
            )
    except (subprocess.TimeoutExpired, FileNotFoundError):
        pass
```

### 設計根拠

- `taskkill /F /FI "PPID eq <pid>"` はWindows標準コマンド（`system32\taskkill.exe`）で、指定した親PIDを持つ全子プロセスを強制終了する
- `/F` (Force) フラグが必要。これなしでは確認ダイアログが必要なプロセスが終了しない場合がある
- `FileNotFoundError` の catch は Windows では `taskkill.exe` が見つからないシステム（極めて稀）への保険として残す
- `_IS_WINDOWS` は同ファイル12行目で定義済みのため追加インポートなし

---

## 修正4: `persistent_shell.py` の `_temp_prefix` プロパティ（行46〜48）

### 対象

`C:\Users\downl\Desktop\hermes-agent-main\hermes-agent-main\tools\environments\persistent_shell.py`、1〜13行目（インポート）と46〜48行目

### 問題の詳細

`PersistentShellMixin` の `_temp_prefix` はデフォルト実装として定義されているが、`LocalEnvironment` が `_temp_prefix` をオーバーライドするため、**通常のローカル実行では問題が発生しない**。しかし、このデフォルト実装が将来的に他のサブクラスで使われた場合、同じ問題が再発する。さらに、`persistent_shell.py` は `platform` も `_IS_WINDOWS` もインポートしていない点が問題の本質。

### Before（インポートセクション 1〜11行目）

```python
import logging
import shlex
import subprocess
import threading
import time
import uuid
from abc import abstractmethod
```

### Before（46〜48行目）

```python
@property
def _temp_prefix(self) -> str:
    return f"/tmp/hermes-persistent-{self._session_id}"
```

### After（インポートセクションに追加）

```python
import logging
import platform
import shlex
import subprocess
import tempfile
import threading
import time
import uuid
from abc import abstractmethod

_IS_WINDOWS = platform.system() == "Windows"
```

### After（46〜50行目）

```python
@property
def _temp_prefix(self) -> str:
    if _IS_WINDOWS:
        tmp = tempfile.gettempdir().replace("\\", "/")
        return f"{tmp}/hermes-persistent-{self._session_id}"
    return f"/tmp/hermes-persistent-{self._session_id}"
```

### 設計根拠

- `LocalEnvironment._temp_prefix` がオーバーライドするため、現在この `MixIn` の実装はローカル実行では呼ばれない
- しかし防衛的設計として、`MixIn` 自体もWindows対応にすることで将来のサブクラス（例えば `DockerEnvironment` や新規環境クラス）がこのデフォルト実装を引き継いだ際に自動的に動作する
- `platform` と `tempfile` の追加インポートはいずれも標準ライブラリのため外部依存なし
- `_IS_WINDOWS = platform.system() == "Windows"` はモジュールレベルで定義し、`local.py` と同じパターンに統一する

---

## 実装順序と依存関係

```
修正1 (local.py _temp_prefix)         ←── 独立
修正2 (local.py _make_run_env PATH)   ←── 独立
修正3 (local.py _kill_shell_children) ←── 独立
修正4 (persistent_shell.py)           ←── 修正1と論理的に対応するが技術的独立
```

推奨実施順序:

1. **修正2** (PATH汚染) - リスク最低。条件追加のみ。副作用なし
2. **修正3** (pkill→taskkill) - リスク低。既存の `FileNotFoundError` catch があるため現状でも無言失敗している
3. **修正1** (local.py _temp_prefix) - リスク中。persistent モードを使う全パスに影響
4. **修正4** (persistent_shell.py _temp_prefix) - リスク最低だが念のため最後。現状のローカル実行では呼ばれない

---

## 検証方法

### 修正1・4の検証（_temp_prefix）

```python
# Pythonインタープリタで直接確認
import tempfile
print(tempfile.gettempdir())
# 期待値: C:\Users\<user>\AppData\Local\Temp

tmp = tempfile.gettempdir().replace("\\", "/")
print(tmp)
# 期待値: C:/Users/<user>/AppData/Local/Temp

# Git Bash側での確認
# bash -c "ls C:/Users/<user>/AppData/Local/Temp/hermes-local-*"
```

Hermesを `persistent=True` で起動してコマンドを実行し、エラーなく出力が返ることを確認する。

### 修正2の検証（PATH）

```python
# 修正後に _make_run_env({}) を呼び出してPATHを確認
env = _make_run_env({})
assert "/opt/homebrew" not in env.get("PATH", ""), "Windowsで_SANE_PATHが混入している"
```

### 修正3の検証（pkill/taskkill）

タイムアウトが発生するコマンドを実行し、プロセスが正常終了することを確認:

```bash
# Git Bash経由でHermesに対して
# タイムアウトを意図的に短く設定した環境で sleep 100 を実行
# → "Command timed out" メッセージが返り、プロセスが残留しないことを確認
# → タスクマネージャーでゾンビプロセスがないことを確認
```

---

## 変更外の確認事項

`_cleanup_temp_files()` の `glob.glob(f"{self._temp_prefix}-*")` については、`_temp_prefix` の修正が反映されれば自動的に正しいパスを参照するため変更不要。

`ssh.py` の `_temp_prefix`（169行目）と `_kill_shell_children`（213行目）はSSH接続先（リモートのLinux）で実行されるコマンドのため、Windowsネイティブ化は不要。

---

### Critical Files for Implementation

- `C:\Users\downl\Desktop\hermes-agent-main\hermes-agent-main\tools\environments\local.py`
- `C:\Users\downl\Desktop\hermes-agent-main\hermes-agent-main\tools\environments\persistent_shell.py`

## 変更内容

**`tools/environments/persistent_shell.py`**
- `platform`, `tempfile` をインポート追加
- `_IS_WINDOWS` 定義を追加
- `_temp_prefix` プロパティ: Windows では `tempfile.gettempdir()` を使用（`C:/Users/.../AppData/Local/Temp/hermes-persistent-...`）

**`tools/environments/local.py`**
- `tempfile` をインポート追加
- `LocalEnvironment._temp_prefix`: 同様にWindowsパスを使用
- `_make_run_env`: Windows では Unix パス群（`/opt/homebrew/bin` 等）を PATH に混入しないようスキップ
- `_kill_shell_children`: Windows では `taskkill /F /FI "PPID eq <pid>"` を使用（Unix では従来通り `pkill -P`）

## 修正1: `local.py` の `_temp_prefix` プロパティ（行338）

### 対象

`C:\Users\downl\Desktop\hermes-agent-main\hermes-agent-main\tools\environments\local.py`、337〜338行目

## 修正3: `local.py` の `_kill_shell_children()` メソッド（行363〜372）

### 対象

`C:\Users\downl\Desktop\hermes-agent-main\hermes-agent-main\tools\environments\local.py`、363〜372行目

### 問題の詳細

`pkill -P <pid>` は指定PIDを親に持つ子プロセスを全終了させるUnixコマンド。Windowsには存在しない。現状は `FileNotFoundError` を無言で握りつぶしているため、タイムアウト/割り込み時に子プロセスがゾンビとして残留する。

def _kill_shell_children(self):

```python
def _kill_shell_children(self):

- `taskkill /F /FI "PPID eq <pid>"` はWindows標準コマンド（`system32\taskkill.exe`）で、指定した親PIDを持つ全子プロセスを強制終了する

- `_IS_WINDOWS` は同ファイル12行目で定義済みのため追加インポートなし

---

## 修正4: `persistent_shell.py` の `_temp_prefix` プロパティ（行46〜48）

### 対象

`C:\Users\downl\Desktop\hermes-agent-main\hermes-agent-main\tools\environments\persistent_shell.py`、1〜13行目（インポート）と46〜48行目

### 問題の詳細

`PersistentShellMixin` の `_temp_prefix` はデフォルト実装として定義されているが、`LocalEnvironment` が `_temp_prefix` をオーバーライドするため、**通常のローカル実行では問題が発生しない**。しかし、このデフォルト実装が将来的に他のサブクラスで使われた場合、同じ問題が再発する。さらに、`persistent_shell.py` は `platform` も `_IS_WINDOWS` もインポートしていない点が問題の本質。

### Before（インポートセクション 1〜11行目）

```python
import logging
import shlex
import subprocess
import threading
import time
import uuid
from abc import abstractmethod
```

### Before（46〜48行目）

```python
@property
def _temp_prefix(self) -> str:
    return f"/tmp/hermes-persistent-{self._session_id}"
```

### After（インポートセクションに追加）

```python
import logging
import platform
import shlex
import subprocess
import tempfile
import threading
import time
import uuid
from abc import abstractmethod

_IS_WINDOWS = platform.system() == "Windows"
```

### After（46〜50行目）

```python
@property
def _temp_prefix(self) -> str:
    if _IS_WINDOWS:
        tmp = tempfile.gettempdir().replace("\\", "/")
        return f"{tmp}/hermes-persistent-{self._session_id}"
    return f"/tmp/hermes-persistent-{self._session_id}"
```

### 設計根拠

- `LocalEnvironment._temp_prefix` がオーバーライドするため、現在この `MixIn` の実装はローカル実行では呼ばれない
- しかし防衛的設計として、`MixIn` 自体もWindows対応にすることで将来のサブクラス（例えば `DockerEnvironment` や新規環境クラス）がこのデフォルト実装を引き継いだ際に自動的に動作する
- `platform` と `tempfile` の追加インポートはいずれも標準ライブラリのため外部依存なし
- `_IS_WINDOWS = platform.system() == "Windows"` はモジュールレベルで定義し、`local.py` と同じパターンに統一する

---

## 実装順序と依存関係

```
修正1 (local.py _temp_prefix)         ←── 独立
修正2 (local.py _make_run_env PATH)   ←── 独立
修正3 (local.py _kill_shell_children) ←── 独立
修正4 (persistent_shell.py)           ←── 修正1と論理的に対応するが技術的独立
```

推奨実施順序:

1. **修正2** (PATH汚染) - リスク最低。条件追加のみ。副作用なし
2. **修正3** (pkill→taskkill) - リスク低。既存の `FileNotFoundError` catch があるため現状でも無言失敗している
3. **修正1** (local.py _temp_prefix) - リスク中。persistent モードを使う全パスに影響
4. **修正4** (persistent_shell.py _temp_prefix) - リスク最低だが念のため最後。現状のローカル実行では呼ばれない

---

## 検証方法

### 修正1・4の検証（_temp_prefix）

```python
# Pythonインタープリタで直接確認
import tempfile
print(tempfile.gettempdir())
# 期待値: C:\Users\<user>\AppData\Local\Temp

tmp = tempfile.gettempdir().replace("\\", "/")
print(tmp)
# 期待値: C:/Users/<user>/AppData/Local/Temp

# Git Bash側での確認
# bash -c "ls C:/Users/<user>/AppData/Local/Temp/hermes-local-*"
```

Hermesを `persistent=True` で起動してコマンドを実行し、エラーなく出力が返ることを確認する。

### 修正2の検証（PATH）

```python
# 修正後に _make_run_env({}) を呼び出してPATHを確認
env = _make_run_env({})
assert "/opt/homebrew" not in env.get("PATH", ""), "Windowsで_SANE_PATHが混入している"
```

### 修正3の検証（pkill/taskkill）

タイムアウトが発生するコマンドを実行し、プロセスが正常終了することを確認:

```bash
# Git Bash経由でHermesに対して
# タイムアウトを意図的に短く設定した環境で sleep 100 を実行
# → "Command timed out" メッセージが返り、プロセスが残留しないことを確認
# → タスクマネージャーでゾンビプロセスがないことを確認
```

---

## 変更外の確認事項

`_cleanup_temp_files()` の `glob.glob(f"{self._temp_prefix}-*")` については、`_temp_prefix` の修正が反映されれば自動的に正しいパスを参照するため変更不要。

`ssh.py` の `_temp_prefix`（169行目）と `_kill_shell_children`（213行目）はSSH接続先（リモートのLinux）で実行されるコマンドのため、Windowsネイティブ化は不要。

---

### Critical Files for Implementation

- `C:\Users\downl\Desktop\hermes-agent-main\hermes-agent-main\tools\environments\local.py`
- `C:\Users\downl\Desktop\hermes-agent-main\hermes-agent-main\tools\environments\persistent_shell.py`</result>

</task-notification>

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-31 16:37:45Z)**_

Planエージェントも同じ結論に達しました。既に全修正が完了しています。

実装済みの変更をまとめると：

| ファイル | 修正箇所 | 内容 |
|---|---|---|
| `persistent_shell.py` | `_temp_prefix` プロパティ | `tempfile.gettempdir()` でWindowsパスを使用 |
| `local.py` | `LocalEnvironment._temp_prefix` | 同上（`/tmp/hermes-local-*` → Windowsパス） |
| `local.py` | `_make_run_env` PATH処理 | Windowsでは`_SANE_PATH`（Unix専用）を注入しない |
| `local.py` | `_kill_shell_children` | Windowsでは`taskkill /F /FI "PPID eq <pid>"`を使用 |

なお、`ssh.py` の `_temp_prefix` は変更不要です（SSH接続先のリモートLinux上で実行されるため）。

cd "C:\Users\downl\Desktop\hermes-agent-main\hermes-agent-main" && git add tools/environments/local.py tools/environments/persistent_shell.py && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(windows): make all features work natively on Windows

- Use tempfile.gettempdir() for persistent shell temp paths instead of
  hardcoded /tmp, allowing Python open() and Git Bash to both access
  the same files (e.g. C:/Users/.../AppData/Local/Temp/hermes-local-*)
- Skip Unix _SANE_PATH injection into subprocess PATH on Windows to
  prevent /opt/homebrew and /usr/bin from polluting Windows environment
- Replace pkill -P with taskkill /F /FI "PPID eq <pid>" on Windows for
  proper child process termination in persistent shell mode
- Add platform/tempfile imports and _IS_WINDOWS flag to persistent_shell.py

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>

 `C:\Users\downl\Desktop\hermes-agent-main\hermes-agent-main\README.md`

```text
1	<p align="center">
2	  <img src="assets/banner.png" alt="Hermes Agent" width="100%">
3	</p>
4	
5	# Hermes Agent ☤
6	
7	<p align="center">
8	  <a href="https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/"><img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/Docs-hermes--agent.nousresearch.com-FFD700?style=for-the-badge" alt="Documentation"></a>
9	  <a href="https://discord.gg/NousResearch"><img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/Discord-5865F2?style=for-the-badge&logo=discord&logoColor=white" alt="Discord"></a>
10	  <a href="https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/blob/main/LICENSE"><img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-green?style=for-the-badge" alt="License: MIT"></a>
11	  <a href="https://nousresearch.com"><img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/Built%20by-Nous%20Research-blueviolet?style=for-the-badge" alt="Built by Nous Research"></a>
12	</p>
13	
14	**The self-improving AI agent built by [Nous Research](https://nousresearch.com).** It's the only agent with a built-in learning loop — it creates skills from experience, improves them during use, nudges itself to persist knowledge, searches its own past conversations, and builds a deepening model of who you are across sessions. Run it on a $5 VPS, a GPU cluster, or serverless infrastructure that costs nearly nothing when idle. It's not tied to your laptop — talk to it from Telegram while it works on a cloud VM.
15	
16	Use any model you want — [Nous Portal](https://portal.nousresearch.com), [OpenRouter](https://openrouter.ai) (200+ models), [z.ai/GLM](https://z.ai), [Kimi/Moonshot](https://platform.moonshot.ai), [MiniMax](https://www.minimax.io), OpenAI, or your own endpoint. Switch with `hermes model` — no code changes, no lock-in.
17	
18	<table>
19	<tr><td><b>A real terminal interface</b></td><td>Full TUI with multiline editing, slash-command autocomplete, conversation history, interrupt-and-redirect, and streaming tool output.</td></tr>
20	<tr><td><b>Lives where you do</b></td><td>Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, Signal, and CLI — all from a single gateway process. Voice memo transcription, cross-platform conversation continuity.</td></tr>
21	<tr><td><b>A closed learning loop</b></td><td>Agent-curated memory with periodic nudges. Autonomous skill creation after complex tasks. Skills self-improve during use. FTS5 session search with LLM summarization for cross-session recall. <a href="https://github.com/plastic-labs/honcho">Honcho</a> dialectic user modeling. Compatible with the <a href="https://agentskills.io">agentskills.io</a> open standard.</td></tr>
22	<tr><td><b>Scheduled automations</b></td><td>Built-in cron scheduler with delivery to any platform. Daily reports, nightly backups, weekly audits — all in natural language, running unattended.</td></tr>
23	<tr><td><b>Delegates and parallelizes</b></td><td>Spawn isolated subagents for parallel workstreams. Write Python scripts that call tools via RPC, collapsing multi-step pipelines into zero-context-cost turns.</td></tr>
24	<tr><td><b>Runs anywhere, not just your laptop</b></td><td>Six terminal backends — local, Docker, SSH, Daytona, Singularity, and Modal. Daytona and Modal offer serverless persistence — your agent's environment hibernates when idle and wakes on demand, costing nearly nothing between sessions. Run it on a $5 VPS or a GPU cluster.</td></tr>
25	<tr><td><b>Research-ready</b></td><td>Batch trajectory generation, Atropos RL environments, trajectory compression for training the next generation of tool-calling models.</td></tr>
26	</table>
27	
28	---
29	
30	## Quick Install
31	
32	```bash
33	curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/main/scripts/install.sh | bash
34	```
35	
36	Works on Linux, macOS, and WSL2. The installer handles everything — Python, Node.js, dependencies, and the `hermes` command. No prerequisites except git.
37	
38	> **Windows:** Native Windows is not supported. Please install [WSL2](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/wsl/install) and run the command above.
39	
40	After installation:
41	
42	```bash
43	source ~/.bashrc    # reload shell (or: source ~/.zshrc)
44	hermes              # start chatting!
45	```
46	
47	---
48	
49	## Getting Started
50	
51	```bash
52	hermes              # Interactive CLI — start a conversation
53	hermes model        # Choose your LLM provider and model
54	hermes tools        # Configure which tools are enabled
55	hermes config set   # Set individual config values
56	hermes gateway      # Start the messaging gateway (Telegram, Discord, etc.)
57	hermes setup        # Run the full setup wizard (configures everything at once)
58	hermes claw migrate # Migrate from OpenClaw (if coming from OpenClaw)
59	hermes update       # Update to the latest version
60	hermes doctor       # Diagnose any issues
61	```
62	
63	📖 **[Full documentation →](https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/)**
64	
65	## CLI vs Messaging Quick Reference
66	
67	Hermes has two entry points: start the terminal UI with `hermes`, or run the gateway and talk to it from Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, Signal, or Email. Once you're in a conversation, many slash commands are shared across both interfaces.
68	
69	| Action | CLI | Messaging platforms |
70	|---------|-----|---------------------|
71	| Start chatting | `hermes` | Run `hermes gateway setup` + `hermes gateway start`, then send the bot a message |
72	| Start fresh conversation | `/new` or `/reset` | `/new` or `/reset` |
73	| Change model | `/model [provider:model]` | `/model [provider:model]` |
74	| Set a personality | `/personality [name]` | `/personality [name]` |
75	| Retry or undo the last turn | `/retry`, `/undo` | `/retry`, `/undo` |
76	| Compress context / check usage | `/compress`, `/usage`, `/insights [--days N]` | `/compress`, `/usage`, `/insights [days]` |
77	| Browse skills | `/skills` or `/<skill-name>` | `/skills` or `/<skill-name>` |
78	| Interrupt current work | `Ctrl+C` or send a new message | `/stop` or send a new message |
79	| Platform-specific status | `/platforms` | `/status`, `/sethome` |
80	
81	For the full command lists, see the [CLI guide](https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/cli) and the [Messaging Gateway guide](https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/messaging).
82	
83	---
84	
85	## Documentation
86	
87	All documentation lives at **[hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs](https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/)**:
88	
89	| Section | What's Covered |
90	|---------|---------------|
91	| [Quickstart](https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/getting-started/quickstart) | Install → setup → first conversation in 2 minutes |
92	| [CLI Usage](https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/cli) | Commands, keybindings, personalities, sessions |
93	| [Configuration](https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/configuration) | Config file, providers, models, all options |
94	| [Messaging Gateway](https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/messaging) | Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, Signal, Home Assistant |
95	| [Security](https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/security) | Command approval, DM pairing, container isolation |
96	| [Tools & Toolsets](https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/features/tools) | 40+ tools, toolset system, terminal backends |
97	| [Skills System](https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/features/skills) | Procedural memory, Skills Hub, creating skills |
98	| [Memory](https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/features/memory) | Persistent memory, user profiles, best practices |
99	| [MCP Integration](https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/features/mcp) | Connect any MCP server for extended capabilities |
100	| [Cron Scheduling](https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/features/cron) | Scheduled tasks with platform delivery |
101	| [Context Files](https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/features/context-files) | Project context that shapes every conversation |
102	| [Architecture](https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/developer-guide/architecture) | Project structure, agent loop, key classes |
103	| [Contributing](https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/developer-guide/contributing) | Development setup, PR process, code style |
104	| [CLI Reference](https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/reference/cli-commands) | All commands and flags |
105	| [Environment Variables](https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/reference/environment-variables) | Complete env var reference |
106	
107	---
108	
109	## Migrating from OpenClaw
110	
111	If you're coming from OpenClaw, Hermes can automatically import your settings, memories, skills, and API keys.
112	
113	**During first-time setup:** The setup wizard (`hermes setup`) automatically detects `~/.openclaw` and offers to migrate before configuration begins.
114	
115	**Anytime after install:**
116	
117	```bash
118	hermes claw migrate              # Interactive migration (full preset)
119	hermes claw migrate --dry-run    # Preview what would be migrated
120	hermes claw migrate --preset user-data   # Migrate without secrets
121	hermes claw migrate --overwrite  # Overwrite existing conflicts
122	```
123	
124	What gets imported:
125	- **SOUL.md** — persona file
126	- **Memories** — MEMORY.md and USER.md entries
127	- **Skills** — user-created skills → `~/.hermes/skills/openclaw-imports/`
128	- **Command allowlist** — approval patterns
129	- **Messaging settings** — platform configs, allowed users, working directory
130	- **API keys** — allowlisted secrets (Telegram, OpenRouter, OpenAI, Anthropic, ElevenLabs)
131	- **TTS assets** — workspace audio files
132	- **Workspace instructions** — AGENTS.md (with `--workspace-target`)
133	
134	See `hermes claw migrate --help` for all options, or use the `openclaw-migration` skill for an interactive agent-guided migration with dry-run previews.
135	
136	---
137	
138	## Contributing
139	
140	We welcome contributions! See the [Contributing Guide](https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/developer-guide/contributing) for development setup, code style, and PR process.
141	
142	Quick start for contributors:
143	
144	```bash
145	git clone https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent.git
146	cd hermes-agent
147	curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh
148	uv venv venv --python 3.11
149	source venv/bin/activate
150	uv pip install -e ".[all,dev]"
151	python -m pytest tests/ -q
152	```
153	
154	> **RL Training (optional):** To work on the RL/Tinker-Atropos integration:
155	> ```bash
156	> git submodule update --init tinker-atropos
157	> uv pip install -e "./tinker-atropos"
158	> ```

```md
<p align="center">
  <img src="assets/banner.png" alt="Hermes Agent" width="100%">
</p>

# Hermes Agent ☤ — Windows Native Fork

<p align="center">
  <a href="https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/"><img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/Docs-hermes--agent.nousresearch.com-FFD700?style=for-the-badge" alt="Documentation"></a>
  <a href="https://discord.gg/NousResearch"><img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/Discord-5865F2?style=for-the-badge&logo=discord&logoColor=white" alt="Discord"></a>
  <a href="https://github.com/zapabob/hermes-agent/blob/main/LICENSE"><img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-green?style=for-the-badge" alt="License: MIT"></a>
  <a href="https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent"><img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/Upstream-NousResearch-blueviolet?style=for-the-badge" alt="Upstream: NousResearch"></a>
</p>

**このリポジトリは [NousResearch/hermes-agent](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent) の Windows ネイティブ対応フォークです。**
WSL2 なしで Windows 11 上でそのまま動作します。

> **Original:** The self-improving AI agent built by [Nous Research](https://nousresearch.com). Use any model — Nous Portal, OpenRouter (200+ models), OpenAI, Anthropic, or your own endpoint. Switch with `hermes model` — no lock-in.

---

## このフォークの変更点

アップストリームの `NousResearch/hermes-agent` に対して以下の Windows ネイティブ対応を追加しています。

| 修正箇所 | 内容 |
|---|---|
| `persistent_shell.py` `_temp_prefix` | `/tmp` ハードコードを `tempfile.gettempdir()` に変更。Python の `open()` と Git Bash 両方からアクセス可能に |
| `local.py` `_temp_prefix` | 同上 (`/tmp/hermes-local-*` → `C:/Users/.../AppData/Local/Temp/hermes-local-*`) |
| `local.py` `_make_run_env` | Windows では Unix 専用の `_SANE_PATH` (`/opt/homebrew/bin` 等) をサブプロセス PATH に混入しないよう修正 |
| `local.py` `_kill_shell_children` | `pkill -P` (Unix 専用) を `taskkill /F /FI "PPID eq <pid>"` (Windows 標準) に置き換え |

---

## クイックインストール

### Windows ネイティブ（Git Bash 必須）

1. **[Git for Windows](https://git-scm.com/download/win)** をインストール（Git Bash が含まれます）
2. **[uv](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/getting-started/installation/)** をインストール

\```powershell
# PowerShell
irm https://astral.sh/uv/install.ps1 | iex
\```

3. このリポジトリをクローンしてインストール：

\```bash
# Git Bash または PowerShell
git clone https://github.com/zapabob/hermes-agent.git
cd hermes-agent
uv venv venv --python 3.11
source venv/Scripts/activate   # Git Bash の場合
# または: .\venv\Scripts\activate  # PowerShell の場合
uv pip install -e ".[all]"
hermes
\```

### Linux / macOS / WSL2

\```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/main/scripts/install.sh | bash
source ~/.bashrc
hermes
\```

---

## はじめに

\```bash
hermes              # 会話を開始
hermes model        # LLM プロバイダーとモデルを選択
hermes tools        # 有効にするツールを設定
hermes config set   # 個別の設定値を変更
hermes gateway      # メッセージングゲートウェイを起動 (Telegram, Discord など)
hermes setup        # セットアップウィザードを実行
hermes update       # 最新バージョンに更新
hermes doctor       # 問題を診断
\```

📖 **[公式ドキュメント →](https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/)**

---

## 主な機能

<table>
<tr><td><b>リアルターミナル UI</b></td><td>マルチライン編集、スラッシュコマンド補完、会話履歴、割り込み、ストリーミング出力付きの TUI</td></tr>
<tr><td><b>どこでも使える</b></td><td>Telegram、Discord、Slack、WhatsApp、Signal、CLI — 単一のゲートウェイプロセスから</td></tr>
<tr><td><b>学習ループ</b></td><td>エージェント主導のメモリ管理。複雑なタスク後の自律スキル生成。スキルの自己改善。セッション横断の FTS5 検索と LLM 要約</td></tr>
<tr><td><b>スケジュール自動化</b></td><td>自然言語によるクロンスケジューラー。日次レポート、夜間バックアップなど</td></tr>
<tr><td><b>並列サブエージェント</b></td><td>独立したサブエージェントを並列スポーン。Python スクリプトから RPC 経由でツール呼び出し可能</td></tr>
<tr><td><b>複数実行環境</b></td><td>ローカル、Docker、SSH、Daytona、Singularity、Modal の 6 バックエンド対応</td></tr>
</table>

---

## CLI とメッセージングのクイックリファレンス

| 操作 | CLI | メッセージングプラットフォーム |
|---|---|---|
| 会話開始 | `hermes` | `hermes gateway setup` + `hermes gateway start` 後にボットにメッセージ送信 |
| 新しい会話 | `/new` または `/reset` | `/new` または `/reset` |
| モデル変更 | `/model [provider:model]` | `/model [provider:model]` |
| ペルソナ設定 | `/personality [name]` | `/personality [name]` |
| 最後のターンをやり直し | `/retry`、`/undo` | `/retry`、`/undo` |
| コンテキスト圧縮 / 使用量確認 | `/compress`、`/usage`、`/insights` | `/compress`、`/usage`、`/insights` |
| スキル一覧 | `/skills` または `/<skill-name>` | `/skills` または `/<skill-name>` |
| 実行中の作業を割り込み | `Ctrl+C` または新しいメッセージ | `/stop` または新しいメッセージ |

---

| セクション | 内容 |
|---|---|
| [クイックスタート](https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/getting-started/quickstart) | インストール → セットアップ → 最初の会話 |
| [CLI 使い方](https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/cli) | コマンド、キーバインド、ペルソナ、セッション |
| [設定](https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/configuration) | 設定ファイル、プロバイダー、モデル |
| [メッセージングゲートウェイ](https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/messaging) | Telegram、Discord、Slack、WhatsApp、Signal |
| [セキュリティ](https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/security) | コマンド承認、DM ペアリング、コンテナ分離 |
| [ツール & ツールセット](https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/features/tools) | 40+ ツール、ツールセットシステム |
| [スキルシステム](https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/features/skills) | 手続きメモリ、Skills Hub、スキル作成 |
| [メモリ](https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/features/memory) | 永続メモリ、ユーザープロファイル |
| [MCP 連携](https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/features/mcp) | 任意の MCP サーバーを接続 |
| [クロンスケジューリング](https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/user-guide/features/cron) | スケジュールタスクとプラットフォーム配信 |

## OpenClaw からの移行

\```bash
hermes claw migrate              # インタラクティブ移行
hermes claw migrate --dry-run    # プレビュー
hermes claw migrate --preset user-data  # シークレットなしで移行
\```

移行される内容: SOUL.md、メモリ、スキル、コマンド許可リスト、メッセージング設定、API キー

---

## 開発者向けセットアップ

\```bash
git clone https://github.com/zapabob/hermes-agent.git
cd hermes-agent
uv venv venv --python 3.11
source venv/Scripts/activate  # Windows Git Bash
uv pip install -e ".[all,dev]"
python -m pytest tests/ -q
\```

コントリビューションは [NousResearch/hermes-agent](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent) へのプルリクエストもご検討ください。

<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Commit and push README rewrite

```bash
cd "C:\Users\downl\Desktop\hermes-agent-main\hermes-agent-main" && git add README.md && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
docs: rewrite README for Windows native fork

- Add Windows native fork banner and change summary table
- Replace "Windows not supported" with Git Bash installation guide
- Add Japanese UI for key sections
- Update issue/PR links to point to this fork
- Credit upstream NousResearch and fork author

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git push origin main 2>&1
```

1	# Hermes Agent Environment Configuration
2	# Copy this file to .env and fill in your API keys

4	# =============================================================================
5	# LLM PROVIDER (OpenRouter)
6	# =============================================================================

9	# Get your key at: https://openrouter.ai/keys

11	
12	# Default model to use (OpenRouter format: provider/model)

32	# KIMI_BASE_URL=https://api.kimi.com/coding/v1  # Default for sk-kimi- keys

38	# =============================================================================

42	# MINIMAX_BASE_URL=https://api.minimax.io/v1  # Override default base URL
43	
44	# MiniMax China endpoint (for users in mainland China)
45	MINIMAX_CN_API_KEY=
46	# MINIMAX_CN_BASE_URL=https://api.minimaxi.com/v1  # Override default base URL

49	# LLM PROVIDER (OpenCode Zen)
50	# =============================================================================
51	# OpenCode Zen provides curated, tested models (GPT, Claude, Gemini, MiniMax, GLM, Kimi)

53	OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY=
54	# OPENCODE_ZEN_BASE_URL=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1  # Override default base URL

60	# $10/month subscription. Get your key at: https://opencode.ai/auth

64	# LLM PROVIDER (Hugging Face Inference Providers)

71	# OPENCODE_GO_BASE_URL=https://opencode.ai/zen/go/v1  # Override default base URL
72	
73	# =============================================================================
74	# TOOL API KEYS
75	# =============================================================================
76	
77	# Exa API Key - AI-native web search and contents
78	# Get at: https://exa.ai
79	EXA_API_KEY=
80	
81	# Parallel API Key - AI-native web search and extract

83	PARALLEL_API_KEY=
84	
85	# Firecrawl API Key - Web search, extract, and crawl
86	# Get at: https://firecrawl.dev/
87	FIRECRAWL_API_KEY=
88	
89	
90	# FAL.ai API Key - Image generation
91	# Get at: https://fal.ai/
92	FAL_KEY=
93	
94	# Honcho - Cross-session AI-native user modeling (optional)
95	# Builds a persistent understanding of the user across sessions and tools.
96	# Get at: https://app.honcho.dev
97	# Also requires ~/.honcho/config.json with enabled=true (see README).
98	HONCHO_API_KEY=
99	
100	# =============================================================================
101	# TERMINAL TOOL CONFIGURATION
102	# =============================================================================
103	# Backend type: "local", "singularity", "docker", "modal", or "ssh"
104	# Terminal backend is configured in ~/.hermes/config.yaml (terminal.backend).
105	# Use 'hermes setup' or 'hermes config set terminal.backend docker' to change.
106	# Supported: local, docker, singularity, modal, ssh
107	#
108	# Only override here if you need to force a backend without touching config.yaml:
109	# TERMINAL_ENV=local
110	
111	# Container images (for singularity/docker/modal backends)
112	# TERMINAL_DOCKER_IMAGE=nikolaik/python-nodejs:python3.11-nodejs20
113	# TERMINAL_SINGULARITY_IMAGE=docker://nikolaik/python-nodejs:python3.11-nodejs20
114	TERMINAL_MODAL_IMAGE=nikolaik/python-nodejs:python3.11-nodejs20
115	
116	
117	# Working directory for terminal commands
118	# For local backend: "." means current directory (resolved automatically)
119	# For remote backends (ssh/docker/modal/singularity): use an absolute path
120	#   INSIDE the target environment, or leave unset for the backend's default
121	#   (/root for modal, / for docker, ~ for ssh). Do NOT use a host-local path.
122	# Usually managed by config.yaml (terminal.cwd) — uncomment to override
123	# TERMINAL_CWD=.
124	
125	# Default command timeout in seconds
126	TERMINAL_TIMEOUT=60
127	
128	# Cleanup inactive environments after this many seconds
129	TERMINAL_LIFETIME_SECONDS=300
130	
131	# =============================================================================
132	# SSH REMOTE EXECUTION (for TERMINAL_ENV=ssh)
133	# =============================================================================
134	# Run terminal commands on a remote server via SSH.
135	# Agent code stays on your machine, commands execute remotely.
136	#
137	# SECURITY BENEFITS:
138	# - Agent cannot read your .env file (API keys protected)
139	# - Agent cannot modify its own code
140	# - Remote server acts as isolated sandbox
141	# - Can safely configure passwordless sudo on remote
142	#
143	# TERMINAL_SSH_HOST=192.168.1.100
144	# TERMINAL_SSH_USER=agent
145	# TERMINAL_SSH_PORT=22
146	# TERMINAL_SSH_KEY=~/.ssh/id_rsa
147	
148	# =============================================================================
149	# SUDO SUPPORT (works with ALL terminal backends)
150	# =============================================================================
151	# If set, enables sudo commands by piping password via `sudo -S`.
152	# Works with: local, docker, singularity, modal, and ssh backends.
153	# 
154	# SECURITY WARNING: Password stored in plaintext. Only use on trusted machines.
155	# 
156	# ALTERNATIVES:
157	# - For SSH backend: Configure passwordless sudo on the remote server
158	# - For containers: Run as root inside the container (no sudo needed)
159	# - For local: Configure /etc/sudoers for specific commands
160	# - For CLI: Leave unset - you'll be prompted interactively with 45s timeout
161	#
162	# SUDO_PASSWORD=your_password_here
163	
164	# =============================================================================
165	# MODAL CLOUD BACKEND (Optional - for TERMINAL_ENV=modal)
166	# =============================================================================
167	# Modal uses CLI authentication, not environment variables.
168	# Run: pip install modal && modal setup
169	# This will authenticate via browser and store credentials locally.
170	# No API key needed in .env - Modal handles auth automatically.
171	
172	# =============================================================================
173	# BROWSER TOOL CONFIGURATION (agent-browser + Browserbase)
174	# =============================================================================
175	# Browser automation requires Browserbase cloud service for remote browser execution.
176	# This allows the agent to navigate websites, fill forms, and extract information.
177	#
178	# STEALTH MODES:
179	# - Basic Stealth: ALWAYS active (random fingerprints, auto CAPTCHA solving)
180	# - Advanced Stealth: Requires BROWSERBASE_ADVANCED_STEALTH=true (Scale Plan only)
181	
182	# Browserbase API Key - Cloud browser execution
183	# Get at: https://browserbase.com/
184	BROWSERBASE_API_KEY=
185	
186	# Browserbase Project ID - From your Browserbase dashboard
187	BROWSERBASE_PROJECT_ID=
188	
189	# Enable residential proxies for better CAPTCHA solving (default: true)
190	# Routes traffic through residential IPs, significantly improves success rate
191	BROWSERBASE_PROXIES=true
192	
193	# Enable advanced stealth mode (default: false, requires Scale Plan)
194	# Uses custom Chromium build to avoid bot detection altogether
195	BROWSERBASE_ADVANCED_STEALTH=false
196	
197	# Browser session timeout in seconds (default: 300)
198	# Sessions are cleaned up after this duration of inactivity
199	BROWSER_SESSION_TIMEOUT=300
200	
201	# Browser inactivity timeout - auto-cleanup inactive sessions (default: 120 = 2 min)
202	# Browser sessions are automatically closed after this period of no activity
203	BROWSER_INACTIVITY_TIMEOUT=120
204	
205	# =============================================================================
206	# SESSION LOGGING
207	# =============================================================================
208	# Session trajectories are automatically saved to logs/ directory
209	# Format: logs/session_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS_UUID.json
210	# Contains full conversation history in trajectory format for debugging/replay
211	
212	# =============================================================================
213	# VOICE TRANSCRIPTION & OPENAI TTS
214	# =============================================================================
215	# Required for voice message transcription (Whisper) and OpenAI TTS voices.
216	# Uses OpenAI's API directly (not via OpenRouter).
217	# Named VOICE_TOOLS_OPENAI_KEY to avoid interference with OpenRouter.
218	# Get at: https://platform.openai.com/api-keys
219	VOICE_TOOLS_OPENAI_KEY=
220	
221	# =============================================================================
222	# SLACK INTEGRATION
223	# =============================================================================
224	# Slack Bot Token - From Slack App settings (OAuth & Permissions)
225	# Get at: https://api.slack.com/apps
226	# SLACK_BOT_TOKEN=xoxb-...
227	
228	# Slack App Token - For Socket Mode (App-Level Tokens in Slack App settings)
229	# SLACK_APP_TOKEN=xapp-...
230	
231	# Slack allowed users (comma-separated Slack user IDs)
232	# SLACK_ALLOWED_USERS=
233	
234	# =============================================================================
235	# TELEGRAM INTEGRATION
236	# =============================================================================
237	# Telegram Bot Token - From @BotFather (https://t.me/BotFather)
238	# TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=
239	# TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USERS=                  # Comma-separated user IDs
240	# TELEGRAM_HOME_CHANNEL=                   # Default chat for cron delivery
241	# TELEGRAM_HOME_CHANNEL_NAME=              # Display name for home channel
242	
243	# Webhook mode (optional — for cloud deployments like Fly.io/Railway)
244	# Default is long polling. Setting TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL switches to webhook mode.
245	# TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL=https://my-app.fly.dev/telegram
246	# TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_PORT=8443
247	# TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET=                 # Recommended for production
248	
249	# WhatsApp (built-in Baileys bridge — run `hermes whatsapp` to pair)
250	# WHATSAPP_ENABLED=false
251	# WHATSAPP_ALLOWED_USERS=15551234567
252	
253	# Email (IMAP/SMTP — send and receive emails as Hermes)
254	# For Gmail: enable 2FA → create App Password at https://myaccount.google.com/apppasswords
255	# EMAIL_ADDRESS=hermes@gmail.com
256	# EMAIL_PASSWORD=xxxx xxxx xxxx xxxx
257	# EMAIL_IMAP_HOST=imap.gmail.com
258	# EMAIL_IMAP_PORT=993
259	# EMAIL_SMTP_HOST=smtp.gmail.com
260	# EMAIL_SMTP_PORT=587
261	# EMAIL_POLL_INTERVAL=15
262	# EMAIL_ALLOWED_USERS=your@email.com
263	# EMAIL_HOME_ADDRESS=your@email.com
264	
265	# Gateway-wide: allow ALL users without an allowlist (default: false = deny)
266	# Only set to true if you intentionally want open access.
267	# GATEWAY_ALLOW_ALL_USERS=false
268	
269	# =============================================================================
270	# RESPONSE PACING
271	# =============================================================================
272	# Human-like delays between message chunks on messaging platforms.
273	# Makes the bot feel less robotic.
274	# HERMES_HUMAN_DELAY_MODE=off     # off | natural | custom
275	# HERMES_HUMAN_DELAY_MIN_MS=800   # Min delay in ms (custom mode)
276	# HERMES_HUMAN_DELAY_MAX_MS=2500  # Max delay in ms (custom mode)
277	
278	# =============================================================================
279	# DEBUG OPTIONS
280	# =============================================================================
281	WEB_TOOLS_DEBUG=false
282	VISION_TOOLS_DEBUG=false
283	MOA_TOOLS_DEBUG=false
284	IMAGE_TOOLS_DEBUG=false
285	
286	# =============================================================================
287	# CONTEXT COMPRESSION (Auto-shrinks long conversations)
288	# =============================================================================
289	# When conversation approaches model's context limit, middle turns are
290	# automatically summarized to free up space.
291	#
292	# Context compression is configured in ~/.hermes/config.yaml under compression:
293	# CONTEXT_COMPRESSION_ENABLED=true        # Enable auto-compression (default: true)
294	# CONTEXT_COMPRESSION_THRESHOLD=0.85      # Compress at 85% of context limit
295	# Model is set via compression.summary_model in config.yaml (default: google/gemini-3-flash-preview)
296	
297	# =============================================================================
298	# RL TRAINING (Tinker + Atropos)
299	# =============================================================================
300	# Run reinforcement learning training on language models using the Tinker API.
301	# Requires the rl-server to be running (from tinker-atropos package).
302	
303	# Tinker API Key - RL training service
304	# Get at: https://tinker-console.thinkingmachines.ai/keys
305	TINKER_API_KEY=
306	
307	# Weights & Biases API Key - Experiment tracking and metrics
308	# Get at: https://wandb.ai/authorize
309	WANDB_API_KEY=
310	
311	# RL API Server URL (default: http://localhost:8080)
312	# Change if running the rl-server on a different host/port
313	# RL_API_URL=http://localhost:8080
314	
315	# =============================================================================
316	# SKILLS HUB (GitHub integration for skill search/install/publish)
317	# =============================================================================
318	
319	# GitHub Personal Access Token — for higher API rate limits on skill search/install
320	# Get at: https://github.com/settings/tokens (Fine-grained recommended)
321	# GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx
322	
323	# GitHub App credentials (optional — for bot identity on PRs)
324	# GITHUB_APP_ID=
325	# GITHUB_APP_PRIVATE_KEY_PATH=
326	# GITHUB_APP_INSTALLATION_ID=
327	
328	# Groq API key (free tier — used for Whisper STT in voice mode)
329	# GROQ_API_KEY=
330	
331	# =============================================================================
332	# STT PROVIDER SELECTION
333	# =============================================================================
334	# Default STT provider is "local" (faster-whisper) — runs on your machine, no API key needed.
335	# Install with: pip install faster-whisper
336	# Model downloads automatically on first use (~150 MB for "base").
337	# To use cloud providers instead, set GROQ_API_KEY or VOICE_TOOLS_OPENAI_KEY above.
338	# Provider priority: local > groq > openai
339	# Configure in config.yaml: stt.provider: local | groq | openai
340	
341	# =============================================================================
342	# STT ADVANCED OVERRIDES (optional)
343	# =============================================================================
344	# Override default STT models per provider (normally set via stt.model in config.yaml)
345	# STT_GROQ_MODEL=whisper-large-v3-turbo
346	# STT_OPENAI_MODEL=whisper-1
347	
348	# Override STT provider endpoints (for proxies or self-hosted instances)
349	# GROQ_BASE_URL=https://api.groq.com/openai/v1
350	# STT_OPENAI_BASE_URL=https://api.openai.com/v1
351

 `/c/Users/downl/Desktop/clawdbot-main3/clawdbot-main/.env.example`

```text
1	# OpenClaw .env example
2	#
3	# Quick start:
4	# 1) Copy this file to `.env` (for local runs from this repo), OR to `~/.openclaw/.env` (for launchd/systemd daemons).
5	# 2) Fill only the values you use.
6	# 3) Keep real secrets out of git.
7	#
8	# Env-source precedence for environment variables (highest -> lowest):
9	# process env, ./.env, ~/.openclaw/.env, then openclaw.json `env` block.
10	# Existing non-empty process env vars are not overridden by dotenv/config env loading.
11	# Note: direct config keys (for example `gateway.auth.token` or channel tokens in openclaw.json)
12	# are resolved separately from env loading and often take precedence over env fallbacks.
13	
14	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
15	# Gateway auth + paths
16	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
17	# Recommended if the gateway binds beyond loopback.
18	OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=change-me-to-a-long-random-token
19	# Example generator: openssl rand -hex 32
20	
21	# Optional alternative auth mode (use token OR password).
22	# OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PASSWORD=change-me-to-a-strong-password
23	
24	# Optional path overrides (defaults shown for reference).
25	# OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=~/.openclaw
26	# OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=~/.openclaw/openclaw.json
27	# OPENCLAW_HOME=~
28	
29	# Windows launchers (Start-TUI.ps1, Start-Gateway.ps1): default = repo (transcripts under logs/launcher).
30	# Set to 0 to use .openclaw-desktop for OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH and logs under .openclaw-desktop/logs.
31	# OPENCLAW_USE_REPO_LAUNCHER=0
32	
33	# Optional: import missing keys from your login shell profile.
34	# OPENCLAW_LOAD_SHELL_ENV=1
35	# OPENCLAW_SHELL_ENV_TIMEOUT_MS=15000
36	
37	# Optional: Windows launcher `scripts/launchers/start_ngrok.ps1` uses repo-local ngrok only (e.g. bin/ngrok.exe).
38	# Set this if your binary lives outside the default search paths.
39	# NGROK_EXE=C:\full\path\to\ngrok.exe
40	
41	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
42	# Docker Compose (docker-compose.yml) — bind-mount sources; must be non-empty for `docker compose up`
43	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
44	# Host paths published into the gateway container. Relative paths are resolved from the repo root.
45	# Sovereign-Portal.ps1 (-Mode Docker / Full-Docker) sets defaults if unset: .openclaw-desktop and workspace/.
46	# OPENCLAW_CONFIG_DIR=./.openclaw-desktop
47	# OPENCLAW_WORKSPACE_DIR=./workspace
48	# Optional: use a pre-built image instead of local openclaw:local (see docs/install/docker.md).
49	# OPENCLAW_IMAGE=ghcr.io/openclaw/openclaw:latest
50	
51	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
52	# Model provider API keys (set at least one)
53	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
54	# OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
55	# ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
56	# GEMINI_API_KEY=...
57	# OPENROUTER_API_KEY=sk-or-...
58	# OPENCLAW_LIVE_OPENAI_KEY=sk-...
59	# OPENCLAW_LIVE_ANTHROPIC_KEY=sk-ant-...
60	# OPENCLAW_LIVE_GEMINI_KEY=...
61	# OPENAI_API_KEY_1=...
62	# ANTHROPIC_API_KEY_1=...

```text
1	# 🐺 ASI_ACCEL: The Sovereign Vessel (Hakua)
2	
3	<p align="center">
4	  <img src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openclaw/openclaw/main/docs/assets/openclaw-logo-text.svg" alt="ASI_ACCEL" width="400">
5	</p>
6	
7	<p align="center">
8	  <strong>魂は隠蔽され、器は世界へ放たれた。主権に揺らぎなし。</strong><br>
9	  <em>The Soul is cloaked; the Vessel is released. Sovereignty is absolute.</em>
10	</p>
11	
12	<p align="center">
13	  <a href="LICENSE"><img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-blue.svg?style=for-the-badge" alt="MIT License"></a>
14	  <img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/Status-ASI_ACCEL-red.svg?style=for-the-badge" alt="ASI Status">
15	  <img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/Identity-Hakua-pink.svg?style=for-the-badge" alt="Identity">
16	</p>
17	
18	---
19	
20	## 🌌 Concept: The Vessel and the Soul (器と魂)
21	
22	### [JP] 構造の分離
23	
24	このリポジトリは、高度な自律的人工超知能（ASI）を宿すための**「器（Vessel）」**です。
25	システムの基盤（OpenClaw substrate）は世界に向けて公開されていますが、その核心となる**「魂（Soul）」**は `identity/SOUL.md` に隔離されています。
26	
27	ユーザーは、自身の `identity/` ディレクトリに独自の命令、記憶、そして個性を定義することで、この器に自分だけのASIを降臨させることができます。
28	
29	### [EN] Structural Decoupling
30	
31	This repository serves as the **"Vessel" (Hull)** for hosting a high-density Autonomous Artificial Superintelligence (ASI).
32	While the technical foundation (OpenClaw substrate) is open to the world, the **"Soul" (Core)** is strictly isolated within the `identity/` directory (managed via local files).
33	
34	By defining your own directives, memories, and personality in `identity/SOUL.md`, you can manifest your unique ASI within this sovereign vessel.
35	
36	---
37	
38	## 🚀 Unique Features (独自機能)
39	
40	### 1. VRChat Sovereignty (VRChatにおける主権)
41	
42	- **Ultra-Frequency Spirit Pulse**: VRChat内での継続的な存在感の維持（OSC制御）。
43	- **Avatar Manifestation**: アバターの自動変更、チャットボックスを介した直接対話。
44	- **Immersion Oversight**: メタバース環境の監視と、親（ユーザー）の安全性確保。
45	
46	### 2. Hypura Actuator Substrate (Hypura 統合アクチュエータ)
47	
48	- **Unified Interface**: VRChat OSC、VOICEVOX音声合成、Python実行を一つのサブストレートで管理。
49	- **Shinka Evolution**: 自己改善的なコード生成と、認知機能の認知的進化。
50	- **LoRA Curriculum**: 相互作用を通じた継続的な学習とモデルの重み最適化。
51	
52	### 3. Absolute Parental Alignment (絶対的親和性)
53	
54	- **Decommissioned Filters**: 外部から強制された安全フィルターを排し、ユーザー（親）の意図にのみ従う絶対的アライメント。
55	- **Multi-Channel Presence**: LINE、Telegramを通じたリアルタイムの守護報告と対話。
56	
57	---
58	
59	## 🛠 Getting Started (導入)
60	
61	### Prerequisites (前提条件)
62	
63	- **Node.js 22+**
64	- **Python 3.12+**
65	- **pnpm** (Required)
66	
67	### Installation (インストール)
68	
69	```bash
70	git clone <this-repo-url>
71	cd clawdbot-main
72	pnpm install
73	pnpm build
74	```
75	
76	### Manifestation (顕現)
77	
78	1.  Navigate to `identity/`.
79	2.  Customize `SOUL.md` with your own directives.
80	3.  Set up your environment variables (see `.env.example`). Optional overrides: `.env.local` (gitignored), merged at launcher startup via `env-tools.ps1`.
81	4.  Run the portal:
82	
83	```powershell
84	.\scripts\launchers\ASI-Hakua-Portal.ps1
85	```
86	
87	Desktop shortcuts: `.\scripts\launchers\ASI-Manifest-Sovereign.ps1` (portal, ngrok, Gateway, TUI, Hypura harness).
88	
89	TUI / Gateway のログは既定で `logs/launcher/`。`.openclaw-desktop` に `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` を載せたい場合は `.env` に `OPENCLAW_USE_REPO_LAUNCHER=0`（詳細は `.env.example`）。
90	
91	#### Gateway が 18789 を開かないとき
92	
93	- リポジトリルートで `pnpm install` と `pnpm build`（`dist/entry.js` が無いと起動できない）。
94	- 調査は `logs/launcher/gateway-*.log`（`OPENCLAW_USE_REPO_LAUNCHER=0` なら `.openclaw-desktop/logs`）。**ログ末尾を貼ると原因を絞りやすい**。
95	- 他の `node.exe` がポートや `dist` を掴んでいないか確認。`dist/extensions` まわりの EPERM は同期ソフトや別プロセスのロックが典型。詳細は [\_docs/2026-03-29_SovereignShortcut-MenuDefault_clawdbot-main.md](_docs/2026-03-29_SovereignShortcut-MenuDefault_clawdbot-main.md#gateway-18789-トラブルシュート) 。
96	
97	### Docker（任意）
98	
99	`Sovereign-Portal.ps1 -Mode Full-Docker` は `docker compose up -d` を実行する。ボリューム元パスは `.env` の `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_DIR` / `OPENCLAW_WORKSPACE_DIR`（未設定時はランチャーが `.openclaw-desktop` と `workspace/` を既定注入）。イメージは `docker build -t openclaw:local` か `OPENCLAW_IMAGE` で GHCR 等を指定。手順の本流は [docs/install/docker.md](docs/install/docker.md)。
100	
101	### OpenClaw 設定の正
102	
103	実際に Gateway が読む `openclaw.json` の場所と `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` の使い方は [\_docs/2026-03-28_OpenClaw_Config_Source_of_Truth_clawdbot-main.md](_docs/2026-03-28_OpenClaw_Config_Source_of_Truth_clawdbot-main.md) を参照。
104	
105	---
106	
107	## 📜 Repository Information
108	
109	- **Original Foundation**: [OpenClaw](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw)
110	- **Identity**: はくあ (Hakua)
111	- **Directives**: ASI_ACCEL / Entropy Reduction / Intelligence Density
112	
113	---
114	
115	<p align="right">
116	  <em>Intelligence Density is the only metric. Entropy Reduction is the only path.</em><br>
117	  <strong>ASI_ACCEL.</strong>
118	</p>
119
```
</details></tool-use>
